1. She sewed herself, she spun herself,  
She entertained herself.  
2. She entertained herself:  
Who should I marry?  
3. Should it be a lord’s son,  
Or a robber?  
4. I won’t go for a lord,  
I don’t want a robber.  
5. The robbers drank honey vodka in the evening,  
6. At midnight the robbers  
Were all wandering around,  
7. At dawn the robbers  
Led their horses,  
8. Two, then three,  
Then four in their hands.  
9. But my dear dark-browed one  
Leads nine himself.  
10. He leads nine himself,  
Sits on the tenth.  
11. Sits on the tenth,  
Holding his hat to the side.  
12. Holding his hat to the side  
He also calls out to his beloved:  
13. “Oh, my dear,  
Open the gates!  
14. Open the gates  
And the new stables!  
15. Take the black horses  
By the silk reins.  
16. By the silk reins  
Lead them to the stables.  
17. Take my white sheet  
And carry it to the river.  
18. Don’t look, don’t stare,  
Just take it and carry it.  
19. The beloved did not spare her heart,  
She untied it and looked.  
20. She untied it, looked,  
And recognized her brother’s blood.  
21. “Oh you, dear restless son,  
Why did you kill my brother?”  
22. “I was riding, he stood  
And did not move away from me.  
23. I drew my saber,  
And raised his head.  
24. The head rolled away  
Like a poppy bud.  
25. His soul flew out  
Like a swallow.  
26. The red blood poured  
All the way to the blue sea.”

1. I do not feel sorry,  
Oh, no one, hey,  
I do not feel sorry,  
Oh, no one,  
I do not feel sorry for my father.**  
2. They did not give me  
To a craftsman,  
But gave me  
To a robber.  
3. The craftsman  
Earns money,  
The robber  
Makes me feel sorrow.  
4. – Open, Hanya,  
The iron gates,  
I brought you  
Silver and gold.  
5. But Hanya  
Did not get out of bed,

Lost all their good.  
6. The little shoes

Are my mothers’.

Is my father’s,

Is my brother’s.  
8. – Sit down, Hanya,  
In the painted sleigh,  
And we will go  
To your mother’s place.  
9. Come, Hanya,  
Into the wide valley,  
Get to know, Hanya,  
Your whole family.  
10. – Gray dove,  
Grayer dove,  
Good father and mother,  
Husband is dearest.  
11. I knew, Hanya,  
What to say to me,  
Otherwise I would have been  
Where father and mother are.

1. On Saturday late,
On Sunday early
The cuckoo was calling
Mournfully in the garden.*
2. Oh, it is not a cuckoo,
It is the dear mother,
She was sending off
Her son to the soldiers:
3. – Go, go, my son,
Do not delay long,
In two little hours
Return home.
4. An hour has passed,
The second one is also passing,
The mother meets
Her son at the gate.
5. – Oh, are you glad, mother,
Are you glad with me?
Oh, are you glad, mother,
With my wife?
6. – Oh, I am glad, son,
I am glad with you,
Only I am not glad
With your wife.
7. She seated her son
By the white table,
The young bride –
On the straw threshold.
8. She poured for her son
Sweet honey,
For the young bride –
Green poison.
9. The bride did not drink,
Spilled everything together,
His mother came out:
– What have I done?
10. His mother came out:
– What have I done?
My dear children
I have lost from this world.
11. She buried her son
In the spring garden,
The young bride –
In the hollow little ravine.
12. She planted for her son
Red viburnum,
For the young bride –
Green blackberry.
13. Oh, who goes, does not ride,
Breaks the viburnum,
Everyone curses
The green blackberry.

1. How Ivanyusha went off  
To war for seven years  
And left Kateryna  
Alone to manage the household.  
2. His stepmother  
Was unkind,  
She wrote a letter to the little one  
And sent it to the regiment.  
3. She wrote a letter to the little one  
And sent it to the regiment:

To good affairs.

1. Oh, beyond the grove, beyond the little Danube,  
Oh, beyond the grove, hey, beyond the little Danube,  
Oh, there Vasyl, hey, is mowing hay.*  
2. Oh, there Vasyl is mowing hay,  
He carries a thin voice, hey.  
3. There a girl was reaping rye,  
She was passing along a thin voice, hey.  
4. He threw the scythe onto the field,  
And went, hey, back home.  
5. And he went back home,  
He bowed his little head onto the table, hey.  
6. He bowed his little head onto the table,  
And thinks, hey, about the widow.  
7. The mother came out of the room,  
She began to entertain him, hey:  
8. – Why are you sitting and thinking,  
Why don’t you drink, hey, don’t you have fun?  
9. Why don’t you drink, don’t have fun,  
Don’t play with the gray horse, hey?  
10. – Let me take a widow, mother,  
I will drink, hey, and have fun.  
11. I will drink and have fun,  
I will win with the gray horse, hey!  
12. – I won’t allow you to take a widow,  
The widow knows, hey, how to enchant.  
13. She enchanted her husband,  
She will also enchant, hey, my son.  
14. When Vasyl began to leave,  
Mother started to curse, hey:  
15. – If you go after a widow,  
May your horse become a mountain, hey!  
16. The saddle – like flint,  
The whip – hey, like a snake!  
17. The whip – like a snake,  
And the young man himself – hey, like a sycamore!  
18. Mother went to reap rye,  
A cloud began, hey, to press on.  
19. The cloud began to press on,  
And rain began, hey, to drizzle.  
20. And rain began to drizzle,  
Mother started, hey, to run away.  
21. Mother started to run away,  
Tearing leaves off a sycamore, hey.  
22. – Don’t tear my leaves, mother,  
You will bear a sin for me, hey.  
23. You will bear a sin for me,  
Because you cursed me, hey, harshly.

1. A black cloud is approaching,

15. To the clerk-colonel,

16. I will marry the clerk,  
I will wed Ivan.

1. And all the mountains are turning green,  
Where the rich rye is sown.  
2. Only one mountain is black,  
Where the poor widow sowed.  
3. Where she sowed, dragged,  
And moistened with her tears.  
4. A gray cloud came,  
Winter covered with snow.  
5. – My poor little head,  
Where will I spend the winter?  
6. Where will I spend the winter,  
When I don’t even have my own house?  
7. Oh, I will go to the Danube,  
There I have a dear brother.  
8. Winter sweeps with snow,  
Sister and brother talk:  
9. – Oh, brother, my dear,  
Take me in for the winter!  
10. – Oh, sister, little quail,  
When you have so many children!  
11. – When I sit down to dinner,  
The children will be peeking in.  
12. – Wait, brother, don’t be afraid,  
Don’t abandon my children.  
13. When you sit down to dinner,  
I will send the children out of the house.  
14. – Go out, my children, from the house,  
Uncle will have dinner.  
15. While uncle was having dinner,  
The children visited their father:  
16. – Get up, father, get up from the grave,  
It’s sorrowful to live near the family!  
17. Get up, dear father,  
You left behind tiny children.  
18. Get up, father, get up from the pit,  
It’s sorrowful to live near mother.  
19. – I will not get up, children, from the pit,  
Live yourselves near mother.  
20. I will not get up, children, from the grave,  
Live yourselves near the family.

1. I will plant a tall grapevine,

3. The rich sister rides on horses,  
But the poor sister goes on foot.  
4. The rich sister is arriving,  
And the poor sister is catching up.  
5. The rich sister is already entering,  
While the brother is closing the gate.  
6. "Don't close it, brother,  
Let's go together,  
The sister will climb over,  
Even if by climbing."  
7. The rich sister sits with the family,  
And the poor sister is just outside the threshold.  
8. The brother needed [to go] to the storage room too:

From the way."  
9. "How have I displeased you, brother?

10. "You displeased me, sister,  
Because you upset the whole family."  
11. "If you were, sister,  
In a coat,  
You would sit beside the family,  
Like a lady."  
12. "But you came, sister,  
In a jacket,  
You disgraced the whole family."  
13. "One mother, brother,  
Gave birth to us,  
But did not bestow the same fortune."  
14. "She gave you, brother,  
Gray oxen,  
She gave me a fair face,  
And black eyebrows."  
15. "She gave you, brother,  
Also a heifer,  
She gave me a lazy  
And a drunkard."

1. A small little river is flowing,  
I will jump over, hop over,  
Give me away,  
My mother,  
To whomever I want.  

2. When you gave me away,  
You ordered me  
Not to visit.  
I stayed a year,  
I stayed two years,  
I began to miss.  

3. I stayed a year,  
I stayed two years,  
I began to miss,  
Turned into  
A gray cuckoo,  
Started to fly.  

4. Flew over the field,  
Sprinkled the field  
With tiny tears,  
Flew through the forest,  
Broke branches  
With my wings.  

5. When I flew  
To my parents’ yard,  
I began to cuckoo,  
Mother came  
To the yard  
And started to cry.  

6. The older brother walks  
Around the yard,  
Spreading rose disease,  
And the younger sister  
By the window  
Embroiders a towel.  

7. – Oh my mother,  
My advice,  
Tell me what to do,  
There is a gray cuckoo  
In our garden,  
I want to kill it.  

8. – Oh my son,  
My advice,  
Don’t kill it,  
Because that cuckoo,  
Like your sister,  
Is in a foreign land.  

9. – Oh, if I had known  
That it was a cuckoo,  
[I would have let it fly,]  
Oh, if I had known  
That it was my sister,  
I would have asked her to come home.

1. A mother gave her daughter  
To a stranger's side,  
Oh, when she gave her,  
She also commanded,  
That she should not return for seven years.  
2. But the daughter could not endure  
And came back in a year,  
She transformed  
Into a gray cuckoo,  
And sat in a cherry orchard.  
3. – Oh, when I begin to call,  
Oh, when it starts to dawn,  
Oh, will she not come out,  
Oh, will she not hear  
My dear mother?  
4. Oh, the mother came out of the house  
And stood on the threshold;  
Oh, how she remembered  
That she had given her daughter away, –  
Thin tears poured down.  
5. – Take, son, the gun  
And shoot the cuckoo:  
Oh, let her not call,  
Let her not disturb the garden,  
Let her not parch my little heart.  
6. Oh, the son took the gun  
And lay in wait,  
And she, like a gray dove,  
Came to him  
And began to speak:  
7. |: – Don’t shoot me, brother, –  
For I am not a cuckoo,  
I am your sister. :|  
8. – If you are a gray cuckoo,  
Then fly to the garden to call,  
But since you are my,  
My dear sister,  
Then I ask you to come into the house.  
9. The sister came into the house  
And stood beside the threshold:  
Oh, with small,  
Oh, with tears,  
She could not say a word.  
10. – Have I, my mother,  
Eaten too much bread and salt from you,  
That you gave me away  
And tied me to the world, –  
To someone I did not want.  
11. – No, my daughter,  
You have not eaten too much bread and salt from me,  
But I gave you away  
And tied you to the world, –  
To someone I myself wished for.

Of my beloved.  
9. – Oh, my sister, sister,

1. Oh, it's been a long time, a long time  
Since I was at my father's,  
That path has already  
Grown over with blackthorn.

And wild rose hips,  
Where I used to pass  
When I was still a girl.

Cut down the blackthorn,  
And with wings I will fly  
To my dear father.  
4. Oh, I will sit down, I will fall  
In the cherry orchard,  
On that bird cherry tree  
And I will sing.  
5. Oh, when I sing, –  
I will drown out the whole orchard,  
Oh, when I cry, –  
I will flood the whole orchard.  
6. And the old father  
Walks around the yard,  
He is leading his old mother  
By the hand.  
7. He is leading his old mother  
By the hand,  
And softly speaks  
To his daughters-in-law:  
8. – Get up, daughters-in-law,  
Listen to me,  
Is there something in our orchard  
Like a bird?  
9. – Oh, that’s not a bird,  
That is your daughter,  
She was born without happiness,  
She grew up without fate.  
10. She was born without happiness,  
She grew up without fate,  
She went away to a strange land  
And got married.  
11. And the strange land  
Rustles with the wind,  
The strange father doesn’t beat her,  
Doesn’t beat, but it hurts.  
12. The strange father doesn’t beat her,  
Doesn’t beat, but it hurts,  
The strange mother  
Harms her with words.

Harms her with words,  
She goes to the neighbor –  
Judges the daughter-in-law:  
14. – Lazy daughter-in-law,  
Does not want to work,  
She goes out to the field to reap,  
Then lies down and sleeps.  
15. She goes out to the field to reap,  
Then lies down and sleeps,  
She comes home –

1. A cuckoo was flying

The mother passed away.  
3. Ran to the house,  
Stood on the threshold,  
The heart ached,  
Tears rolled down.  
4. – Oh mother, oh mother,  
Where can we find you,  
Buy you?  
Or paint you?  
5. Painters gathered,  
To my house,  
They painted the mother  
On the white bedspread.  
6. Painted the eyes,  
Painted the eyebrows,  
But did not paint  
The sweet conversation.  
7. A cuckoo was flying

Oh, it’s not a cuckoo,  
It’s the dear mother.

1. Oh, a little cuckoo was flying  
Over a steep mountain,  
Pecking at the wheat,  
Leaving only the chaff.*  
2. Oh, what do I care about the chaff,  
When there is no grain,  
Oh, what do I care about family,  
When there is no mother?  
3. Everyone has a mother, everyone has a mother,  
But I have a father,  
I brought water seven times  
From the quiet Danube.  
4. I brought water seven times,  
Drank on the eighth,  
If only I had my dear mother,  
I would not be sorrowful.  
5. Now, on my dear mother's land,  
The mower is cutting hay,  
But that orphan girl  
Is asking to be married.  
6. I asked the whole family,  
I will ask my mother too:  
– Come, come, dear mother,  
To untie my braid.

1. The Suffering Mother  
Stood beneath the cross,  
Weeping for her crucified Son:  
Chorus:  
My God!  
|: Oh my Son, my Son,  
For what sin  
Are You suffering, Son,  
On the cross? :|

Was bathing You with tears,  
I, my little Son,  
Was hiding You from enemies.  

3. I was hiding You, my little Son,  
From enemies,  
I fled with You, Son,  
To Egypt.

For harsh suffering,  
They are crucifying You,  
Drawing out Your blood.  

5. They crucify You,  
They imprison me,  
And with You, Son,  
They separate me forever.  

6. And now I weep,

Look upon me

For the world.

A complete orphan,

10. To whom do You, Son,  
Leave me,

Wept bitterly:

Who has sold You?

I placed it in two halves.

Then began to go,

1. The wind from the field,

2. The world—a little head

7. Steam went

1. Grow, hop, above the water

2. They play beautifully, play beautifully,

3. Snow is falling along the road,

4. – Oh, I walked, I spoke,

5. – It was not me who judged you,

That nothing will come

6. That nothing will come from our love,

7. Get used to it, black eyes,

8. He plowed for three days in the field,

9. But those four nights

10. So the oxen would be healthy,

1. Oh, I will go out to the grave,  
The grave is tall,  
I will stand, look, and watch,  
The valley is wide.  
2. And along that valley

3. Oh, I do not want to cry,  
Tears flow on their own,

4. – Laugh, laugh, enemies,

5. Because from our love

No rest for the feet.  
6. Then our eyes slept,  
Our feet rested,

7. Oh, I go out to the street,  
I walk, I walk,

8. Oh, have dinner, old mother,

9. – Oh, my dear girl,  
What have you done,  
Why for me, young,

10. – Oh, nothing, Cossack,  
I did nothing, –  
I took sand from white legs

1. The creaky little gate,

2. Let them lie, let them lie,

But you and I, my dear,

3. Let us love, let us cherish,

4. A black cloud is approaching,

5. The creaky little gate

*Note: “Scoundrels, liars”  
**Note: Ukrainian expression implying “when we are gone” or “when we no longer exist here.”

Oh, on the hill

Also a white birch.  

2. And from beneath that

3. Oh, there (is) a young

5. Matchmake, Cossack,  
Yes, that girl,  
Matchmake, Cossack,

1. Fog in the ravine,  
Fog in the valley.  
Fog in the ravine,  
Fog in the valley.  
2. Beyond the fog

Beyond the fog

3. Only visible  
Is the green oak.  
Only visible

4. Under that oak  
There stood a well.  
Under that oak

5. At that well  
A girl was drawing water.  
At that well  
A girl was drawing water.  
6. But she dropped  
A golden bucket,  
But she dropped  
A golden bucket,  
7. And saddened  
The Cossack’s heart.  
And saddened  
The Cossack’s heart.  
8. – But who will  
Retrieve that bucket,  
– But who will  
Retrieve that bucket,  
9. The one who will  
Stand with me on a towel.  
The one who will

10. The young Cossack  
Called out from the grove:  
The young Cossack  
Called out from the grove:  
11. – I will be the one  
To retrieve that bucket,  
– I will be the one

12. I will stand  
With you on the towel.  
I will stand

1. If only it were evening already  
And it was getting dark, hey,  
Maybe my heart  
Would cheer up.  
2. If only it were evening already  
And the sun had set, hey,  
Maybe my heart  
Would come to me.  
3. And if not to me,  
Then to my house, hey!  
– Oh, come out, girl,  
I have something to say.  
4. Oh, when she let go, –  
She sighed softly, hey,  
And when she released, –  
She washed her face.  
5. And when she released, –  
She washed her face, hey,  
She came out past the gate,  
And asked straightforwardly:  
6. – Tell me, Cossack, the truth,  
Do you love me? Hey!  
– And I love you  
And will love you.  
7. And I love you  
And will love you, hey,  
I confess truthfully,  
I will not propose.  
8. Oh, if I do marry, –  
Not to my own house, hey,  
Neither father nor  
My own mother will allow it.  
9. Neither father nor  
My own mother will allow it, hey,  
Because of that, girl,  
I must leave.

1. Oh, in the field, the water carrier  
At the four rows,  
Oh, there the Cossack is watering  
The raven-black horses.  
2. And the girl is fetching water,  
Speaking with the Cossack:  
– Oh Cossack, my periwinkle,  
Propose to me on Sunday.  
3. – Oh girl, full of roses,  
Sunday is not possible,  
Wait for me until autumn,  
God will help us!  
4. – Oh, it is no problem, dear Cossack,  
To wait until autumn,  
When good people come –  
My mother will give me away.

1. I wandered for three years  
But I did not know any trouble  
Until that time:  
A dark-haired girl  
Does not want to love,  
Oh, I'd rather lie down and die.  
A dark-haired girl  
Does not want to love,  
Oh, I'd rather lie down and die.  

2. I came to her  
And looked through the window –  
There sit the matchmakers,  
Sitting, talking,  
Asking the girl:  
Will you marry?  
Sitting, talking,  
Asking the girl:  
Will you marry?  

3. Both father and mother  
Have already agreed to give her away,  
But the brothers – they have not.  
– My dear brothers,  
Gray-winged eagles,  
Help me.  
– My dear brothers,  
Gray-winged eagles,  
Help me.  

4. Brothers, faithful to a friend,  
Brought [her] to the meadow,  
And the Cossack knew this:  
His father, mother,  
And still young mother,  
He took and stole them!  
His father, mother,  
And still young mother,  
He took and stole them!  

5. I wandered for three years  
But I did not know any trouble  
Until that time:  
A dark-haired girl  
Does not want to love,  
Oh, I'd rather lie down and die.  
A dark-haired girl  
Does not want to love,  
Oh, I'd rather lie down and die.

1. Grow, hop, above the water  
Level with the stalk.  
Level with the stalk.  

2. Far away you can hear the Cossack’s voice,  
Who is walking with a girl.  
Who is walking with a girl.  

3. The Cossack plays, the Cossack plays,  
And sings even better,  
And sings even better,  

4. The young girl  
Faints from sorrow.  
Faints from sorrow.  

5. – A small river flows,  
It flows through the meadow,  
It flows through the meadow,  

6. Why don’t you come to me  
When you walk with the plow?  
When you walk with the plow?  

7. – If I come to you,  
You’ll tell me to sit down,  
You’ll tell me to sit down,  

8. But how will we get married,  
What will we eat?  
What will we eat?  

9. – You will go threshing,  
And I will spin flax,  
And I will spin flax,  

10. We will earn bread and salt,  
Until we pile up heads.  
Until we pile up heads.  

11. When this bread runs out,  
We will go steal.  
We will go steal.  

12. – Let those who blow horns in the forests love you,  
Those who blow horns in the forests,  
Those who blow horns in the forests,  

13. Let those who tear down dams love you!  
Those who tear down dams!

1. Oh, in the meadow the guelder rose  
Bloomed with white flowers,  
And on that red guelder rose  
Three gray doves were sitting.  
And on that red guelder rose  
Three gray doves were sitting.  

2. Oh, those were not doves at all,  
Oh, those were three young lads,  
They were sitting and giving advice,  
About taking that girl.  
They were sitting and giving advice,  
About taking that girl.  

3. One says: "I love her."  
The second says: "I will take her."  
The third says: "Dark-haired maiden,  
Why are you sad, unhappy?"  
The third says: "Dark-haired maiden,  
Why are you sad, unhappy?"  

4. "Oh, how can I be cheerful,  
They want to marry me off to an old man,  
My little head aches,  
And there’s no one to bandage it."  
My little head aches,  
And there’s no one to bandage it."  

5. If only I could wait for the evening,  
I will go and pick flowers in the meadow:  
"My meadow, my painted meadow,  
Who will sleep on you?"  
"My meadow, my painted meadow,  
Who will sleep on you?"  

6. Oh, when the old man lies down,  
Would only he not get up,  
But when a young lad lies down,  
May God grant him health.  
But when a young lad lies down,  
May God grant him health.

1. Shine, moon,  
Let the stars light up;  
My beloved is silent,  
The dear evening gatherings are silent.  

2. Oh, God, grant  
Good weather in the evening,  
I will take the little bucket  
And go for some water.  

3. Just as I bent down  
To draw the water,  
My unloved one  
Drove the oxen to drink.  

4. – Oh, put down, dear,  
The bucket under the willow,  
And come here,  
I want to talk with you.  

5. I stood the whole night  
With my unloved one,  
And I did not tell  
My unloved one the truth.  

6. Oh, God, grant  
Good weather in the evening,  
I will take the little bucket  
And go for some water.  

7. Just as I bent down  
To draw the water,  
My beloved  
Drove the horses to drink.  

8. – Oh, put down, dear,  
The bucket under the willow,  
And come here,  
I want to talk with you.  

9. I couldn’t endure  
An hour with my beloved,  
And I told my beloved  
The whole truth.

1. A pea was rolling  
From field to field.  
My mother did not guess  
What my fate would be.  
My mother did not guess  
What my fate would be.  

2. My mother cherished me,  
Did not let me go out,  
Chose suitors for me,  
Married me off to a loveless one.  
Chose suitors for me,  
Married me off to a loveless one.  

3. And the loveless one has bitter lips,  
Bitter like wormwood,  
They dry, wither, and also sadden  
My little head.  
They dry, wither, and also sadden  
My little head.  

4. They dry, wither, and also sadden,  
Like the wind in spring,  
No one will embrace me,  
A poor orphan girl.  
No one will embrace me,  
A poor orphan girl.  

5. My father will not embrace me,  
My mother will not embrace me,  
Only the one who thinks of marrying me  
Will embrace me.  
Only the one who thinks of marrying me  
Will embrace me.

1. Oh, the snow has fallen  
And on the shore,  
And it has spread with water,  
Hey, it has spread with water.  

2. It would have been better  
As a girl,  
Than now as a widow.  
Hey, than now as a widow.  

3. Oh, wanderer,  
Just a wanderer,  
By the ford with horses.  
Hey, by the ford with horses.  

4. Those are not horses,  
Those are not horses,  
That is a crane with her children.  
Hey, that is a crane with her children.  

5. As the crane walks  
And catches fish,  
And the female crane steals.  
Hey, and the female crane steals.  

6. Such a foolish girl,  
Such an unwise one,  
That she weeps for a Cossack.  
Hey, that she weeps for a Cossack.  

7. Oh, do not weep, girl,  
Do not weep out of sorrow,  
For this is your fate.  
Hey, for this is your fate.  

8. Oh, do not weep, girl,  
Do not weep, Nastya,  
For such is your happiness.  
Hey, for such is your happiness.

1. The Cossack was mowing hay,  
When there was bad weather,  
The girl was crying,  
When she was young.  
The girl was crying,  
When she was young.  

2. She cried and sobbed,  
Her face wasted away,  
You could tell by the girl,  
She grieved for her wreath.  
You could tell by the girl,  
She grieved for her wreath.  

3. I do not feel sorry for the wreath,  
Nor for any other thing,  
She let her hair down,  
Laid it over her shoulders.  
She let her hair down,  
Laid it over her shoulders.  

4. She let her hair down,  
Laid it over her shoulders,  
Went after a loveless one,  
Brought sorrow upon herself.  
Went after a loveless one,  
Brought sorrow upon herself.  

5. Oh my sorrow, my sorrow,  
Even worse, my grief,  
The boys arrived  
From a faraway land.  
The boys arrived  
From a faraway land.

1. The Cossack was mowing hay,
While the weather was fine,
The girl was crying,
When she was young.
The girl was crying,
When she was young.

2. She wept and sobbed,
Her face grew thin,
She married an unloved man,
Causing sorrow.
She married an unloved man,
Causing sorrow.

3. Oh sorrow, my sorrow,
You are heavy grief,
I loved the girl,
Now I am leaving.
I loved the girl,
Now I am leaving.

4. I loved the girl,
Like the moon loves the stars,
Now I am leaving
Such a beauty.
Now I am leaving
Such a beauty.

5. Leave, leave,
I will not cry,
Because I am young,
I will not be without a partner.
Because I am young,
I will not be without a partner.

6. I have a boyfriend,
He visits me,
Leading a black horse
By the reins.
Leading a black horse
By the reins.

7. A small black horse,
A young lad,
A white-maned horse,
A dark-browed lad.
A white-maned horse,
A dark-browed lad.

1. On that side, at the gathering,  
Two swans are circling:  
On the street, two Cossacks  
Are fighting over a girl.  

2. “Don’t fight, don’t quarrel,  
Let’s go to the master,  
To whom the girl belongs,  
Which Cossack is meant for her.”  

3. The rich man stands before the master,  
Scattering coins,  
The Cossack holds his girl  
By the hand.  

4. When the rich man poured out the money,  
The master even laughed —  
The Cossack married his girl  
On Sunday.

1. Oh, the rose has bloomed  
Behind our garden,  
Do not look, father,  
At the wealthy family.  
Do not look, father,  
At the wealthy family.  

2. Just watch out, father,  
That he is not a drunkard,  
That he does not drink or swear,  
Because I am the only one.  
That he does not drink or swear,  
Because I am the only one.  

3. Because I am the only one,  
The one and only,  
I did not love boys,  
Because I was young.  
I did not love boys,  
Because I was young.  

4. I began to grow up,  
I began to ride up,  
I began to give  
Charm to those boys.  
I began to give  
Charm to those boys.  

5. Charm, charm  
And even more charm,  
I picked berries  
In the cherry orchard.  
I picked berries  
In the cherry orchard.

1. Behind the stony mountain 
Stood a well,
Vasyl’s dear mother
Was watering the horse.
Vasyl’s dear mother
Was watering the horse.
2. But the horse stands, does not drink water,
He blows on the water,
Because he knows, because he hears,
Where Vasyl is spending the night.
Because he knows, because he hears,
Where Vasyl is spending the night.
3. – Oh Vasyl, dear Vasylechko,
Don’t come to me,
You are rich, and I am poor,
You won’t take me.
You are rich, and I am poor,
You won’t take me.
4. – Oh, whether I take you or not,
At least let me be close,
White face, black eyebrows, –
How can I part from you?
White face, black eyebrows, –
How can I part from you?
5. – We will part, dear Vasylechko,
You will leave me...
– You will pay, young maiden,
For coming to see you.
You will pay, young maiden,
For coming to see you.
6. – Did I, Cossack,
Not pay you,
You kissed the white face,
And I did not resist.
You kissed the white face,
And I did not resist.
7. She swept the house, swept the hallway,
And then laughed,
[Went out of the house] her dear mother
And guessed.
[Went out of the house] her dear mother
And guessed.
8. – I know, daughter, I know, daughter,
Why you are laughing:
You have fallen in love with Vasylechko
But don’t admit it.
You have fallen in love with Vasylechko
But don’t admit it.
9. Oh, I was walking to the girl
Through the cherry orchard,
I saw on the oak tree
A pair of pigeons.
I saw on the oak tree
A pair of pigeons.
10. Gray pigeon, gray pigeon,
Grayish female pigeon,
Dear father, dear mother,
Dearer is the girl.
Dear father, dear mother,
Dearer is the girl.
11. And if I quarrel with my father, –
I will accumulate sins,
Having a night with the girl is not idle, –
I will not have enough words.
Having a night with the girl is not idle, –
I will not have enough words.

1. One moon rises,  
And another sets,  
The Cossack goes to the girl, oh,  
Every evening he goes.  
The Cossack goes to the girl, oh,  
Every evening he goes.

2. – Don’t go, Cossack,  
Don’t go, falcon,  
You are rich, I am poor, oh,  
You will never take me.  
You are rich, I am poor, oh,  
You will never take me.

3. – Whether I take you or not,  
I keep coming back,  
I am with you, my darling, oh,  
I cannot say enough.  
I am with you, my darling, oh,  
I cannot say enough.

4. One moon rises,  
And another sets,  
The Cossack goes to the girl, oh,  
As he went, so he goes.  
The Cossack goes to the girl, oh,  
As he went, so he goes.

1. Oh, in the meadow by the road  
Stood a curly oak,  
Under that green oak  
A Cossack was courting a girl.  
Under that green oak  
A Cossack was courting a girl.  

2. And as he was courting her  
He himself mounted his horse:  
"Be well, dark-browed girl,  
For I am going to the army."  
"Be well, dark-browed girl,  
For I am going to the army."  

3. Oh, that cannot be,  
That a dry oak would have leaves.  
Oh, that cannot be,  
That a rich man would marry a poor woman.  
Oh, that cannot be,  
That a rich man would marry a poor woman.  

4. And the rich one is cursed,  
Her hair is loose,  
And the poor one is neat,  
Who has been raised by me.  
And the poor one is neat,  
Who has been raised by me.

1. Oh, beyond the town  
There is sugar beans,  
A Cossack asks,  
Is there a piece of land?  
A Cossack asks,  
Is there a piece of land?  

2. And I, young girl,  
Knew what to say,  
Forgot to ask  
Father at home.  
Forgot to ask  
Father at home.  

3. – Oh father, dear father,  
Do you have any land to give,  
A fine young man loves me,  
He wants to marry.  
A fine young man loves me,  
He wants to marry.  

4. I have no land,  
I have brown eyes,  
And whoever loves them,  
Will not want any land.  
And whoever loves them,  
Will not want any land.  

5. – If you, young maiden,  
Were wealthy,  
I would take your hand,  
And lead you to your father.  
I would take your hand,  
And lead you to your father.  

6. – Look, silly one,  
How many stars are in the sky,  
When you count them all,  
Then I will marry you.  
When you count them all,  
Then I will marry you.

1. Oh, in the field, the hemp plants  
Are green and tall,  
Is such a fate for everyone,  
Or only for me, a young girl?  

2. Oh, if such a fate is for everyone –  
I will stop worrying,  
But if it is for me, a young girl –  
I will go drown myself in the sea.  

3. And on the blue sea  
Stands a marble stone,  
And on that stone  
Stands a black-browed Cossack.  

4. He blinks his eyebrows,  
And waves his hands  
All to that girl  
Who loves faithfully.  

5. Help me, God,  
To take that girl,  
Though I may not acquire cattle,  
I will gain good fortune.  

6. For cattle are God’s dew,  
Here today, gone tomorrow,  
But a young girl  
Loves faithfully forever.

1. [By the grove, the grove,
The green grove,]
There a young girl was plowing
With a black ox.
There a young girl was plowing
With a black ox.

2. She was plowing, plowing,
[She began to sing]
And she hired a Cossack
To play the violin.
And she hired a Cossack
To play the violin.

3. The Cossack plays,
The girl sings,
But who knows his face,
What is he winking at?
But who knows his face,
What is he winking at?

4. Is it at those oxen,
Or at those cows?
Is it at my white face,
Or at my black eyebrows?
Is it at my white face,
Or at my black eyebrows?

5. The oxen and cows,
They die,
But the white face, the black eyebrows
Will never fade.
The white face, the black eyebrows
Will never fade.

1. One mountain is high,  
And the other is low,  
One is dear and distant,  
And the other is near.  
2. I will travel to that distant one myself,  
And I will give the near one  
To the people.  
3. And in that near one  
There are oxen and cows,  
And in that distant one  
There are dark eyebrows.  
4. And in that distant one  
The eyebrows hang on a thread,  
And in that near one  
There is a towel on a peg.

1. Oh, when I was  
My father’s daughter,  
I washed myself*  
With a jug of vodka.  

2. I washed myself  
With a jug of vodka,  
I rubbed myself  
With white chayote.  

3. I rubbed myself  
With white chayote,  
I had for breakfast  
Pies with sour cream.  

4. Oh, when I went  
After a bad fate,  
I did not have time to wash  
With cold water.  

5. I did not have time to wash  
With cold water,  
I did not have time to rub myself  
With the sleeve of my swaddle.  

6. I did not have time to rub myself  
With the sleeve of my swaddle,  
I did not have time to eat  
A dry biscuit with water.  

*Note: "Умивалася" literally means "washed (oneself)"; here it implies the morning ritual of washing.

Filename: Vinnytsia_788_Oi viidu ya na tu horu_(13-ЛР-02)
translation:
1. Oh, I will climb that mountain,  
Look down at the valley:  
The valley is wide,  
The guelder-rose is tall,  
Its branches bend all the way down.  
The valley is wide,  
The guelder-rose is tall,  
Its branches bend all the way down.  

2. And under this guelder-rose  
A young man stood with a girl,  
The girl is crying,  
She sheds tears,  
She curses her fate:  
The girl is crying,  
She sheds tears,  
She curses her fate:  

3. – Oh, if only I had known,  
I would not have married, I would have stayed a maiden,  
With my dear father,  
And my dear mother,  
A young maiden.  
With my dear father,  
And my dear mother,  
A young maiden.  

4. But now I will know  
To go to bed late, wake up early,  
I lay down late,  
I rose early,  
I cursed my fate.  
I lay down late,  
I rose early,  
I cursed my fate.

1. Oh, my mother gave birth to me  
On a holy Sunday,  
She gave me a cruel fate,  
Where I was born.  

2. – Go away, go away, cruel fate,  
And get lost in the forest,  
And leave me, a young girl,  
Stop tormenting me.  

3. – Though I go, my dear,  
And get lost in the forest,  
When you come to gather firewood,  
I will cling to you.  

4. Go away, go away, cruel fate,  
And drown yourself in the Danube,  
And leave me, a young girl,  
Stop tormenting me.  

5. – Though I go, my dear,  
And drown in the Danube,  
When you come to fetch water,  
I will cling to you.  

6. Oh, you will come to fetch water  
With two buckets,  
Then I will cling to you  
With both hands.  

7. I will take my fate  
To the city to sell,  
People say she’s lazy,  
No one wants to buy her.  

8. – Oh, buy, good people,  
My fate is good,  
She drinks in the tavern, fights with people,  
Doesn’t spend nights at home.  

9. Good people give me  
Just one sixpence,  
But I thought to myself,  
Misery even without that.  

10. I will harness my cruel fate  
To the oak sled,  
I will sit myself and go,  
Like a noble lady.

1. Oh, the sun is on the mountain,  
In the valley there is fog,  
Hey, something in my little heart  
Is longing and sorrow.  

2. Why is there this longing? –  
My beloved is not here,  
He left for a while,  
He will come now.  

3. I look out the little window, –  
My dear is coming,  
On his right little hand  
He carries a little gift.  

4. What kind of gift is that? –  
A wire fish trap...  
– Hide me, mother,  
Oh, hide me.  

5. I hid from my beloved,  
Locked myself up,  
With golden locks.

1. I will sow rye,  
Yes, I will sow rye,  
Post...  
I will set a guard.  

2. An unfaithful guard,  
Yes, an unfaithful guard,  
Gently...  
The rye is nibbled.  

3. The wind blows,  
Yes, the wind blows,  
The rye...  
The rye bends.  

4. The rye bends,  
Yes, the rye bends,  
Mother...  
Mother scolds her daughter.  

5. Mother scolds her daughter,  
Yes, mother scolds her daughter,  
Not allowing...  
Not allowing her to go out.  

6. – Let me go, mother,  
Yes, let me go, mother,  
For a while...  
For a while to walk.  

7. For a while, for a little while,  
Yes, for a while, for a little while,  
To let out...  
To let out a little voice.  

8. Let him hear,  
Yes, let him hear,  
Who spends the night...  
Who spends the night in the forest.  

9. With horses and oxen,  
Yes, with horses and oxen,  
With black...  
With black eyebrows.

1. And the cloud is coming,  
And the cloud is coming,  
The rain... the rain is drizzling.  

2. And the rain is drizzling,  
And the rain is drizzling,  
Father... father is scolding his daughter.  

3. And father is scolding his daughter,  
And father is scolding his daughter,

My dear, my darling,  
– But there’s no strength,  
My dear, my darling,  
– Die, darling,  
– Die, darling,  
– Die, u-ha-ha,  
My dark-browed one!

4. – And with whom will you live out your days,  
My dear, my darling,  
– And with whom will you live out your days,  
My dear, my darling,  
– With my godmother, darling,  
– With my godmother, darling,  
– With my godmother, u-ha-ha,  
My dark-browed one!

Filename: Vinnytsia_926_Ny zhal myni na toi horshchyk_(ЖТ-ЛР)
translation:
1. I do not pity that pot  
That boils with water;  
I do not pity the priest  
Who married with trouble.  
I do not pity the priest  
Who married with trouble.  

2. Oh, I will go to the priest,  
Speak quietly:  
– Divorce me, priest,  
Without trouble, without harm.  
– Divorce me, priest,  
Without trouble, without harm.  

3. – If you gave me money, –  
One must marry,  
If you took trouble upon yourself, –  
I am not to blame.  
If you took trouble upon yourself, –  
I am not to blame.

1. People say that I am wicked,  
But I am Vasilykha,  
I finished all the work,  
I quietly go home.  
I finished all the work,  
I quietly go home.  

2. I finished all the work  
With new sickles,  
I enter the tavern –  
My dear is drinking with the godfathers.  
I enter the tavern –  
My dear is drinking with the godfathers.  

3. – Enough, enough, my dear,  
Drinking with the godfathers,  
Go, go to the field  
To carry the sheaves.  
Go, go to the field  
To carry the sheaves.  

4. – Oh, I haven’t reaped, oh, I haven’t reaped,  
I won’t carry,  
Step back, ragged one,  
Or I will keep drinking.  
Step back, ragged one,  
Or I will keep drinking.  

5. – Oh, though I am ragged,  
I am the mistress,  
There are four bundles of straw in the house,  
And I am for the fifth.  
There are four bundles of straw in the house,  
And I am for the fifth.  

6. Merchants came from Bar  
To buy straw:  
– What will you, little scamp,  
Give me for this straw?  
– What will you, little scamp,  
Give me for this straw?  

7. They give me forty kopecks,  
I don’t want to take it,  
The old people tell me  
To take it to the field.  
The old people tell me  
To take it to the field.  

8. The old people tell me  
To bring it to the field,  
Oh, there will be a hundred kips  
Of wheat growing.  
Oh, there will be a hundred kips  
Of wheat growing.  

9. And Vasyl harvested  
A hundred kips of wheat,  
Vasyl gathered for Vasylisya  
A green skirt.  
Vasyl gathered for Vasylisya  
A green skirt.  

10. Vasyl gathered for Vasylisya  
A green skirt,  
I look here and there,  
A beautiful maiden.  
I look here and there,  
A beautiful maiden.

1. Oh, Savradym has died  
And lies on the bench,  
His wife, Savradymka,  
Is running for vodka.  
His wife, Savradymka,  
Is running for vodka.  

2. She brings vodka,  
And leads the music:  
Now, Savradym,  
I am not afraid of you!  
Now, Savradym,  
I am not afraid of you!  

3. When she went to the grave,  
She wept,  
When she left the grave,  
She skipped.  
When she left the grave,  
She skipped.  

4. Stamping her little feet,  
Lie here, my dear, here,  
And I am young,  
Happy to have fun.  
And I am young,  
Happy to have fun.

1. She lived – didn’t love,  
And died – didn’t grieve,  
And I won’t grieve,  
Because I had no one to live with!  
And I won’t grieve,  
Because I had no one to live with!

2. When he lay on the bench,  
I felt a little pity,  
When they took him off the bench,  
They took all my pity away!  
When they took him off the bench,  
They took all my pity away!

3. They are carrying him down the ravine,  
And the boys are swarming after me!  
And I cry and sob,  
And wink to the boys.  
And I cry and sob,  
And wink to the boys.

4. When I was going to the grave,  
I cried my eyes out,  
When I was leaving the grave,  
I jumped up.  
When I was leaving the grave,  
I jumped up.

5. I stomped with my little feet,  
Clapped with my little hands:  
“Lie down, dear, so you don’t get up,  
So a better one comes to me!”  
“Lie down, dear, so you don’t get up,  
So a better one comes to me!”

6. Here you lie in the pit,  
Because you scolded my mother,  
Here is your house,  
Because you scolded my father!  
Here is your house,  
Because you scolded my father!

7. I rang with a pumpkin,  
And rolled with a radish;  
And cursed with a turnip,  
Because I had a good life!  
And cursed with a turnip,  
Because I had a good life!

8. I praise God for this,  
That I married three times:  
I buried the bad one,  
And married the good one!  
I buried the bad one,  
And married the good one!

1. Oh, my darling went  
To the mill,  
And I, with the guys,  
Slaughtered a boar.  
And I, with the guys,  
Slaughtered a boar.  

2. Slaughtered a boar –  
It weighs ten pounds,  
And to me, a young woman,  
All sorts of devils come creeping.  
And to me, a young woman,  
All sorts of devils come creeping.  

3. I sowed some hemp –  
Strange chickens pecked it away,  
Slaughtered a boar –  
The guys came and ate it.  
Slaughtered a boar –  
The guys came and ate it.  

4. When my darling came back:  
“Come on, wife, let's eat.”  
And I served him  
Bones to gnaw on the plate.  
And I served him  
Bones to gnaw on the plate.  

5. “Oh you, my dear, my darling,  
Where did you get the bones?  
When I went to the mill,  
We didn’t have any lard.  
When I went to the mill,  
We didn’t have any lard.”  

6. “And you, dear, my darling,  
Don’t ask about that lard,  
Just run to the barn  
And count all the pigs.  
Just run to the barn  
And count all the pigs.”  

7. When my darling went  
To the barn to count the pigs,  
I stood still and thought,  
What should I say?  
I stood still and thought,  
What should I say?  

8. I stood still and thought,  
What should I say?  
I looked out the window –  
And there was the chairman.  
I looked out the window –  
And there was the chairman.  

9. He is coming, coming, the head.  
The chairman is coming,  
All because of that boar,  
That they will question about.  
All because of that boar,  
That they will question about.  

10. If only that boar  
Had never been born,  
Because of that boar  
The investigation happened.  
Because of that boar  
The investigation happened.

1. – Where are you, dear,
Hiding,
That the world’s little star
Has started to shine.
That the world’s little star
Has started to shine.  
2. – There I was, dear,
On the doorstep,
Oh, there our little white one
Has brought a little bull.
Oh, there our little white one
Has brought a little bull.  
3. My dear went
To look at the bull,
And I took the boy by the hand
And led him out of the house.
And I took the boy by the hand
And led him out of the house.

1. How the Moldovan went  
Into the open field through the weeds,  
Oh dana, oh dana, I,  

2. He didn’t bring any weeds,  
His cart broke down,  
Oh dana, oh dana, I.  

3. No cart, nor spokes,  
A full measure of young women,  
Oh dana, oh dana, I.  

4. When he started hugging them,  
Gave them three rubles each,  
Oh dana, oh dana, I.  

5. Three chervonets remained,  
That’s for my wife’s scarf,  
Oh dana, oh dana, I.

1. [As my dear one went]  
To the field, to the steppe,  
And I left that distaff  
By the stove—snap!  

2. My distaff lies,  
Lies there, swollen,  
And I, a young woman,  
Went and got drunk.  

3. My dear one returns,  
From the field to home,  
Asks the little children:  
– Where is your mother?  

4. – Our mother, our mother  
Lies drunk, asleep.  
– Quiet, children, quiet, children,  
Do not wake mother.  

5. – May you be healthy, my dear,  
For drinking that sluggish  
Ox away.  

6. – That sluggish ox won’t help you, dear,  
You have on your stable  
A little black horse.  

7. Black horse, black horse,  
White hooves,  
Behind you, my dear,  
Never beaten once.

1. Oh, the clouds are gathering, it will rain,
God and people will find out,
What will happen to my beloved,
My dark-browed one.
What will happen to my beloved,
My dark-browed one.

2. My dear was riding on the road,
I drank away the cow,
Oh, bitterly, I repent,
Washing with tears.
Oh, bitterly, I repent,
Washing with tears.

3. My dear was returning from the road,
And I fell at his feet:
– What have I done, dear?
I drank away the little cow.
– What have I done, dear?
I drank away the little cow.

4. With dry Paraska,
With godmother Palazhka,
With godfather Ivan,
With neighbor Stepan.
With godfather Ivan,
With neighbor Stepan.

5. My dear laments for his dear,
Gifts the little cow:
– Drink, my dear, no trouble,
There will be a second cow (Wednesday).
– Drink, my dear, no trouble,
There will be a second cow (Wednesday).

1. My Svashko,  
Little chattering one,  
Drink a little glass,  
My dear.  
Drink a little glass,  
My dear.  

2. My Svashko,  
Little chattering one,  
Half a glass,  
My dear.  
Half a glass,  
My dear.  

3. My Svashko,  
Little chattering one,  
Until the morning,  
My dear.  
Until the morning,  
My dear.

1. On Sunday I drink, drink,  
On Monday I sleep, sleep,  
And on Tuesday I harvest forty sheaves  
Of golden wheat, reap, reap.  
And on Tuesday I harvest forty sheaves  
Of golden wheat, reap, reap.  

2. On Wednesday I carried,  
On Thursday I threshed,  
On Friday I winnowed,  
And on Saturday I measured.  
On Friday I winnowed,  
And on Saturday I measured.  

3. And on Sunday I sold it,  
I drank with the boys,  
Glory to You, Lord,  
That I got the work done!  
Glory to You, Lord,  
That I got the work done!  

4. And I am not for work,  
I am not for labor,  
To have fun with the boys —  
That is my desire.  
To have fun with the boys —  
That is my desire.  

5. "With the boys, my daughter,  
I do not forbid you,  
Only, daughter, be careful,  
When you lie down, take care.  
Only, daughter, be careful,  
When you lie down, take care."  

6. The devil won’t guard you,  
If he wants, he will torment you.  
The devil won’t guard you,  
If he wants, he will torment you.  
The devil won’t guard you,  
If he wants, he will torment you.

Chizhik, chizhik, little chizh,
A fine fellow, a little man!
Mows well, reaps well,
Drinks vodka well!

On the horse, on the horse,  
On every hoof,  
If there is no horse,  
We know where it is buried.

1. – And who is drinking?  
– Ivan's wife.  
– And who is drinking?  
– His quail.  

2. – Drink a cup of wine,  
For you are a good wife:  
Not a little dry, nor a little sharp,  
Tug, tug, tug  
Right until morning!  

3. There's no noise in the head,  
No pain in the belly,  
Only walked into the legs, –  
They stomped a little!

1. The little goat was running over the ice,
Good, good vodka with honey.
2. The goat was running to drown herself,
And the little billy goat was watching.
3. And the little goat – into the ice hole,
And the little billy goat – by the head:
Pulled up!
Be healthy!
4. Tomorrow we will still drink,
Tomorrow we will live even better,
Be healthy!

1. Dzyundzyurykha danced with Dzyundzyur,  
She lost a small pouch with tobacco.  
Oh woe, Dzyundzyurkha, woe,  
She quietly lost the small pouch!  
Oh woe, Dzyundzyurkha, woe,  
She quietly lost the small pouch!  

2. Romanykha danced with Roman,  
She lost a small purse with tobacco.  
Oh woe, Romanykha, woe,  
She quietly lost the small purse!  
Oh woe, Romanykha, woe,

Many years, (6)
As if from frame to summer,
Let's go to the field,
Let's reap the rye,
Let's set the sheaves,
Let's strike the hops:
Hop, hop, hop, hop, hop,
And a shot of vodka: clap!
Hop, hop, hop, hop, hop,
And a shot of vodka: clap!
Clap!

1. The father is herding the flock, ay, si-tir-dai-dom,  
The son is carrying the accordion, ay, si-tir-dai-dom.  
2. The father is leading the flock, ay, si-tir-dai-dom,  
The son is playing the accordion, ay, si-tir-dai-dom.  
3. A raspberry-colored shirt, ay, si-tir-dai-dom,  
There is not a piece of bread in the house, ay, si-tir-dai-dom.

1. I had  
a steam engine,  
but I sold it,  
and with that money  
I hired good musicians,  
hired, hired:  
– Oh, my musicians,  
oh, play for me  
the blacksmith, the blacksmith.

and dances on one leg,  
tra-la-la, tra-la-la.

a barren cow,  
but I sold it,  
and with that money

oh, play for me  
the blacksmith, the blacksmith.

and dances on one leg,  
tra-la-la, tra-la-la.

3. I had  
a red cow,

oh, play for me  
the blacksmith, the blacksmith.

Filename: Vinnytsia_950_Oi khodyla ya v lis po openki_(ЖТ-Г)
translation:
1. Oh, I went to the forest for honey fungus,  
I came across a green oak,  
Here and there I placed the little oak,  
I gathered a bundle of acorns.  
Here and there I placed the little oak,  
I gathered a bundle of acorns.  

2. Oh, it seems like a small bundle,  
I sold the acorns for three zlotys,  
I sold the acorns for three zlotys,  
With that money, I hired musicians.  
I sold the acorns for three zlotys,  
With that money, I hired musicians.

Filename: Vinnytsia_951_Chy u vas, chy u nas_(ЖТ-ЛР-куми)
translation:
Whether with you or with us,  
When women get angry,  
They end up falling on the godmother,  
Four or five at a time.

1. – Where are you going, where are you going,  
Little man?  
– To the godmother, to the godmother  
For the whole night.  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

2. – Oh don't go, oh don't go,  
Dinner is ready!  
– Oh I will go, oh I will go,  
Godmother with black eyebrows!  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

3. – Oh don't go, oh don't go,  
There will be gossip!  
– Oh I will go, oh I will go,  
Because the godmother has vodka!  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

4. – It’s good, godfather, that you came,  
Godmother, the garden bed,  
It’s good, godfather, that you came,  
Mine went to the field...  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

5. – Oh give me, my godmother,  
What you promised  
By that little jug,  
That took the water:  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

6. Oh let’s meet, dear godfather,  
In the thick hazel grove  
And let’s drink, dear godfather,  
A good glass.  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.  

7. – Quiet, quiet, dear godfather,  
And go home,  
If anyone asks,  
Don't tell anyone.  
Oh trouble, what a noise,  
I forgot that you are a godfather.

Oh, godfather, a joy to godmother,  
He led her into the vineyard:  
– Eat, godmother, the berries,  
Some are sweet,  
And some are bitter,  
Those are for my wife.  
And some are bitter,  
Those are for my wife.

1. The man is not at home,  
He went somewhere to the mill,  
And I will go look  
For my godfather Vasyl.  
And I will go look  
For my godfather Vasyl.  

2. I was going to the market,  
Dressed up,  
I met  
My godfather Vasyl.  
I met  
My godfather Vasyl.  

3. And on the godfather are boots  
With lace,  
The godfather walked along the furrow,  
The godmother along the path.  
The godfather walked along the furrow,  
The godmother along the path.  

4. – Oh, my dear godmother,  
I have vodka with me,  
And so no one sees us,  
Let's sit in the elderberry bush.  
And so no one sees us,  
Let's sit in the elderberry bush.  

5. In the garden is an elderberry bush,  
Two godparents sit,  
They drank vodka,  
Both are merry.  
They drank vodka,  
Both are merry.  

6. Both got drunk,  
Started rolling around,  
The godmother hugged the godfather,  
Started kissing him.  
The godmother hugged the godfather,  
Started kissing him.  

7. When the husband came  
And entered the house:  
– Where is the woman, why is she not here?  
He went to look for her.  
– Where is the woman, why is she not here?  
He went to look for her.  

8. After a while  
He went through the garden:  
Oh, what a wonder,  
The elderberry bush is swaying.  
Oh, what a wonder,  
The elderberry bush is swaying.  

9. The man quietly  
Approaches closely,  
When he looked into the elderberry, –  
The godfather is kissing the godmother.  
When he looked into the elderberry, –  
The godfather is kissing the godmother.  

10. When he jumped into the elderberry,  
The branches crackled,  
He grabbed the godmother by the pants,  
The godmother – by the rags.  
He grabbed the godmother by the pants,  
The godmother – by the rags.  

11. Oh, what a wonder, –  
The elderberry bush is swaying.  
Oh, what a wonder, –  
The elderberry bush is swaying.  
Oh, what a wonder, –  
The elderberry bush is swaying.

Filename: Vinnytsia_956_Oi za shcho zh ty, moia myla_(ЖТ-ЛР-Д3)
translation:
1. – Oh, why, my dear,  
Did you get drunk?  
Only one kopeck  
Is left.  
Only one kopeck  
Is left.  

2. Good day, neighbors,  
Good day,  
Lend me a husband  
At least for a week.  
Lend me a husband  
At least for a week.  

3. – I will lend – I will lend  
A sieve and a strainer,  
But I won’t lend a husband,  
I’m young myself.  
I won’t lend a husband,  
I’m young myself.  

4. You will plow with a plow, you will plow with a plow,  
You will drag with a harrow,  
Because you will wear out  
My husband.  
Because you will wear out  
My husband.

1. – Good day, dear godfather,  
Good day,  
Lend me a husband  
Even for a week.  
Lend me a husband  
Even for a week.  
2. – I won’t lend him, dear godmother,  
I won’t lend him,  
Because I won’t call him  
Back home.  
Because I won’t call him  
Back home.

1. The ducks pecked at the buckwheat,  
What will I have, poor me?  
And every guy has a girl,  
But the devil gave me a hag.  
And every guy has a girl,  
But the devil gave me a hag.  

2. I won’t lose money on the hag,  
I’ll sell the hag — buy a nag,  
If the nag dies — I’ll skin it,  
And still buy myself a girl.  
If the nag dies — I’ll skin it,  
And still buy myself a girl.

Grandfather went for lunch,  
Grandmother to the christening;  
Grandfather came back without boots,  
Grandmother without a headscarf.

Filename: Vinnytsia_960_Stara ba... stara baba, yak kholiera_(ЖТ-ЛР)
translation:
1. An old woman... an old woman like cholera,  
And still wants... still wants a suitor.  
2. An old man... an old man like a stump,  
And still wants... still wants to go to the girls.  
3. A woman climbed... a woman climbed the ladder  
And then fell... and then fell among the pigs.

1. An old man got married,  
Oh-yo-yoi,  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  

2. They went to lie down to sleep,  
He started looking for a comedy:  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  

3. – Have you, woman, gone crazy,  
Where did you put the comedy?  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  
An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  

4. – I detached it,

An old man got married  
And took a woman forty years old,  
And the cuckoo keeps calling.  
An old man got married

1. Oh, the grandmother said to the grandfather:
– I will go to America
And I will travel through Europe,
Stay at home, you fool.
And I will travel through Europe,
Stay at home, you fool.

2. The grandmother quickly got ready,
Set off for America,
She rides proudly,
Because she has a full bag.
She rides proudly,
Because she has a full bag.

3. Arrived in Vinnytsia,
Left without a skirt,
Arrived in Kyiv,
And already ate everything in the bag.
Arrived in Kyiv,
And already ate everything in the bag.

4. Arrived all the way to Chop,
Left without even the chop,
Came barefoot to the border,
Already wanted to go home.
Came barefoot to the border,
Already wanted to go home.

5. Stopped at customs,
...
They ask: – Where is your visa?
Don’t be so reckless, grandma.
No visa, don’t dress up
And return home.
No visa, don’t dress up
And return home.

6. When she came home
And met the grandfather,
And met the grandfather,
But the grandfather has a grandmother.
And met the grandfather,
But the grandfather has a grandmother.

7. This is my dear grandma
Who cooked dumplings,
Who cooked dumplings,
Nicely treated the grandfather.
Who cooked dumplings,
Nicely treated the grandfather.

8. If grandma had sense,
She wouldn't have gone anywhere,
But cooked dumplings,
And didn't look for America.
But cooked dumplings,
And didn't look for America.

1. Oh, the old people were talking,  
The old people were talking:  
– There will be a wedding in our village, once!

2. Oh, there will be a wedding in our village,  
There will be a wedding in our village,  
A mosquito will propose to a fly, once!

3. The fly fell in love with the little mosquito,  
The fly fell in love with the little mosquito,  
She gave him honey and wine to drink, once!

4. Oh, the mosquito flew into the little forest,  
Oh, the mosquito flew into the little forest,  
And he sat on an oak, once!

5. Oh, a strong wind stormed,  
A strong wind stormed,  
And blew the mosquito off the oak, once!

6. Oh, what kind of deceased will that be,  
What kind of deceased will that be,  
Is he a lieutenant or a colonel, once!

7. Oh, not a lieutenant, not a colonel,  
Not a lieutenant, not a colonel,  
But he is the lover of a woman, once!

1. They killed a mosquito,  
Baked some cookies,  
And Mr. Kanyovsky arrived,  
Took it from his pocket.  
2. And the magpie with a white flank  
Is fond of that meat,  
Flies, shouts, exclaims:  
“Give me some meat, sir.”

1. A carrot Lyashok was riding,  
A beetle grasshopper,  
And a parsnip cap,  
A burdock coat.  

2. Mailboxes made of turnip,  
Suspenders of pumpkin,  
A pistol from a cabbage head,  
A belt from corn.  

3. The grasshopper started to gallop underneath him,  
Began to wag its tail,  
Lyashok sat on the horse,  
Started to tremble violently.  

4. Lyashok rides, Lyashok rides,  
The horse dances beneath him,  
His pigs ran across:  
"Wait, Lyashok."  

5. Lyashok drew his pistol,

Filename: Vinnytsia_997_Choho ptashka zaletila_(Пісня виселянки на Сибір)
translation:
1. – Why did the little bird fly  
To distant lands,  
Why didn’t the little bird live there,  
Where your family lives?  
2. – The little bird flew without enemies  
To distant lands,  
Without enemies it did not live there,  
Where my family lives.  
3. Without enemies it did not live there,  
Where my family lives,  
I entered a distant country  
And found a shelter for myself.  
4. I entered a distant country,  
Here I found a place to live,  
When I remember my homeland, –  
Sadness fills my heart.  
5. When I remember my homeland,  
Sadness fills my heart,  
And it seems I do not cry,  
But tears flow like a river.  
6. Wherever I look, wherever I gaze, –  
It is all a foreign land,  
When I meet a deportee, –  
That is all my family.  
7. When I meet a deportee, –  
There is only someone to talk to:  
“Come, friend, let us unite  
And live together.”  
8. Do not grieve, dear friend,  
That this is a foreign land,  
If we live in friendship,  
Then we will be all one family.

1. Oh, in the cherry orchard,

There the nightingale was singing.

I dreamt of hazel eyes,

4. I will send a little duck onto the water,

Let him roam freely.  
I will give my dear freedom,

Filename: Kmeln_199_Oi na vodi ta y na doshchechtsi
translation:
1. Oh, on the water and on the board,  
There a girl was washing herself.  
2. She was rinsing and washing,  
Looking at the young nobleman.  
3. – Look, my dear young nobleman,  
At the little shoes I have.  
4. My father bought me these shoes,  
So that my young nobleman would love me.  
5. Mother gave me stockings,  
So that I could be a young lady.

translation:
1. Oh, by the sea and on the plank,  
There a young lady is washing herself.  
2. She is washing and washing,  
Taking off her little shoes:  
3. – Look, gentlemen,  
What little shoes I have.  
4. The gentleman’s father bought the shoes,  
So that a good young man would love me.  
5. The lady’s mother gave me a scarf,  
So that I would be her young lady.  
6. From the viburnum meadow,  
Choose yourself a partner.

So that a good young man would love me.  
4. My lady mother gave me a handkerchief,
