Vysvarovetsky Pond  
All merged together.  
2. There Vasylko  
And Ivan  
Fought over Hanusya.  
3. – Don’t fight,  
Don’t quarrel,  
I won’t be yours,  
Hey, I won’t be yours.  
4. I will buy a handkerchief  
For Vasylko,  
I will be Petrova,  
Hey, I will be Petrova.

1. Oh, under the white birch tree,  
Oh, it is neatly swept there.  
2. Oh, there is Maritsa with goods, with goods.  
With what kind of goods? – With hats.  
3. I sell to all the boys,  
Only to Hryhorets I give on credit.

1. Oh, in the cherry orchard,  
There the little cuckoo is calling.  
2. Oh, there the cherry tree is blooming,  
The Cossack is leading his horse.  
3. He leads the horse to the water  
With silk reins.  
4. While he was watering the horse,  
He stood with the girl.  
5. Oh, what does it matter to me  
That I stand with her?  
6. If only the Lord would grant me  
That I marry her.

1. The moon rolled over the path,  
And our Ivanko is lame on one leg.  
2. Give a stick – to lean on,  
To get to Hannusia.  
3. Hannusia has fierce dogs,  
No one can go out, they chase everyone away.  
4. Hannusia came out and spoke,  
She drove the fierce dogs away:  
5. “Fierce dogs, sleep in the straw!  
And you, Ivanko, go into the house!  
6. And you, Ivanko, go into the house:  
Should I give you a little towel?  
7. She gave the little towel, tied his hands,  
And also hugged and kissed him.

1. Oh, in the field, the field  
Under the buckwheat  
A little horse is grazing  
And with a bridle.  
2. – Oh horse, horse,  
Let yourself be caught,  
Let yourself be caught  
And even saddled.  
3. Because we will ride  
To drink mead-beer,  
[To drink mead-beer,]  
To court a girl.  
4. And that girl  
Was tying buckwheat,  
Was tying buckwheat,  
She said to the boy:  
5. – I don’t like beer,  
Because it makes noise,  
I don’t like a show-off,  
Because he is arrogant.  
6. I like honey,  
Because it is sweet,  
I like the boy –  
Because he is young.

1. Rivers flowed, they murmured  
To Zinunya without shade.  
2. And in Zinunya was a cherry orchard,  
There they gathered the boys in a warehouse.  
3. Oh, they gathered, gathered,  
They gave honey-wine.  
4. And Vasylko gave more,  
Because he liked Zinunya.  
5. Oh, he liked, liked the black horse,  
She gave him a scarf made of silk.

1. Oh, the red sun is rising,  
Shining,  
Young Petrunko,  
Hey, is saddling his horse.  
2. He saddled his little horse  
By the candlelight,  
Riding to Manyusya,  
Hey, at night.  
3. When he arrived under the window,  
He whistled,  
Young Manyusya,  
Hey, was not at home.  
4. Young Manyusya  
Was somewhere,  
That from midnight till dawn,  
Hey, she was not out.  
5. And in the evening, she was combing  
Her fair hair,  
And at midnight, putting on  
Hey, her shoes.  
6. And at midnight, putting on  
Her shoes,  
At dawn, washing  
Hey, her white face.  
7. At dawn, washing  
Her white face,  
Waiting for young Ivan,  
Hey, waiting.

1. Kupala, girl,  
Kupala,  
The sun was shining  
On Ivan.  

2. Where the sun walks,  
There it plays,  
There Ivan saddles  
His little horse.  

3. Saddle up, saddle up the little horse  
By the candle,  
Because you will ride to the midnight  
Feast.  

4. When he arrived at the meadow,  
He whistled,  
But I did not find  
My Marusya anymore.  

5. – And somewhere my Marusya  
Has grown splendid,  
So she did not come out  
To the feast with me.  

6. – Oh, she did not come out to the feast,  
She had no time,  
Because since the evening they were combing  
The dew from her hair.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There a little swallow was bathing.  
2. There the little swallow was bathing,  
On the shore it was drying itself.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There a little swallow was bathing.  
2. There a little swallow was bathing,  
On the shore she was drying herself.

1. Oh, in the field there is a little spring,  
A little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. She bathed, washed herself,  
Dried herself on the shore.  
3. She dried herself on the shore,  
There Marusya was sorrowful:  
4. She had not yet woven the towels,  
But she already loved Vasylko.  
5. The towels were still on the loom,  
But Vasylko was already sitting in the house.

1. Oh, on Ivana, on Kupala,  
There a swallow was bathing.  
2. Oh, it was bathing in the river,  
On the little shore it was drying.  
3. On the little shore it was drying,  
The girl Marusya was grieving.

1. Oh, in the garden, the bathhouse flower,  
There the little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. On the little shore, she was drying herself,  
The girl Marusya was grieving:  
3. I haven't even finished washing the scarves,  
And I already like Ivan.  
4. I still have scarves on the loom,  
And Ivan is already in the house.

1. Oh, on the garden, a marsh marigold,  
There a little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. On the little shore, she was drying herself,  
The magpie daughter was sighing,  
3. Because she hadn’t even washed the handkerchief yet,  
But Ivanka was already liked.  
4. Because the handkerchief was still on the loom,  
But Ivanko was already in her house.

1. Oh, Shulyak flew through the street  
And kidnapped some worker woman.  
2. After him, Ivan: – Hey, hey, hey, hey!  
Give back that woman, give her back, give her back.  
3. Give back that worker woman  
[And take yourself] a lazy one.

1. Oh, a bunting was flying without a stick,  
He kidnapped Zoyunya, the worker.  
2. Volodya runs: – Hey, hey, hey, hey,  
Give back Zoyunya, give her back, give her back.  
3. My Zoyunya is a worker  
Also a gardener in the vegetable patch.  
4. Oh, she plows the field until she sweats. –  
Volodya runs until he faints.  
       *       *       *  
1. Oh, in the garden there is a burdock, burdock,  
May the boys’ bellies swell because of it.  
2. Oh, let them swell, let them know,  
Let them not break the water lilies.  
3. Because that water lily is linked to the moon,  
Maybe some will get married.

1. On Kupala Night, on Ivan's Day,  
A beautifully dressed girl came out.  
2. The boys were watching her,  
But were shy to greet her.  
3. But Ivanko was not shy—  
He approached Hanusia and greeted her.

1. On St. John’s Day, Jana,  
Nastunya was beautifully dressed.  
2. The boys are walking, they meet,  
They are shy to greet.  
3. But Volodya was not shy,  
He took her hand and greeted her.  
4. He took her hand and greeted,  
Asked about her father:  
5. – You, young Nastunya,  
Is your father at home?  
6. – Oh, he is at home sometimes, –  
He walks around the garden.  
7. He walks around the garden,  
Looking for his son-in-law.

1. – Oh you, Maryko, oh you,  
Where can I come to you?  
– Through the garden, Ivanyu, through the garden,  
Good horilka with honey.  
2. And a little pillow under the side  
Also for the little horse as a bridle.  
Young Maryka  
Pressed close to her heart.

1. Oh, the little star was shining,  
It was shining.  
– With whom, Zoyunya,  
Were you standing?  

2. – With you, Volodya,  
With you,  
Like the bright moon  
With the star.  

3. – How did you, Zoyunya,  
Recognize  
That you called me the moon?  

4. – By your speech, Volodya,  
By your speech,  
And also by your tall hat.  

5. And also by your tall hat,  
That mother bought  
In Lviv.  
       *       *       *  
1. Oh, on the hill, the little wheat  
Was standing,  
There young Zoyunya  
Was reaping.  

2. When Volodya arrived,  
He took off his hat:  
– God help you, Zoyunya.  

3. [God help you, Zoyunya,]  
From this little wheat  
There will be a loaf,  

4. From this little wheat  
There will be seed,  
You will have, Zoyunya,  
A wedding.  

5. On Saturday, Zoyunya,  
On Saturday,  
I desire you.  

6. – On Sunday, Volodya,  
On Sunday,  
I have hope for you.  
       *       *       *  
1. Oh, in the forest, in the grove,  
On the oak,  
There hung a cradle  
On a cord.  

2. Oh, who was rocking  
In that cradle?  
There young Volodya  
Was swaying.  

3. – Rock me, boys,  
High up,  
Let me look far away.  

4. Where my Zoyunya  
Is passing by,  
Wearing embroidered sleeves,  
She glimmers.  

5. [Wearing embroidered sleeves,]  
She shines with golden rings.

1. Oh, up above, little sun,  
Up above,  
Ivanko is setting out  
The pantry.  
2. When his Hanusya  
Comes to him,  
She quietly  
Speaks to him:  
3. – You’re setting out the pantry, Ivanko,  
Break, break a little window  
From the outside.  
4. So that a nightingale  
Would fly to me,  
So that he would wake me  
Early in the morning.  
5. – Why do you need a nightingale, Hanusya?  
– I have a dear mother.  
6. She will wake you early,  
And when she comes outside  
She will scold:  
7. – The wandering daughter-in-law,  
She sleeps, she sleeps,  
Oh, how her little head  
Does not ache?  
8. Oh, how her little head  
Does not ache?  
Oh, how she does not want  
To work?

1. Oh, mothers, mothers, with the May celebration  
To a foreign village after a girl.  
2. Oh, the night of St. Peter’s feast is short  
Our girl did not get enough sleep.  
3. She did not get enough sleep, did not have fun,  
Did not spend the night with the Cossack.

1. The first night of Petrivka,  
Maria the girl did not sleep well.  
Chorus:  
Kupaylo, Kupaylo on Ivan's day!  
2. For three nights she did not sleep,  
She was twisting the cords.  
Chorus.

1. I had a short night  
And I did not get enough sleep.  
2. May the rooster be annoyed  
That he woke me up so early.  
3. Cause, God, the night  
For my dark eyes.  
4. Cause also the second one  
For me, a young woman.

1. Oh, in the wheat field,  
In the field,  
Hannusenka was picking  
From the couch grass.  
2. And Vasylunya arrived,  
Took off his hat:  
– God help you, Hannununya!  
Help!  
3. God help you, Hannununya!  
Help!  
There will be from this wheat  
A loaf of bread.  
4. There will be from this wheat  
Seeds,  
When is your wedding,  
Hannununya?  
5. – On Saturday, Vasylunya,  
On Saturday,  
Because I have a desire  
For you.  
6. On Sunday, Vasylunya,  
On Sunday,  
Because I have hope  
For you.

1. Oh, in the field, the wheat  
Was standing,  
Oh, there a girl  
Was reaping it.  
2. When a young man arrived,  
He took off his cap:  
"God bless you, young lady,  
Please help me!"  
3. "God bless you, young lady,  
Please help me,  
When will this wheat  
Become a loaf of bread?"  
4. "On Sunday, young man,  
On Sunday,  
Because I have  
Hope for you."  
5. "And on Saturday, young man,  
On Saturday,  
Because I have  
Desire for you."

Filename: Kmeln_287v_Oi u lisi, oi u lisi
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, oh, in the forest,  
On the maple,  
There hung a cradle  
On it.  
2. – Oh, rock me, boys,  
High up,  
I will see my beloved  
Far away,  
3. Where my dear, dark-browed one  
Is walking,  
Shining with golden rings!  
4. Shining with golden rings,  
He is walking  
With another girl.  
5. He is walking  
With another girl,  
But he forgets  
About me, the young one!  

№ 8  
1. Oh, in the forest, oh, in the forest,  
On the oak,  
There hung a cradle  
On it.  
2. And in that cradle  
Rocked,  
Young Ivanko  
Was swaying:  
3. – Oh, rock me, boys,  
High up,  
Oh, let me see  
Far away,  
4. Oh, where my girl is walking,  
Waving a silk scarf.  
5. Waving a silk scarf,  
She is waiting for me,  
The young one!

1. And the sun is already setting,  
We want to go home.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.  

2. And it’s already time,  
The moon has risen over the yard.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.  

3. And the sun is already on the wheat,  
Let us go, sir, young women.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.  

4. And it’s already time,  
The moon and the star have risen.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.  

5. And the sun is already behind the ponds,  
Let us go, sir, with the lads.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.  

6. And the sun is already behind the hill,  
I will go home now.  
Our lord, ours,  
It’s time for us to go home.

1. Oh, our lord,
It's already time to go home,
The time has come,
The moon has risen and the plowing is done.
2. Oh, our lord,
It's already time to go home,
The sun is already over the willows,
Let us leave with the bags.
3. Oh, our lord,
It's already time to go home.
4. Our lord has
A speckled cow,
May a grievous illness
Drive it away.

Unhurriedly, in a free rhythm  
And at our master’s  
A gilded gate  
Also a golden porch,  
Three hundred reapers have gathered.

And our lord has  
A golden gate,  
Golden doorposts,  
The lord will give us some spirits.

1. Our lady is at home,  
She is preparing dinner for us.  
The dinner is unpleasant to us,  
The field has tired us.  

2. Our lady is not splendid,  
She went out the gate,  
Rang the keys,  
To do some fieldwork.  

3. Our young master,  
Under him is a black horse,  
|: Golden hooves,  
The beaten road beneath him. :|

Oh, my reapers, my reapers,  
Finish harvesting the wheat.

1. We have pressed early,
Cut us a ram,
A horned ram,
We belong to a rich master.
2. Our master is at home,
He is preparing dinner for us,
The dinner is not dear to us,
The field has tired us.
3. Hey you, master, pay attention,
Sell the gray mare
Along with the hen and chicks,
Prepare a barrel of moonshine.

1. They squeezed, they tied up,  
And stacked them into piles.  
2. Come out, miss, come out,  
But do us no harm, –  
3. Put your hands in your pockets,  
Take out handfuls of money.  
4. And our proud lady  
Went out the gate in her slippers.  
5. The master said early on  
That they would slaughter a ram,  
6. A horned ram  
At the wealthy master’s place.  
7. The well is full, full,  
Like wool on a ram.

1. Our lush beard,  
Our wheat-colored beard!  
Today we will tie the beard  
On this fertile field,  
So that next year this field  
Yields an even richer harvest.  

2. Oh, little white quail,  
Where will you sit then?  
Because everything has been gathered to the last grain,  
There is no more wheat in the field,  
We have already harvested the last patch,  
We have tied the last sheaf.  

3. Oh, little quail,  
Where will you hide then?  
Because there is no rye or wheat,  
Because this harvest is now finished.  
We have already harvested the last patch,  
We have tied the last sheaf.

1. Our beard is lush,  
Our beard is wheat-like!  
Today we will tie the beard  
On this fertile field,  
So that next year this field  
Yields an even richer harvest.  

2. Oh, little white quail,  
Where will you sit?  
For all the grain has been gathered,  
There is no more wheat in the field,  
We have already reaped the last field,  
We have tied the last sheaf.  

3. Oh, little quail,  
Where will you hide?  
For there is no rye or wheat,  
For this harvesting season is over.  
We have already reaped the last field,  
We have tied the last sheaf.

1. Little quail,  
Where will you hide?  
2. We have reaped and tied,  
Stacked in sheaves.  
3. Open the gate for us –  
The wreath is rolling from the field.  
4. From the field to the barn,  
From the barn to the granary.  
5. From the granary to the field  
In a happy little hour.

1. Little white quail,  
Where will you be sitting?  
2. In the rye, or in the wheat,  
Or in the green grass?

1. Little white plover,  
Where will you sit?  
2. In rye, or in wheat,  
Or in the green grass?  
3. Here is our young master,  
Beside him a black horse,  
4. With golden hooves,  
The road beaten with thorn bushes,  
5. Sprinkled with chervil,  
Decorated with viburnum.  
6. He rides there,  
Driving reapers along:  
7. – Quickly, reapers, quickly,  
There will be vodka and beer.

1. The end of the ribbon, the end,  
The lady has woven a wreath.  
2. Our lady, elegant,  
Went out through the little gate,  
3. Rung the keys,  
Prayed to God.  
4. Prayed to God,  
That she has already set out into the field.  
5. The Jewish woman told us,  
That the master has horilka (Ukrainian vodka)  
6. In the room on the shelf,  
In a crystal flask.  
7. I am at our master's  
Golden gate,  
8. I am the silver cover,  
On it, three hundred grains of wheat.  
9. In a circle, little wreath, in a circle,  
I gathered you from the field.  
10. I from the field to the house  
In a happy moment.  
11. At our master's place,  
They slaughtered a ram,  
12. A horned ram,  
Because the master is wealthy.

The end, nivontsi, the end,  
We will weave a wreath,  
We will put it on the girl,  
We will drink some horilka.*  
* We leave the discrepancies in the subtext and the song’s lyrics. (L. Ye.)

1. Moon and mountain*,  
Light our way,  
2. So that we do not get lost here,  
Nor lose the wreath.  
3. For if we get lost on the road,  
We will lose the wreath.  
4. That will be a reprimand for us,  
From the master-landlord.  

№ 3  
1. The wreath rolls from the field  
To the master's gate,  
2. From the gate to the barn,  
From the barn to the storage room.  

№ 4  
1. Reap, reapers, swiftly,  
There will be vodka and beer.  
2. Oh, beer is no wonder to us,  
Nor is vodka dear.  
3. Nor is vodka dear,  
The field has tired us out.  
4. The field has tired us out,  
Broken our sickles.  
5. Golden sickles,  
Young reapers.  

* Probably "star". (L. Ye.)

1. Little birth-month,  
Lay a path for us.  
2. Open the gate, master*,  
The wreath is rolling from the field.  
3. A wreath on a stake,  
For the young men to dance.  
4. A wreath on a plate,  
For the young men to drink vodka.  
* In the manuscript, the recorder crossed out the word "master" and wrote "lord" instead. (L. Ye.)

1. Behind our settlement  
Rye grows with lamb's quarters.  
2. There Hanusya was reaping rye,  
Holding a sickle in her right hand.  
3. Holding a sickle in her right hand,  
And bundling with her left.  
4. And bundling with her left,  
While thinking of her beloved.

We were reaping wheat,  
Tying it into sheaves,  
|: Think, guess,  
Let us have dinner. :|

1. It is good to harvest rye  
in the priest's meadow,  
2. And to water the horse  
at the priest's well.  
3. "Why, horse, do you not drink water,  
why are you so sad?"  
4. "Why, daughter Stefanyna,  
do you not stay at home at night?"  
5. "Oh, how can I, my mother,  
stay home at night?" –  
6. "Autumn night will come, –  
there is no one to talk to."

1. Vinchik, vinchik, my vinchik,  
Vinchik, vinchik, my golden one,  
I wove you, vinchik,  
The best girl,  
A leading worker of the brigade,  
Famous for her labor,  
From good wheat,  
From the collective farm's field,  
My vinchik,  
My golden one.  

2. Vinchik, vinchik, my vinchik,  
Vinchik, vinchik, my golden one,  
What is in you, vinchik,  
Is a mature grain,  
Filled with strength,  
Washed with dew,  
From good wheat,  
From the collective farm's field,  
My vinchik,  
My golden one.  

3. Vinchik, vinchik, my vinchik,  
Vinchik, vinchik, my golden one,  
Grow for us, little wheat  
And all the harvest,  
In the collective farm's field  
For the next summer,  
We will have a rich crop  
And for the next year,  
My vinchik,  
My golden one.

1. Oh, how beautiful  
Our wreath is,  
Worthy of a loaf  
Of bread  
And also a liqueur  
On honey  
In the cherry  
Orchard, in the orchard.  

2. You, head,  
Don’t doze off,  
Bathe our little wreath,  
Give us a glass  
Of wine,  
Because we have finished  
The harvest.  

3. But slaughter a  
Goose for us,  
And let there be  
Music,  
So that we can start  
Dancing,  
So that we are  
Merry.

1. The beds burned down,  
The nails burned down.  
What kind of scoundrel is the young man,  
What does he say to the girl?  
2. What kind of scoundrel is the young man –  
The worst wretch,  
He is not worthy of Zhvanetsky,  
A lousy Jew.

1. And I am its shrub,  
And I am its thicket*,  
2. And I am its raw stump,  
So that it smokes all day,  
3. And I am its chatterbox,  
So that it smokes to its liking.  
* Small brushwood.

Our master  
Had three daughters.  
Fith, fith, fith,  
Tjokh, tjokh, tjokh,  
Ay, yay, yay,  
Oh, kho-khok,  
Had three daughters.

Beneath the heavens  
Lay the path.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
Along that path  
The Mother of God was walking.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
The Mother of God was walking  
To her son.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
To her son,  
Son Vasyl.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
She met  
Three angels.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
She asked them  
About her son.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
And the first said,  
That I have not even heard.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
And the second said,  
That I have not seen.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
And the third said,  
That I was with him.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
That I was with him,  
Sitting at the table.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
Sitting at the table,  
Drinking mead-wine.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
Sitting at the table,  
Giving three pieces of advice.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
The first advice  
Holy Nativity.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
The second advice  
For Vasyl's Day.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!  
The third advice  
For Epiphany.  
Generous evening,  
Saint Vasyl!

Oh, on the leaf,  
On the paper  
Jesus Christ sat  
For dinner.  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!  
The Mother of God flies in:  
Sit down, Son,  
To dine.  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!  
Take the golden keys,  
Let the souls  
Of hell go free.  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!  
Only do not release  
Those who on Friday  
Sang songs.  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!  
And on Friday  
They sang songs,  
And on Saturday  
Ate early,  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!  
And on Sunday  
Did not wash,  
Until sunrise  
Was still preparing.  
Generous evening,  
Holy evening!

Oh, give us, God,  
Oh, to the water, to the water,  
To the water, to the water.  
Oh, give us, God,  
Oh, there some saints  
Were sanctifying the water.  
Oh, give us, God,  
They sanctified the water,  
They dipped the cross.  
Oh, give us, God,

And the gates ask to be opened. The gates say: – Please come in.

Game instructions: Two boys stand a few steps away from the group, hold hands, and imagine they are the "gates." The group (girls and boys), holding hands, stands facing the gates and begins to sing "Lord...". The gates ask questions, and the group answers them. At the end of the song, the "gates" say: – Please come in. The group passes through the gates (under their arms). The very last person is stopped by the gates and asked: – Whom do you love? He answers and then goes to stand at the front of the group. The singing game begins again. When the first participant ends up last, the "gates," after asking him: – Whom do you love? – run away, and the group chases them and, upon catching them, also asks: – Whom do you love?

Until Saturday,

Is already passing,

1. Oh, chip, chip,

1. I cannot finish a crooked dance.

Its end must be found.  

2. Across the beams,

3. So the springs  
Do not flood,  
So the caretaker  
Is not awakened.  

4. Mother calls  
To the room  
To prepare the bed  
For the unloved one.  

5. And I spread it  
Sorrowfully, unhappily,  
A small stone for a pillow,  
And a pebble for the side.  

6. Mother calls  
To the room  
To prepare the bed  
For the beloved.  

7. And I spread it  
Joyfully, cheerfully,

8. Oh little shoe,

I will sow you  
In the little garden.  

9. I will cherish you,  
Water you seven times a day,

I will weed you,

11. The little grass answered:  
"Water me,  
My girl!"  

12. "Oh, I would gladly  
Water you,  
But the caretaker stands  
By the gate  

13. With a whip—  
A wire one,  
With a red  
Silk whip."

Filename: Vinnytsia_247_Cherez richku ta y na kladochtsi
translation:

4. My mother gave me little stockings,

10. The little flute is playing –  
Calling to the dance.

9. Black fruits –  
10. Slobodyan bachelors.

1. The goslings are out in the pasture.  
Cut, chop wood, goslings,  
Plant green rue, goslings.  
2. Oh, a young man was passing by.  
3. He was choosing a girl.  
4. He led her by the kerchief,  
5. Took her to the garden.  
6. Led her to eat berries.  
7. "Eat, girl, the berries,  
8. Forget about other girls."  
9. "And you, boy, eat sorrel,  
10. Admit that you are mine."  
11. Oh, a swan was flying by.  
12. He took a swan for himself.  
13. Gave her titmice to eat.  
14. Gave her water to drink  
15. From a deep well.

1. Oh, I will go to the garden,  
I will dig up some cherry trees.  
2. I will dig up some cherry trees  
And plant them in a row.  
3. And those cherry trees  
Bore berries.  
4. The Ivashkiv lads  
Walk around like gentlemen.  
5. And that Vasyl plays,  
Thinking of getting married.  
6. He left his pipe in the oats,  
And went himself to Odessa.  
7. Went himself to Odessa,  
Bought a ribbon and a comb.  
8. Bought a ribbon and a comb  
For Marusya’s wedding.  
9. Bought a ribbon and a ring  
For Marusya (and) her hand.  
10. Where Vasylko grazed the horse,  
There the grass grows up to the waist.  
11. Where Marusya stood,  
There the grass flourished.  
12. – Oh, Vasyl, dear heart,  
Take me on the horse.  
13. – Oh, Marusya, little bird,  
It will be hard for the horse.  
14. – May your horse die  
If it doesn’t carry us both.

1. Oh, on the hill, a little church,  
   Hey, little church.  
   Chop, cut the wood,  
   Around the garden is green,  
   Little church.  
2. In that little church, the young men,  
3. All talk about girls.  
4. Somewhere appeared that Ivan,  
5. Took Marusya by the scarf,  
6. Led her into the garden,  
7. Picked her some berries:  
8. "Eat, Marusya, the berries  
9. And forget about the girls."

1. In the cherry orchard  
I will dig a garden bed.  
2. I will sow cherry seeds,  
So that berries will grow.  
3. And the young lads from Loziany  
Pick berries from the cherries.  
4. They wear Yarmak hats,  
And swita coats.  
5. And belts as sashes,  
And boots called shkarbuntsi.

1. In the cherry orchard  
Marusya was sewing a shirt.  
2. Ivan came, stole the threads  
And swears that he did not take them.  
3. Yes, he swears, he curses himself,  
Grabs his head:  
4. – If I stole your threads,  
May I fall off this horse.  
5. May I fall off this horse  
And may I not spend the night with you.  
6. – You do not sow, you do not plow,  
So you will not take me either.  
7. – I do not sow, I do not plow,  
But if I get a chance, I will take you.  
8. In the cherry orchard  
Marusya was sewing a shirt.

Filename: Vinnytsia_269_Oi vid toho ta y Semena
translation:
1. Oh, because of that Semyon  
A ditch was dug.  
2. Ksenia ran there  
Still barefoot in her shirt.  
3. When she ran under the window,  
She tapped with her foot, tap-tap.  
4. – Come out, come out, Semyon,  
Because I am already here, here.  
5. But Semyon did not come out,  
His mother came out instead.  
6. – When you become a daughter-in-law,  
We will invite you into the house.

1. Oh, rise, moon,  
And shine on our street.  
2. And on our street,  
All the boys are fine fellows.  
3. And there is no one better  
Than Ivan, the young one.  
4. He wore out seven pairs of boots,  
Visiting his mother-in-law often.  
5. And the mother-in-law praised him,  
Saying that Tetyana has fallen in love.

1. A pot was rolling  
Across the road,  
Whiz, whiz, whiz,  
Thud, thud, thud,  
Oh-ho-ho, oh, oh, oh,  
Across the road.  
2. Crooked Mykhailo  
On his right leg.  
3. – Give me sticks  
To lean on,  
4. I’m going to Liuba  
To propose.  
5. But that Liuba  
Has fierce dogs.  
6. The mother came out,  
And defended him.  
7. Liuba came out,  
And spoke:  
8. – The dogs went  
To sleep in the straw,  
9. And you, Mykhailo,  
Go home.

1. The pot was rolling  
Across the road,  
Whiz, whiz, whiz,  
Thok, thok, thok,  
Oh-ho-ho, oh, oh, oh,  
Across the road.  
2. Crooked Mykhailo  
On his right foot.  
3. – Give me a stick  
To lean on,  
4. I’m going to Liuba  
To propose.  
5. But Liuba’s place  
Has fierce dogs.  
6. The mother came out,  
Defended.  
7. Liuba came out,  
Spoke:  
8. – Let the dogs  
Sleep in the straw,  
9. And you, Mykhailo,  
Go home.

1. The pot was rolling  
Across the path,  
For some reason Vasylko  
Is limping on his leg.  
2. Give me a stick  
To lean on,  
And also to visit  
Halyna.  
3. At this Halyna's place  
There are fierce dogs,  
No one will go out  
To defend.  
4. The mother came out,  
Spoke,  
Halyna came out,  
Defended:  
5. – Go, dogs,  
Sleep in the straw,  
And you, Vasylko,  
Go home.

Filename: Vinnytsia_273_Oi za dubi, divchata, za duby
translation:
1. Oh, beyond the oaks, girls, beyond the oaks,  
Beyond the oaks, the gray-blue ones.  
2. Beyond the oaks, the gray-blue ones,  
They have caused me to lose my head.  
3. They have caused me to lose my head,  
They did not let me sleep.  
4. They did not let me sleep,  
They have led me astray from my beloved.

1. And it is already spring,  
Water drips from the eaves,  
The young Cossack  
Smells of a journey.  
2. The little Cossack traveled  
Into the open field,  
A young girl followed him:  
– Return, falcon!  
3. – I will not return, I will wander! –  
You are proud of me!  
Your pride  
Will be before you!

1. Miracle or not a miracle, –  
The girls are going to war.  
2. Miracle or not a miracle, –  
The young wives are going to war.  
3. Miracle or not a miracle,  
And the young men are in pursuit.

1. Over the dam  
The water flows,  
And there Nina  
Is walking drunk.  
2. And Ivanko  
Got upset,  
[Gave a chop]  
To the vodka.  
3. And Maria  
A coin,  
Because Maria  
Is not like that.

1. Oh, I will light the fire  
And cook some porridge.  
2. – What is in that porridge?  
– A lump of butter.  
3. – Who will eat it?  
– The girls, the girls.  
4. Oh, I will light the fire  
And cook some porridge.  
5. – What is in that porridge?  
– A frog’s leg.  
6. – Who will eat it?  
– The lads, the lads.

1. Oh, up the hill  
The Levantar walks.  
Oh, why is he  
Walking up the hill?  

2. He is searching, asking  
For his wife,  
His young wife.  

3. – Oh, where did  
The black horse go?  
Where did  
The little black one go?  

4. – Oh, I drank away  
With a priest’s servant,  
With a young priest’s servant.  

5. With sweet  
Honey,  
And with green  
Wine.  

6. And I will  
Beat my wife,  
So she doesn't go  
To drink with the priest’s servant.  

7. To the tavern  
For some vodka,  
With the priest’s servant  
For a little talk.

1. Oh Leventar  
He walks through the mountain,  
Oh Leventar  
He wades through the greenery.  
2. He is looking  
For his beloved,  
He is looking  
For a young maiden!  
3. – Where have the oxen and cows gone?  
Where have the black horses gone?  
4. – Oh, she squandered them  
With the young priest,  
Oh, she squandered them  
With the young lad!  
5. Oh, she squandered them  
With the young lad  
On sweet  
Honey.

1. Olevenko  
Walks along the mountain,  
Searching, asking  
His own wife:  
2. – My young  
Wife,  
Where did you put  
The black horse?  
3. – I drank it up  
For a little honey,  
For a little honey,  
So sweet!  
4. For a little honey,  
So sweet,  
With a young priest!

1. And Leventur  
Walks on the water,  
And Leventur  
On the green.  
2. He searches, asks  
His wives,  
He searches, asks  
The young one:  
3. – Where did you lose  
The black horse,  
Where did you lose  
The little black one?  
4. – Oh, I drank away  
The black horse,  
With the young priest.

1. In the garden  
Three little herbs:  
– Water me,  
My girl.  
2. – Oh, I am glad  
To water,  
The unloved one stands  
By the gate.  
3. The unloved one stands  
By the gate,  
Holding the horse  
By the reins.  
4. And the reins  
Are silk,  
And the stirrups  
Are golden.  
5. My mother calls me  
From the garden to the house  
To prepare a bed  
For the unloved one.  
6. But I am spreading the bed,  
Not making it properly:  
Under the head –  
Stones,  
And on the sides –  
Stubs.  
7. And under the feet –  
Straw mats,  
Oh, so that  
Sleep is hard.  
8. Oh, so that  
Sleep is hard,  
And to talk  
With the unloved one.

1. Early, early  
Mown down,  
The grass and herbs  
Are cut.  

2. The grass and herbs  
Are cut  
And even gathered  
Late in the stack.  

3. Three little herbs  
Spoke up:  
– Wash me,  
My maiden.  

4. I would gladly  
Wash you,  
But my unloved one  
Stands by the gate.  

5. My mother calls me  
From the city home  
To prepare the bed  
For the unloved one.  

6. And I make for him  
A sad bedding,  
So that he would  
Sleep well.  

7. Stones under his head,  
Flints under his sides.  

8. And straw under his feet,  
So that he would not  
Get up from here.  

9. – Lie down, lie down,  
So that you do not rise,  
So that a better one  
Would come to me.

1. Oh, an arrow flew  
And fell near the village,  
Oh-ho-ho!  
And fell near the village.  
2. Whom did it kill?  
The widow's son.  
3. For the widow's son  
The whole family is crying.  
4. Three letters flew to him.  
5. One flew,  
And landed on his head.  
6. The second flew,  
And landed near his heart.  
7. The third flew,  
And landed on his little legs.  
8. And the one on the head –  
That is his dear grandmother.  
9. The one near the heart –  
That is his dear sister.  
10. The one on the little legs –  
That is his beloved.  
11. Where the grandmother cries,  
A little river flows.  
12. Where the sister cries,  
A well has formed.  
13. Where the beloved cries,  
The earth is dry.

1. You, green curly one,  
2. Do not lie close to me,  
3. Because my dear scolds me,  
4. So that I do not go outside,  
5. Do not lead the girls to dances,  
6. Do not tell the boys the truth.  

To the same melody:  
1. The birch boasted  
2. Between two oaks  
3. With all its branches:  
4. – My branches go down to the roots,  
5. My bark is as white as pain.  
6. Marusya boasted  
7. Between two young men  
8. With all her braids:  
9. – My braid reaches my waist,  
10. My eyebrows are like cords,  
11. My eyes are like blackthorns,  
12. My face is like an apple.

1. Oh, the poplar boasted  
In the meadow.  
Oh, God grant!*  

2. Oh, that I am tall,  
Slim, and high,  

3. Oh, that my root  
Is very deep,  

4. Oh, that I am a leaf,  
A broad leaf.  

5. Oh, the fierce wind spoke up:  

6. – Oh, do not boast,  
My poplar,  

7. Oh, for when I blow  
As the wind,  

8. I will tear from you  
Your broad leaf,  

9. Oh, your tall top  
I will break altogether,  

10. And your thin root  
I will pull from the ground.  

11. Oh, our maiden boasted,  

12. Oh, that I am tall  
By my stature,  

13. I have a little face  
Like an apple,  

14. I have eyebrows  
Like a string,  

15. I have eyes  
Like blackthorn,  

16. I have a braid  
Down to my waist.  

17. Oh, a courteous young man spoke up:  

18. – Oh, do not boast,  
Beautiful maiden,  

19. Oh, when I take you  
As my own,  

20. I will tear off  
Your long braid.  

21. Your black eyebrows  
Will fade,  

22. Your hazel eyes,  
You will cry out.  

23. From these words,  
Be healthy,  

24. Having waited for these holidays,  
Celebrate with health.  

25. Oh, celebrate, celebrate  
And do not be sad,  

26. The carnivals will come,  
And you will marry.  

27. And I have fragrant herbs  
In the town,  

28. You will invite us  
To the wedding.  
Good evening!

1. The poplar was boasting,  
The poplar was boasting,  
Boasting with a gentle whisper.  
2. That I am tall upwards,  
3. That I am deep in the root,

1. Little Hanochka  
And two little brothers  
|: She sent them  
To plow the field. :|  
2. She sent them  
To burn the field,  
She herself carried  
Lunch to eat.  
3. Hanochka was carrying  
Through dark meadows,  
She met her  
Noblemen in the forest,  
4. They gave Hanochka  
A horse to hold,  
They themselves sat down  
To eat lunch.  
5. Hanochka saw  
This was no joke,  
So she let the horse go  
Into the open field,  
She herself galloped into the blue sea.  
6. As she galloped,  
She spoke,  
When she jumped in,  
She spoke these words:  
7. – Do not cut down, people,  
The poplars in the field,  
Because in that poplar  
Is Hanochka’s fate.

1. Oh, on the high mountain, –  
My handsome, faithful one,  
Oh, on the high mountain!  
2. There stands a tall apple tree.  
3. Under that apple tree, a lady walks.  
4. The lady walks and says:  
5. – Whoever shoots through this apple tree,  
6. I will give my daughter to him.  
7. The gentlemen arrived, along with the hussars.  
8. But whoever shot, missed.  
9. Suddenly, a marksman appeared,  
10. He shot through the apple tree.  
11. On Saturday they arranged the match,  
12. On Sunday they got married.  
13. On Monday the son-in-law left,  
14. On Tuesday the beloved fell ill,  
15. On Wednesday the beloved died.  
16. On Thursday they dressed her up,  
17. On Friday they rang the bells,  
18. On Saturday they buried her.  
19. On Sunday the son-in-law came back,  
20. His mother-in-law came out to him:  
21. – Oh, my destined son-in-law,  
22. Destined and parted,  
23. Your beloved has already died.  
24. And the son-in-law grieved,  
25. He leaned over his horse:  
26. – Oh, my black horse,  
27. I am lost, young as I am.  
28. Oh, my white-maned horse,  
29. I am lost, dark-browed as I am.

1. By the mill, the viburnum,  
2. There a girl was walking,  
3. Carrying a knife in her hands,  
4. Digging up a love potion,  
5. Asking her mother:  
6. – Whom shall I enchant?  
7. – Enchant, daughter, [a suitor,]  
8. Who has not come to you,  
9. Do not break white hands,  
10. Do not tear off rings,  
11. Do not give to other girls.

1. A viburnum bush near the mill.  
2. A girl was walking there,  
3. Digging herbs with a knife  
4. And also asking her mother:  
5. – Oh mother, should I dig?  
6. – Dig, daughter, the roots  
7. And wash them in the river.  
8. From under the white stones,  
9. So that the young men would love you.

1. By the mill, the viburnum,  
There walked a girl.  
2. She was digging herbs with a knife,  
And asked her mother:  
3. – My dear mother,  
What herbs should I dig?  
4. – Dig, daughter, roots,  
So that the young men would love you.  
5. So that they would walk with you,  
Like a little fish with water.  
6. Like a little fish with a perch,  
So you, daughter, with a young man.

1. Through our village  
They are carrying a maple tree,  
A tree, a maple tree,  
Small and green.  

2. And from that tree  
They are building a bell tower.  

3. And in that bell tower  
There are three ringing bells,  

4. And in our Medvidka  
There are three handsome young men.  

5. The first ringing bell –  
That is Ivan the handsome.  

6. The second ringing bell –  
That is Fedir the handsome.  

7. The third ringing bell –  
That is Vasyl the handsome.  

8. Through our village  
They are carrying a maple tree.  

9. And from that tree  
They are building a small church.  

10. And in that small church  
There are three bright candles,  

11. And in our Medvidka  
There are three beautiful girls.  

12. The first bright candle –  
That is Zinka the beautiful.  

13. The second bright candle –  
That is Nastia the beautiful.  

14. The third bright candle –  
That is Hustia the beautiful.

1. Oh, behind our garden  
There are three months in a row.  
A tree, a maple tree,  
Small and green.  

2. The first month is bright –  
That is handsome Ivan.  

3. The second month is bright –  
That is fine Fedor.  

4. The third month is bright –  
That is noble Vasyl.  

5. Oh, behind our garden  
There are three bright stars.  

6. The first star is bright –  
That is beautiful Zinka.  

7. The second star is bright –  
That is lovely Nastya.  

8. The third star is bright –  
That is graceful Hustya.  

9. Ivan with his (partner)  
Went to harvest the field.  

10. Fedor with his (partner)  
Went to thresh.  

11. Vasyl with his (partner)

1. On Ivan Kupala Day...
Beyond our town
Are three bright months.
On Ivan Kupala Day.
2. The first month is bright
And that is Ivan the Fair.
3. The second month is bright
And that is Peter the Fair.

1. On Ivan Kupala Day...
Beyond our town
Three bright months.
On Ivan Kupala Day.

They are carrying a maple tree.  
Stop, little viburnum, stop,  
Not far away you can hear a bell.  

2. And from that little tree  
We will make a bell tower.

There are three ringing bells.

1. Oh beyond the garden, the garden  
Three bright months,  
Layer by layer, bar by bar, oh ya-ya,  
Three bright months.  
2. Three bright months,  
Three handsome young men.  
3. The first bright month –  
That is Ivan the handsome.  
4. The second bright month –  
That is Vasyl the handsome.  
5. The third bright month –  
That is Mykola the handsome.  
6. Oh beyond the garden, the garden  
Three bright little stars.  
7. Three bright little stars,  
Three beautiful girls.  
8. The first bright star –  
That is Marusya the beautiful.  
9. The second bright star –  
That is Hanusya the beautiful.  
10. The third bright star –  
That is Katrusya the beautiful.  
11. Oh, there goes Ivan  
To the field to mow.  
12. And he takes Marusya  
To carry his lunch.  
13. Oh, there goes Vasyl  
To the field to plow.  
14. And he takes Hanusya  
To drive the oxen.  
15. Oh, there goes Mykola  
To serve in the army.  
16. And he takes Katrusya  
To carry the rifle.

1. I will lay a path  
For the ant  
Through the willow.  
2. Oh, it is time for us, girls,  
To go home, home.  
3. And you, girl,  
Girl Maryna,  
Stay.  
4. With the young Cossack  
Court, court.  
5. The priest will give you a wreath,  
A wreath, a wreath.  
6. So that the wreath  
May bloom healthily.  
7. Good musicians  
Played, played.  
8. So that boys and girls  
Would dance, dance.

1. I will lay a bridge  
Over the anthill  
Willow, willow.  
It's time for you, girls,  
To go home, to go home.  
2. And you, Marika,  
Stay, stay,  
Marry young Ivan,  
Marry, marry.

1. Over the little Danube  
Willow footbridge,  
Willow.  
2. It is time for you, girls,  
It is time for you, young ones,  
To go home.  
3. But you, Halyunya,  
But you, young one,  
Stay.  
4. Petrunyo will come,  
Will bring a wreath, –  
Get ready.

1. I will lay a little bridge  
Willow, willow,  
Girls, disperse,  
Go home, go home.  

2. Only you, Maria,  
Stay, stay,  
When that Sashko comes,  
Make yourself ready, make yourself ready.  

3. Oh, don’t be, Sashko,  
Such a gentleman, such a gentleman,  
Take that Maria  
Under your coat, under your coat.  

4. – I don’t have a coat,  
Don’t have, don’t have,  
Under a gray jacket  
I will hide, I will hide.

1. Don’t stand still, little willow, spread out,  
It’s time for you, Mykola, to get married.  
2. – Oh, not time, not time, not yet,  
Because my Hanusya is still young.  
3. Let that little willow flourish,  
Let my Hanusya have fun.  
4. Let her enjoy for five years,  
Let her grow at least a span.  
5. Let her have fun until spring,  
So that her braids can grow!  
6. So that in autumn they can be unbraided,  
And she can be led to the village with Mykola.

1. Do not stand still, little willow, unfold,  
It is time for you, Mykola, to marry.  
2. – Oh, it’s not the time, not the time, not the moment,  
For my Hanusia is still young.  
3. Let that little willow flourish,  
Let my Hanusia have fun.  
4. Let her have fun for five years,  
Let her grow at least a span.  
5. Let her have fun until spring,  
So that her braids grow!  
6. So that in autumn they will be unbraided,  
And she will be led to the village with Mykola.

1. – It is time for you, willow, to bloom,  
It is time for you, Ivan, to marry.  
2. – Oh, it's not time, not the season,  
Because Hannusya is still young.  
3. Because Hannusya is still young,  
Let her enjoy her youth in good health.  
4. – Lay down the willow bridge, the willow,  
It is time for you, girls, to go home.  
5. But you, Hannusya, stay,  
Marry young Ivan.

1. Oh, on the hill under the buckwheat  
A horse grazes with a bridle.  
2. And I will go (go, horse), harness you  
And we will ride to the Danube.  
3. To the Danube to drink water,  
To Vasyl to love the girl.  
4. And Vasyl has a violin and a pipe,  
Come, Maria, like a dove.  
5. Come, come and sprinkle,  
And with that Terent kisses on the lips.  
Good night!  

To the same melody:  
1. Oh, on the river, on the Jordan  
The devoted virgin bore Christ.  
2. She wrapped Him on the hearth,  
She rocked Him on the fir tree.  
3. Angels flew in,  
Took Jesus Christ on their wings.

1. Oh, the little frog is jumping through the meadow, through the meadow,  
Boys are chasing her in a swarm, in a swarm:  
2. – Wait, little frog, I will catch you,  
We will go all the way to the Danube.  
3. I go to the Danube to drink water,  
And to Yanev to court the girls.  
4. In Yanev, the girls are wealthy,  
They give flaxen kerchiefs.

1. Oh, beyond the town, on the buckwheat field  
A horse grazes with a bridle.  
2. – Oh, come here, horse, I'll tie your tail  
And we will go to the Danube.  
3. And in that Danube, a little fish jumps,  
Marusya stands like a dove.  
4. People look at her,  
Because she dresses beautifully.  
5. There is nothing to be surprised about,  
Her mother did not neglect her.  
6. Ivan worked hard for her,  
He promised to ask for her hand.

1. Oh, in the town  
Birds are circling,  
Boys are fighting  
Over Hanusia.  
2. – Oh, don’t fight,  
There’s no reason,  
Though she is pretty,  
She’s lazy.  
3. She doesn’t hurry  
To work,  
Sees Mykola  
And just laughs.

1. – Oh, Mykola,  
Don't stand alone,  
Take your Hanusya  
Under your coat.  
2. – But I don’t have a coat,  
Under my gray jacket  
I will hide her.  
3. Black jacket,  
Like a jackdaw,  
You are mine, Hanusya,  
My beloved.

1. Oh, by the (Malyar)  
Tall fence,  
There hanged himself  
(the policeman’s) son.  
2. He hanged himself  
Yesterday morning  
Because he took Hanusya  
As a lover.  
3. Oh, let him hang,  
Do not raise him,  
Let him walk,  
Do not stop him.

1. Oh, beyond the town, linden trees, linden trees,  
So that our boys’ stomachs swell.  
2. Let them swell, let them know,  
Let them cut down the Kupala.

1. Oh, outside the town, linden trees, linden trees,  
Katya's belly is swollen.  
2. Oh, let it swell, let her know,  
Let her not accept Vasyl.

1. Our Kupaylo is on three branches,  
And our boys are gawkers.  
2. If they cared for us,  
They would have chopped down our Kupaylo.  
3. At the edge, Irina behind the ditch  
Held out a white hand to both:  
4. – Here, Ivan, take the left one,  
And you, Vasyl, take the right one.  
5. You, Ivan, take it or not,  
Go talk to another.  
6. And you, Vasyl, stay,  
Get married to young Irina.

1. Oh, our boys are careless,
They didn't cut down the bathing willow.
2. Oh, if they cared for us,
They would have cut down the bathing willow.
3. They said: – From the willow, from the willow.
But we will break off willow branches.
4. Oh, cut it down with berries,
So that we could go with the young men.

1. And our careless guys
Did not cut down the Kupaylo plants.
2. Oh, if they cared for us,
They would have cut down the Kupaylo.
3. They would have cut down the Kupaylo
And even buried it in the meadow.
4. Even buried it in the meadow,
And also adorned Marynka.

1. And our careless boys  
Did not cut down the kupaylytsia (willow wreath for Kupala Night).  
2. Oh, if they cared for us,  
They would have cut down the kupaylo for us.  
3. Oh, do not cut the willow,  
Because we will break the willow ourselves.  
4. But cut down the one with berries,  
We will sing with the girls.  
5. Our kupaylo is made of willow, of willow,  
Oh, Katya, come out, come out.  
6. And if you do not come out for Kupala Night,  
Then your soul and steam will come out of you.

Filename: Vinnytsia_324_A nash Ivanko z verbi, z verbi
translation:
1. And our Ivanko from the willow, from the willow,  
And you, Marusya, come, come.  
2. And if you don’t come on Ivan's day,  
Your soul and steam will leave you.  
3. ...  
Wait, Marusya, until autumn.  
4. And we vouch for you,  
We will wed you with Mykhailo.

1. Oh, our boys are shooters, shooters  
They hit a frog on a stone.  
2. Girls, go and watch  
How the boys will share.  
3. So the head, so the paw,  
And Ivan gets the whole frog.

1. The geese were flying side by side, side by side  
And they were sheltering each other with their wings.  
2. The girls are running to watch  
How Kolya will get married.  
3. How he will take the faithful girl  
That Liuba, the young lady.  

Variant:  
1. The geese were flying side by side, side by side  
The bells rung together, together.  
2. The girls were running to watch  
How Vanya will get married.  
3. How he will take the faithful girl  
That Tanya, the young lady.  
4. And that Ivan took the best one,  
Because he liked Tanya very much.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There a little swallow was bathing.  
2. There a little swallow was bathing,  
On the shore drying itself.  
3. On the shore drying itself,  
Little Marusya was sorrowful.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There a little swallow was bathing.  
2. There a little swallow was bathing,  
On the shore she was drying herself.  
3. On the shore she was drying herself,  
Marusynochka was grieving.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
The little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. By the riverbank she was drying herself,  
The girl Hannusya was sorrowful:  
3. – I haven’t even woven my towels yet,  
And Ivan has already taken a liking to me.  

To the same melody:  
1. Our little branch from the willow, from the willow,  
And you, Ivanko, come, come.  
2. Because if you don’t come on Kupala,  
Your soul and steam will disperse.  

Variant group  
To the same melody:  
1. Oh, in the garden there is white clay,  
Oh, why did mother beat Hannu?  
2. Oh, because mother beat Hannu,  
So that she wouldn’t love boys.  

To the same melody:  
1. Oh, in the garden the hay is mown, mown,  
The devil took the boys away, carried them off.  
2. Oh, in the garden the cornflower blooms,  
The devil took the boys and is rushing away with them.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There the little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. By the shore she was drying herself,  
The girl Hannusia was sorrowful:  
3. – I have not yet woven my towels,  
But Ivan already liked me.  

To the same melody:  
1. Our little branch from the willow, from the willow,  
And you, Ivanko, come, come.  
2. Because if you do not come on Kupala,  
Your soul and steam will leave you.  

Solo  

To the same melody:  
1. Oh, in the garden there is white clay,  
Oh, why did mother beat Hanna?  
2. Oh, mother beat Hanna  
So that she would not love boys.  

To the same melody:  
1. Oh, in the garden there is a mowed grass, mowed grass,  
The devil took the boys, carried them away, carried away.  
2. Oh, in the garden it’s crowing,  
The devil took the boys and is running.

1. Oh, on Petro’s and Ivan’s day,  
There the little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. There the little cuckoo was bathing,  
Drying off on the riverbank.  
3. Drying off on the riverbank,  
Maiden Hannusia was grieving,  
4. Because she had not yet acquired any towels,  
But she already liked Ivan.  
5. She had not yet embroidered any towels,  
But she already loved Ivan.  

To the same melody:  
1. In the garden under the bush,  
The boys covered themselves with a blanket.  
2. The girls are running to see  
How the boys will behave.  

*       *       *  

1. Our Kupaylo [a summer solstice celebration] is from the willow, from the willow,  
And you, Ivan, come, come.  
2. And if you do not come to Kupaylo,  
Your soul and vapor will leave you.

1. Oh, on Ivana Kupala,
There the little cuckoo was bathing.
2. There the little cuckoo was bathing,
On the shore it was drying itself.
3. On the shore it was drying itself,
Maiden Hanunya was grieving,
4. That she had not yet washed the towels,
And I was already meeting the headman.
5. I already hung the towels on the pole,
And the elders are sitting in the house.
6. I already hung the towels on the pole,
And Hanunya is already in a wreath.

1. Oh, in the garden a little bath,
A swallow was bathing there.
2. On the riverbank drying,
The girl Marusya was sorrowful:
3. She hasn’t yet woven the towels,
But already Ivanka has taken a liking.
4. She hasn’t yet hung the towels,
But already she loved Ivanka.
5. The towels are still on the loom,
But the matchmakers have long been in the house.
6. The pot was rocking, tossing,
The father was boasting about his daughter:
7. – Ivan, marry my daughter,
I will give you a speckled cow.
8. Oh speckled, speckled, speckled one,
Marry pretty Marusya.
9. Oh speckled, speckled, sharp-horned,
Marry Marusya with black eyebrows.

1. Oh, on Kupala, on Ivan's day  
Maria came out beautifully dressed.  
2. The boys are admiring her,  
Gathering over honey mead.  
3. And that Ivan gave the most,  
Because he liked Maria.  

To the same melody:  
1. Oh, Petry, Petry, little Petrova,  
The swallow did not get enough sleep.  
2. She dried herself on the ridge,  
What was Maria sad about?  
3. She was sad over her embroidered towels,  
Because Ivan will come with the elders.

1. The pot was rolling,  
Bouncing,  
(Malyar) was boasting  
About his daughter.  
2. – Oh, Mykola,  
My son,  
Propose to Hanusia,  
My daughter.  
3. Propose to Hanusia,  
My daughter,  
I will give you a speckled  
Little cow.  
4. I will give you a speckled  
Heifer too,  
Propose to my chubby-faced  
Hania.  
5. I will give you a speckled  
And strong-horned one,  
Propose to my Hania  
With black eyebrows.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
Maryika came out like a peacock.  
2. The young men are watching her,  
But are too shy to greet her.  
3. But Ivan was not shy,  
He took her hand and greeted her.  
4. – My Maryika, my dear,  
I like your face.  
5. Oh, not so much your face as yourself,  
As if written on paper.  
6. Like on paper, on a leaf,  
Black eyebrows on a string.

1. On Kupala, on Ivan's day  
Maria came out, beautifully dressed.  
2. The boys glanced at her,  
But were shy to greet her.  
3. But that Sasha was not shy,  
He took her hand and greeted her:  
4. – Oh you, Maria, my heart,  
I liked your face.  
5. Oh, not the face, but you yourself,  
Because the face is written on paper.  
6. The church clerks wrote at school  
A white face, black eyebrows.

1. On Kupala Night, on Ivan's Day,  
Maria came out, beautifully dressed.  
2. The boys were watching her,  
But were shy to speak.  
3. But that Sasha was not ashamed,  
He took her hand and greeted her:  
4. – Oh, Maria, my heart,  
I like your face.  
5. Oh, not so much your face as you yourself,  
Because the face is written on paper.  
6. The clerks wrote in school  
A white face, black eyebrows.

1. Oh, it is raining in Lila,  
And water is flowing to our Wall.  
2. By the mill it gathers,  
There our boys are assembling.  
3. There our boys are assembling,  
Pooling money for honey vodka.  
4. Oh, on Kupala, on Ivan's day,  
Halia went to the village dressed up.  
5. The boys are looking at her,  
But are shy to dance with her.  
6. But that Ivan was not shy,  
He offered his hand and greeted her:  
7. – Oh you, Halia, my heart,  
I like your face.  
8. It’s not the face, but you yourself,  
As if written on fine paper.

1. Oh, outside the town is saffron, saffron,  
Tolyunya stands like a gentleman, like a gentleman.  
2. The saffron presses on the cumin,  
Tolia embraces Zinunya.  
3. – Oh you, Zinunya, oh you, oh you,  
How can I come to you, come to you?  
4. – Through the garden, Tolyunya, through the garden,  
There will be vodka with honey, with honey.  
5. He also made a vow to the horse, to the horse,  
And Zinunya by his side, by his side.

Filename: Vinnytsia_338_Oi ty, Halyno, oi ty, oi ty
translation:
1. – Oh, Halyna, oh you, oh you,  
Where can I come to you, come to you?  
2. – Through the garden, Ivanko, through the garden, through the garden,  
There will be vodka with honey, with honey.  
3. And for Mykola, a blessing, a blessing,  
A pillow for your side, for your side.

1. And in the green meadow  
Natalka was grazing a duck.  
2. She lost it while grazing,  
Got lost while walking.  
3. She wandered into the field,  
Where Mykhalko was plowing.  
4. Oh, whether he is plowing or harrowing,  
He is following Natalka closely.  
5. – Oh you, Natalka, oh you,  
How can I come to you?  
6. – Over the mountain and valley,  
Through weeds and nettles.  
7. With a garden, Mykhalko, with a garden,  
There will be vodka with honey.  
8. Also a bridle for the horse,  
And a pillow under the side.

1. Oh, water flows in the grooves,  
There Tanya is weaving linen.  
2. And that [Ivanko] carries water  
And asks that Tanya for linen.  
3. – Oh, give me linen, linen,  
You will be my faithful wife.  
4. Oh, at least give me a piece of linen,  
I will buy a treat on Sunday.

1. Oh, there is a flute playing outside,  
But my mother won't let me go.  
2. – Oh, mother, let me go for a walk,  
If you want a son-in-law, mother.  
3. – Oh, daughter, do not delay,  
Just go beyond the gate and come back.  
4. – Oh, mother, what kind of walking is this,  
Just going beyond the gate and returning?  
5. Oh, mother, buy a little lock,  
Lock me in the pantry.  
6. If the boys want to love me,  
They will have to break the locks for a long time.

1. A sparrowhawk flew in from the street,  
Grabbed Marusya by the nape.  
2. That Ivan runs: – Hey, hey!  
Leave Marusya alone, it's very sad.  
3. Leave Marusya the worker,  
Take that other lazy one.  
5. She didn’t milk for me,  
Because she only took raw food for the day.

1. Oh, through the meadow, the meadow, through the puddle  
Hania was mowing with an axe.  
2. And she raked with a rake,  
Adorning Ivan for herself.  
3. Oh, she gave him an adornment, –  
Embroidered a shirt for him with silk.  
4. And you, girls, take this advice  
And embroider for your own boys.

1. Oh, through the forest, the forest, through the valley  
She walked with a scythe.  
2. And with a firebrand stacked up,  
She enticed Ivan for herself.  
3. Oh, give him a little gift  
Of a Chinese dress made of black silk.  
4. A Chinese dress made of black silk:  
– Come to me every morning.  
5. Come to me every morning,  
Bring vodka, my lady.  
6. Bring sweet vodka,  
Love that young Halya.

1. Oh through the forest, through the village,
Who is riding the gray horse?
2. It is Ivan riding drunk:
– Come out, Halia, your beloved.
3. He leaned over on his little horse:
– Come out, Halia, take a look.
4. He is tapping on his little horse:
– Come out, Halia, with a sincere heart.

Filename: Vinnytsia_346_[Khytki] buly ti vorota ta y upaly
translation:
1. The gate was shaky and it fell,  
A young Cossack's girl was stolen.  
2. The Cossack lies, not yet hearing, not hearing,  
Spends the night with a cat in a basket.  
4. Spends the night with a cat in a basket,  
Kisses the kittens in his lap:  
5. – Oh, my little kitty, little kitty,  
Where is your young maiden?

1. Oh, a poplar grew by the field,  
Oh, who is trimming you, poplar?  
2. Oh, who is trimming you, poplar?  
And who is embracing you, Yavdokha?  
3. Vasyl spoke up on his horse:  
– I am the one trimming that poplar.  
4. I am the one trimming that poplar,  
And I am the one embracing you, Yavdokha.  
5. Oh, I will trim the poplar low,  
And I will embrace you closely.  
6. Oh, I will trim the poplar even lower,  
Oh, I will embrace Yavdokha even closer.  
7. Oh, I will trim the poplar to the root,  
Oh, I will embrace Yavdokha to my heart.

1. On Kupala,  
On Ivan...  
2. Ivan was bathing  
And fell into the water.  
3. As he fell, he lay there,  
When suddenly a dear girl ran up.  
4. The girl ran up and cried:  
– Get up, get up, Cossack!

1. – The little star was shining, shining,  
With whom, Halyna, were you standing?  
2. – With you, Ivane, with you,  
Like a bright moon with a star.  
3. – How did you recognize me, Halya,  
That you called me a moon?  
4. – By you, Ivane, by you,  
The red hat on you.  
5. The red hat, it’s glowing bright,  
My heart aches for you, Ivane.

1. – In the field, a little furrow has withered,  
With whom were you standing, Tetyano?  
2. – Under the green willow,  
My heart, Andriy, with you.  
3. With you, Andriy, with you,  
Like a bright star.  
4. – How then, Tetyano, did you know  
That you called me a star?  
5. – By you, Andriyku, by you, –  
The gray hat on you,  
6. The silver ring on your hand,  
That Andriy bought at the fair.

1. – Oh, up, sun, up,  
Build, build a cellar, Ivasi.  
2. Build, build a cellar, Ivan,  
Cut, cut the little window from outside.  
3. So that nightingales fly to you,  
Entertain young Galya.  
4. – Why do I need nightingales, why do I,  
I have an old mother who will entertain me.  
5. I have an old mother who will entertain me,  
She will go to the neighbor and tell her:  
6. – My lazy daughter-in-law is sleeping,  
The livestock is lowing and standing.  
7. My daughter-in-law is sleeping in the cellar,  
The livestock is lowing in the barn.  
8. Get up, get up, daughter-in-law, enough sleeping,  
It’s time, it’s time to drive the livestock to the field.  
9. – I will drive away what I brought,  
I will leave behind what I found.

Filename: Vinnytsia_352_Stoiav, stoiav vynohrad ta y zatsviv
translation:
1. The grapevine stood and stood, then it blossomed,  
A yard full of suitors arrived.  
2. A yard full of suitors arrived,  
Recognize, recognize, Halya, which one is yours.  
3. – And I know that Ivan well,  
Among all the young men, I recognize him.  
4. He sits on his horse above all others,  
Plays, plays the flute better than anyone.  
5. Plays, plays the flute better than anyone,  
Hugs that Halya better than anyone.

Filename: Vinnytsia_353_Stoiav, stoiav vynohrad ta y zatsviv
translation:
1. The vineyard stood and blossomed,  
A yard full of cavaliers arrived.  
2. A yard full of cavaliers arrived,  
Recognize, recognize, Hannočko, which one is yours.  
3. – And I know my Ivan well,  
Among the other young men I recognize him.  
4. And Vin sits on a horse higher than everyone,  
Plays the flute better than anyone.  
5. – Oh, how, Hannočko, did you recognize  
That you called me a cavalier?  
6. – Because you have a hat like a jackdaw,  
And I am yours, Ivanko, your beloved.

1. Oh, Hanusya was walking through the field,  
Picking wheat from the darnel.  
2. Mykola came, took off his hat:  
– God bless you, Hanusya, help me!  
3. [God bless you, Hanusya, help me!]  
There will be a loaf made from this wheat.  
4. There will be seeds from this wheat,  
When is your wedding, Hanusya?  
5. – On Saturday, girls, on Saturday,  
Because I desire Mykola.  
6. On Sunday, girls, on Sunday,  
Because I have hope in Mykola.

1. Oh, a young girl was walking through the field,  
Picking wheat from the darnel.  
2. A Cossack was riding along the road, took off his hat:  
– God bless you, young girl, help me!  
3. God bless you, young girl, help me!  
Do not hire yourself to this wheat.  
4. Do not hire yourself to this wheat,  
There will be a wedding bread from this wheat.  
5. There will be seeds from this wheat,  
When is your wedding, young girl?  
6. – Oh girls, on Saturday, on Saturday,

Filename: Vinnytsia_364_(I)a letila utochka ta y sila
translation:
1. A duck was flying and then landed,  
Who did you choose, Maryko?  
2. For that one, girls, for that one,  
For that young Ivan.  
3. For that young Ivan,  
Because there is no one better than him.  
4. And rain will not fall without clouds,  
And Maryko will not be without a partner.  
5. The cloud will come – there will be rain,  
When that Ivanko arrives – there will be a partner.  
6. And that Ivan will come from the road  
And bring a pink wreath.  
7. So that Maryko would come,  
To invite us to the wedding.

1. In the little forest on the maple tree

2. Oh, in that cradle is a little dove,  
Kolyunya was rocking, hey, a fine young man.  
3. – Rock me, brothers, high up,  
So that I can see far away, hey, far off.  
4. Somewhere my mother-in-law is drinking honey brandy,  
Somewhere my Marusya, hey, is picking flowers.  
5. Oh, she picked a flower along with rue,  
Don’t sit on the dowry, hey, but quickly.  
6. Let that flower wither away,  
Let my Marusya, hey, wander.

1. Oh, in the little forest on the oak tree,

2. And in that cradle was a sparrow,  
That Ivan the young man was rocking.  

3. – Rock me high,  
Let me see far away.  

4. Somewhere my mother-in-law is drinking honey wine,  
Somewhere my Maria is picking wreaths.  

5. – How much for the wreath? – For a coin.  
– You are my Maria forever.  

To the same melody:

Filename: Vinnytsia_367_Oi v lisku, v lisku na dubku
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, in the forest on the oak tree,  
There hung a cradle on a cord.  
2. And in that cradle, a sparrow,  
The young fellow Tolya was rocking.  
3. Rock me, boys, high up,  
So that I can see far away.  
4. Somewhere my mother-in-law is harvesting rye,  
The young Valya is weaving wreaths.  
5. Let her weave wreaths, let her twist,  
Let the young girl have fun.  
6. Let her have fun until spring,  
So that her braids grow longer.  
7. So that her braids grow longer  
And so she can untie them in autumn.  
8. So she can untie them in autumn,  
And put on the royal wreath.  

To the same melody:

2. And Tolya came, stole the threads,

3. – Give back the threads, Tolya,  
Let me finish sewing the shirt.  
4. Not so much the shirt, as the collar,  
The handsome Tolya, young fellow.

Filename: Vinnytsia_368_Oi u lisi, yu lisochku na dubochku
translation:

2. And who rocked in that cradle?  
The young Ivan was rocking.  
3. – Rock me, rock me, my mother, high up,  
Let me see far away.  
4. And where is my Halia walking,  
Waving her embroidered sleeves.  
5. Waving her embroidered sleeves,  
Blinking with her dark eyes:  
6. – To me, Ivan, to me,  
I have a rosy little face too.  
7. A rosy little face, burning bright,  
My heart aches for you, my Ivan.

Filename: Vinnytsia_369_Oi u lisi, u lisochku na dubochku
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, in the little forest on the little oak,

2. And who was rocking in that little cradle?  
Young Ivan was rocking.  
3. Rock me, rock me, my mother, high up,  
Let me see far away.  
4. Somewhere my dear one is walking,  
Waving with embroidered sleeves.  
5. Waving with embroidered sleeves,  
Rocking in a black blouse.

Filename: Vinnytsia_370_Oi u lisi, u lisochku y na dubochku
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, in the grove and on the oak tree,  
There hung a cradle in the coolness.  
2. And who was rocking in that cradle?  
Young Ivan was rocking.  
3. – Rock me, rock me, my dear mother, gently,

4. Somewhere my mother-in-law is drinking mead and wine,  
Somewhere my girl is waiting for me. (She weaves a wreath.)  
5. And what are those wreaths made of? – Of gold?  
Oh, may the doves love her.  
6. And what are those wreaths made of? – Of nettle.  
Oh, may the black-eyed girls love her.

1. Our little Petrivonka is already passing,

3. Ivan went abroad somewhere

4. And I will get some for my Hanusia,  
I will wrap them with a silk ribbon.  
5. I will wrap them with a silk ribbon,

1. Petro passes by Ivan,

3. He gathered, gathered a skirt, like a rose,

4. Wrapped it with a silk ribbon

5. He gathered, gathered the skirt in three layers,  
Send the elders, Ivan.

7. – You are with me, Nastia, with me,

8. You will be my, young man’s wife,

9. You will bring me, the young man, dinner,

10. – I have not been to the market with you,

11. And I am not your wife, young man,  
And I am not a servant to your old mother.

1. All the hay has been mown into stacks,

What kind of ailment have you swollen up with?

Who will embrace you, poplar?

– Who embroidered it for him?

3. With fine needles,  
With white hands,  
Oh, so that he could be recognized  
Among the young men.

1. Oh, the wind blows on the hill,

Karpo goes to Kilyna.  

2. Oh, you, paper rye,  
You, Kilyna, with black eyebrows,  
Oh, you, green oak,  
Oh, you, young Karpo.

1. You, Fedosya, are beautiful and fair,

Shake, sir, the money pouch,

1. Our young lady went out

|: She even washed her feet,

|: She slaughtered a goose

Filename: Vinnytsia_389_Oi litala chorna halka po poliu
translation:
1. Oh, the black jackdaw was flying over the field

Enough wandering around the field.  
3. Enough wandering around the field,  
Picking wheat from among the darnel.  
4. Come out, come out, our master, to us  
And bring us some vodka and honey.  
5. Bring us, our master, also some beer,  
We will thank you for the harvest.

1. Oh moon, moon,  
Oh shine for us clearly,  
2. So that we do not get lost,  
Nor lose the wreath.

1. The wreath rolled from the field  
All the way to the lord’s gate,  
2. By the lord’s doorstep,  
Bring out the vodka, lord.  
3. Our lords are not proud,  
They came out to the gate.  
4. [They came out to the gate,]  
Brought us vodka.

1. And the lord is winnowing the wheat,  
And the lady is threshing,  
2. [And the lady is threshing,]  
Because the harvest is good.

1. Oh, our lady is splendid  
And she went against the gentleman.  
2. She jingled the keys,  
Already she went into the field.  
3. And our young gentleman,  
Under him a black horse.  
4. He rode out to the field  
At a fortunate hour.  
5. I am with our gentleman,  
Oh, I am with our gentleman,  
I am the golden gate,  
6. I am the golden doors,  
And our gentleman will give vodka.  
7. Oh, the gentleman will give vodka  
From a new barrel.  
8. We don't want from the old one,  
Because the old one is cobbled together.

1. There is a white-winged rooster,  
And our lord is dark-browed.  
2. He plays like a horse in the field,  
Driving his reapers.  
3. – Quickly, reapers, quickly,  
There will be moonshine and beer.  
4. The young reapers were mowing.  
They had golden sickles.  
5. Moon, little bulrush,  
Light our way.  
6. So we do not get lost,  
Do not lose the wreath,  
7. Because you will be punished  
By the noble lord.  
8. Our lady is not proud,  
She came out beyond the gate,  
9. Rang the keys,  
Prayed to God.  
10. – And glory to You, God,  
That we have gathered the grain.  
11. From the mountains, from beneath the mountain  
To the lord’s yard.  
12. Go cut supports  
And support the paths.  
13. The room told us:

14. The walls trembled,  
Where the reapers sat.  
15. And it will tremble even more,  
When they drink moonshine.  
16. Oh, the hen told us  
That there is a barrel of moonshine.  
17. When they drank moonshine,  
They thanked the lord.  
18. That we gathered the grain  
From the mountains, from beneath the mountain.  
19. To the lord, to the granary  
At a lucky hour.  
20. From the granary to the barn,  
From the barn to the little field.  
21. From the barn to the little field  
At the lucky little hour.

1. The wreath rolled from the field

2. The stars shone for us

3. They will still shine  
When we are transporting it.  
4. The head of the household is not home,  
He went to Tyvrov  
5. To sell the horse,  
To buy a maple tree.  
6. To buy a maple tree  
To support the sheaves.  
7. So that the sheaves do not lean,

8. The head’s wife, proud,

9. Rang the keys and the bell,

11. The Ozeryansky work brigade

1. A wreath rolled down from the field,

3. And our splendid lady

4. She jingled the keys

2. Oh, they will still shine,

3. The master is not at home –
