Butter, mother, butter,  
For I grazed goats in the barn.  
I drove them to the dew,  
Give me butter for the scythe.

The brother seated his sister on a chair,  
He himself sat down and went to Kremenets  
To buy a brush and a comb,  
To comb the dewy braid for the wreath.

Filename: Kmeln_374_Da y povii, vitre, z yaru
translation:
1. Blow, wind, from the ravine  
To the wheat ravine.  
2. Blow through the valley,  
Through the red viburnum.  
3. There a girl was standing,  
Breaking the viburnum blossom,  
4. Breaking the viburnum blossom,  
Holding it to her face:  
5. – That I may be like  
That little viburnum.  
6. – Oh, you will be, daughter, you will be,  
If you stay near me.  
7. But if you leave me,  
Your beauty will fall away.

1. And the fog over the valley,  
Over the red viburnum.  
2. There a girl was walking,  
Breaking off the viburnum,  
3. Gathering it into bunches,  
Aligning it to her face.  
4. Aligning it to her face,  
Asked her mother:  
5. – Will I be like  
That viburnum?  
6. – You will, daughter, you will,  
As long as you are with me.  
7. And if you leave me,  
Your beauty will fade away.

1. – Oh mother, dear mother,  
Stroke my little head.  
2. – Ask, daughter, your friends,  
Her hands are gentle.

1. Sprinkle us, mother,  
With consecrated water,  
Sprinkle us, mother,  
With consecrated water.  
2. Bless us, mother,  
With a happy fate,  
Bless us, mother,  
With a happy fate.

Sprinkle us, mother,  
With Jordan water,  
With a fortunate fate.

1. Sprinkle us, mother,  
With holy water.  
2. With holy water [2]  
And also with good fortune.

1. A mother loved her daughter,  
She protected her like the moon,  
2. She shone on her like a star,  
Sent her off to the village:  
3. – Oh be, my dear daughter, beautiful,  
Like the bright little star.  
4. Be, daughter, young,  
Obedient to all people:  
5. To the old and the young,  
And to the holy God.  
6. No one will speak ill of you,  
May God bless you.

1. |: Oh, pine tree,  
Green through summer and winter. : |  
|: Our girl  
Was cheerful on Friday. : |  

2. |: And on Saturday  
She was combing her dew-covered hair, : |  
|: And on Sunday  
She was going to the wedding, crying: : |  

3. |: Oh, I pity you,  
My mother,  
: |  
|: The rue and mint  
Remain with you. : |  

4. |: Oh, pine tree,  
Green through summer and winter. : |

Get out of the way, enemies, get out of the way,  
Do not cross the road,  
So that our path is smooth,  
So that our wedding pair is happy.

Out of the way, enemies, out of the way,  
Do not cross the road,  
May the road be smooth for us,  
So that we may be a happy couple.

Oh priest, priest,  
Our father,  
Open the church  
For us,  
Marry the children,  
Of the same age,  
For it is time!

Let us thank Poponko,  
Like our dear father,  
Who married us and took little:  
One red (coin),  
For the young gentleman.

Marusya was walking from under the wedding wreath,  
Sowing seedlings from her sleeve:  
– Grow, seedlings, little heads,  
For our Marusya is already married.

Oh, there is nothing like that in Lviv,  
Like what is on Seryozhenka's table,  
Oh, there is something to eat,  
Oh, there is something to drink,  
There is someone to invite.  
Sweet honey,  
Green wine  
Is set on the table.

Young cabbage, young,  
Marusya, the maiden, was slicing,  
She was slicing and chopping,  
Because she was expecting guests.

Take the spoon, Marusya,  
Show us the path,

Where is that uncle Joseph,  
Who carries the vodka?  
Pressing it under his arm,  
Let it go sour on him.

1. And at our father-in-law's,  
The vodka stands as it stands  
On the table,  
The vodka stands as it stands  
On the table.  
2. And at our sister-in-law's,  
Overgrown, overgrown  
Is the garden bed in the vegetable garden,  
Overgrown, overgrown  
Is the garden bed in the vegetable garden.

The women kneaded the wedding bread, set it to rise, and went outside to joke and dance themselves.
The very old elder,
The dove, very gray,
Sits at the table like a shame,
Lead us out of the house
To the yard to dance,
Watch over us,
Whether the heels are high,
Whether the legs are not twisted,
When they go to dance,
Whether they will not limp.

1. That white feather fell...
That white feather fell,
It pleased us,
Young Nadia
Clung to the heart,
To the heart, to the body,
Because she wanted Vanya.

2. Come out, mother, in a fur coat,
Because it’s not in the holy spirit,
Whatever the shaggy coat,
Such is your rich son-in-law.

In the dark swamp,  
And at the table is gold.  
Let us into the house,  
To gather that gold.

Don’t sit sideways, Ivan,  
Don’t look with one eye,  
Come closer,  
This is your little heart.

Ducks were swimming on the Danube,  
Along the shore they led their mother,  
Along the greenish shore  
With their young father.

1. Stand, mother, against me  
And drink to me  
2. With a full, full cup,  
With good fortune.

1. Was I not your child, my mother,  
That you walk around and say:  
– If only I could get rid of her.  
2. You will get rid of her, my mother,  
You will get rid of her, get rid of her,  
There will come a Sunday  
When you will cry.  
3. There will come such a Sunday,  
There will come such a day,  
When you will weep and wail:  
– Where is my child?

1. Oh, the mother drank away her daughter,  
Drank away, drank away,  
My poor little head,  
What have I done?  

2. Oh, the mother drank away her daughter  
On sweet honey,  
And who will wash the scarf  
In winter on the ice?  

3. Oh, I will go to my son-in-law  
And ask him:  
"Let me, son-in-law, have my daughter,  
To help wash the scarf."  

4. "I will let you once, I will let you twice,  
But not forever,  
If you need to wash the scarf,  
Then ask the neighbors."

1. Farewell, my friend,  
I will be leaving  
From behind your table.  
2. Give me a glass,  
Let's say goodbye,  
Because we will no longer meet  
At the celebration.  
3. Even if we meet,  
It will no longer be the same,  
You as a young woman,  
And I as a girl.  
4. You as a young woman,  
And I as a girl,  
My beloved will not allow  
Me to go out with him.  
5. Let us remember, friend,  
How we lived together,  
No one knew and will not know  
What we talked about.  
6. No one knew and will not know  
What we talked about,  
We shared  
A grain of bitter nut.

1. My little friend,  
Let us say goodbye,  
We will no longer meet  
At the gathering.  

2. Oh, even if we meet,  
It won’t be as girls,  
I will be crying  
For you.  

3. Let’s go, little friend,  
Along that street,  
Better as a girl,  
Than as a grown woman.  

4. No one will knock you down,  
No one will scold you,  
Every guy considers you  
As a girl.  

5. Every guy considers you  
As a girl,  
He goes to the music,  
Strolling with his tub.  

6. My little friend,  
Let us say goodbye,  
We will no longer meet  
At the gathering.  

7. Oh, even if we meet,  
It won’t be as girls,  
I will always  
Cry for you.  

8. Remember, little friend,  
How we lived together,  
We shared  
Like a grain of walnut.  

9. Remember, little friend,  
How it was at first,  
We sewed and embroidered  
By the fence.

1. Bless, O God,  
Both father and mother,  
Sending their child  
On the road.  
2. She bowed to her family,  
Bowed also to strangers,  
Both small and great...

1. Be healthy,
My dear mother,
Did I not cook for you
Dinner early in the morning?

2. Did I not cook dinner,
Did I not bake bread?
What did I, dear mother,
Do to upset you so much?

3. Be healthy,
Holy icons,
I wiped you
Three times a week.

4. Be healthy,
Bright windows,
I wiped you
Until sunrise.

5. Be healthy,
Oak tables,
Where my suitors
Drank and ate.

6. Be healthy,
Pine thresholds,
Oh, where my white feet
Have walked.

7. Be healthy,
Yellow earth,
I anointed you
Early on Saturday.

1. The pine was burning, blazing,  
|: The pine was burning, blazing,  
A girl was standing beneath it.  :|  
2. A girl was standing beneath it,  
|: A girl was standing beneath it,  
Combing her fair hair.  :|  
3. – Oh my braids, my braids,  
|: Oh my braids, my braids,  
You have served me for a long time.  :|  
4. You will serve no longer,  
|: You will serve no longer,  
You will go under the white shroud.  :|  
5. Under the white shroud with edges,  
|: Under the white shroud with edges,  
You will no longer go with the lads.  :|  
6. Under the white shroud with a scarf,  
|: Under the white shroud with a scarf,  
I will no longer go as a bride’s attendant.  :|  
7. Under the white shroud with flowers,  
|: Under the white shroud with flowers,  
I will no longer go with the maidens.  :|  
8. The pine was burning, the stump was burning,  
|: The pine was burning, the stump was burning,  
Play, musicians, all day long,  :|  
9. The pine was burning, the branches too,  
|: The pine was burning, the branches too,  
Play, musicians, the wedding.  :|

1. Play, musicians,  
Play, hurdy-gurdies,  
For I am already departing  
From father, from mother.  

2. Play, musicians,  
Play the pipes,  
Be healthy, those  
Who are in the house.  

3. Play, musicians,  
Play the barishi,  
Be healthy,  
My comrades.  

4. Play, musicians,  
Play the whistles,  
Be healthy,  
My neighbors.  

5. Play, musicians,  
Play, hurdy-gurdy players,  
Be healthy,  
My companions.  

6. A merry house,  
A merry yard,  
Until all  
Of my wedding is finished.  

7. Now my house  
Is already saddened,  
And I have completely  
Set off to a stranger’s.

Filename: Kmeln_431_Oi komu zh ty, Hanusiu, klaniaiesh
translation:
1. – Oh, who are you bowing to, Hanusya,
Oh, who are you bowing to,
That you have no dear father?
2. – Oh, I will bow to a stranger,
I will bow to a stranger, and so it will be,
Since there is no father and there will not be.

1. Good evening, dear mother,  
At your house,  
I want to ask you, dear mother,  
Something.  

2. To ask something  
And also to talk,  
Oh God, mother,  
May we live well.  

3. They seated me  
At the tisy (plane tree) table,  
My mother comes, greeting me  
With her kind word.  

4. My mother comes, greeting me  
And all their family, –  
There, dear and lovely,  
Is a foreign child.  

5. There, dear and lovely,  
There she is splendid,  
Blooming like a poppy,  
Like a cherry in the orchard.  

6. There she is splendid,  
There she is beautiful,  
Blooming like a poppy,  
Like a rose in the orchard.  

7. Under my house  
Flowed a river,  
And you – my mother,  
I – your daughter-in-law.  

8. Under my house  
A pit was dug,  
From now on I know  
That you are my mother.

Oh, my master!  
Where are you going?  
Where will you be looking out from?  
And who will advise us?  
And why do you need such a sad house?  
When you had somewhere to live.  
Get up and see,  
Who will you leave the children with,  
And who will care for them?  
Ah, my master, my master!  
Free tempo

1. Farewell, my soul, for the last time,  
For the last time over the grave,  
|: For I am already going on the final journey,  
And I give my soul to God. :|  
2. My fate is appointed,  
It's time to prepare,  
|: I do not take riches with me,  
For they are not needed there. |  
3. My fate is appointed,  
Unknown to anyone,  
|: You remain with the Father,  
And I am already home forever. :|  
4. And this path is the same for all,  
No one bypasses it,  
|: All peoples walk along it,  
No one bypasses it. :|

(On the Death of Father)  
1. Oh our God, God,  
Our merciful God,  
Our father has died,  
We are left alone.  
2. We are left alone,  
We are left by ourselves,  
Oh our God, God,  
How will we live?  
3. How will we live,  
We will suffer,  
Now we have to  
Pay for everything.  
4. When we had father, –  
We worried about nothing,  
Because our father  
Did everything himself.  
5. Oh when father was alive,  
There was a family too,  
When father died,  
The family forgot.  
6. The family forgot,  
But there are still neighbors,  
Our father died  
And now forever.  
7. I will go to the cemetery  
And will ask:  
– Tell me, dear father,  
How should I live?  
8. – Oh live your life, daughter,  
As you yourself know,  
Because the heavy earth  
Has laid its hands on me.  
9. The earth has laid down,  
That I will no longer rise,  
You already have a husband  
And you still have a mother.  
10. You still have your mother,  
She will give birth again,  
You are still young,  
You will live well.  
11. Oh I had my mother,  
Oh I had her, had her,  
Now I have given my mother  
Away in marriage.  
12. I have given my mother away,  
...  
Now we will  
Be alone again.

1. Oh, the hour is approaching,  
The whole family is saying farewell.  
2. The children are walking and mourning,  
And they are not preparing for the coffin.  
3. Oh you, children, do not grieve,  
But prepare everything for the coffin.  
4. I ask you, please make an effort,  
Pray for the repose of the soul.  
5. And do not cry for me,  
I am not taking anything with me.  
6. I am only taking four boards,  
And some shavings thick enough for a pillow.  
7. When you give birth,  
Do not forget to give money.  
8. Because I will need money there,  
My soul will go up to heaven.

1. We sit at the tables,  
The Mother of God among us.  
2. We sit at dinner,  
And the Savior stands behind.  
3. And people pray to God,  
The soul stands by the table.  
4. People prayed to God,  
And the soul went to the grave.  
5. As soon as the soul left the house,  
The Mother of God met it:  
6. – Where have you been, soul,  
What sins have you committed in this world?  
7. – I went among the family,  
Because I am observing the ninth day memorial.  
8. I asked the whole family  
To pray for me.  
9. We sit at the tables,  
Taking leave of the hosts:  
10. – Thank you, hosts,  
For your God-given gifts.  
11. Thank you, hostess,  
And your entire family as well.  
12. May your God-given gifts  
Shine on the altar.

1. From Saturday to Sunday  
The wedding begins.  
2. Who starts it?  
Who helps him?  
3. Father starts it,  
God helps him.  
4. From Saturday to Sunday  
The wedding begins.  
5. Who starts it?  
Who helps him?  
6. Mother starts it,  
God helps her.  
* Likewise brother and sister.

Express yourself, mother,  
For it is no longer early,  
Whether to the meadow for herbs,  
Or to ask for the wedding.

[– Good day to those  
Who are in this house:]  
To the old and the young,  
And also to the Holy God.  
– Be healthy,  
May you be well,  
For not forgetting us,  
[For visiting us]  
With golden bouquets.

1. Oh, I will stand on the log,  
Waiting for the whole family.  
2. Come together, dear family,  
Both poor and rich.  
3. Poor and rich,  
Far and near.  
4. The rich ride on horses,  
The poor go on foot.  
5. The poor greet,  
The rich scold.

1. – A rooster is singing at the gate,  
And somewhere my old mother is dozing,  
Oh, somewhere my old mother has fallen asleep,  
She forgot about her little child.  

2. – I have not forgotten, my daughter, I have not forgotten,  
When you came to the gate, I heard,  
When you came to the window, I came out,  
I brought out a rue wreath.

No one will guess  
Where we have been:  
Whether to the forest for hazelnuts,  
Or to the meadow for viburnum,  
Or to the mountains for herbs,  
Or asking for a wedding?

1. The evening star has risen,  
A young woman has come from the village.  
2. When she came to her parents' yard,  
She stopped and cried.  
3. Oh, not like a stepchild,  
But like her own father.  
4. She came to her parents' yard  
And stood there, thinking.  
5. She walked under the maple tree,  
And spoke to the maple:  
6. – Oh maple, little maple,  
Unfold your green leaves.  
7. Unfold your green leaves,  
Cheer up my heart.  
8. For it is saddened  
Since I was betrothed.  
9. My mother betrothed me  
When I was little.  
10. She betrothed me,  
But did not teach me how to work.  
11. Strangers will teach me,  
And you, mother, will regret it.  
12. The servants will teach me,  
The Ivan’s little sisters.  
** How hard it will be for you.

1. The evening star has risen,  
A young woman has come from the village.  
2. When she came to her parents' yard,  
She stood there and cried.  
3. Oh, not so much for the household servants,  
As for her own dear father.  
4. – Oh, my yard, my household,  
You are my dear father.  
5. She walked under the maple tree,  
And spoke to the maple:  
6. – Oh, maple, little maple,  
Unfold your green leaves.  
7. Unfold your green leaves,  
Cheer up my heart.  
8. For it is saddened  
Since I was betrothed.  
9. My mother betrothed me  
When I was little.  
10. She betrothed me,  
But did not teach me how to work.  
11. Strangers will teach me,  
And you, mother, will feel sorrow.  
12. Strangers will teach me—  
The sisters of Ivan.

1. A clear dawn has risen,  
Marunya came from the village.  
2. She walked around the yard,  
Spoke to the maple tree:  
3. "Oh maple, dear little maple,  
Unfold yourself, green one.  
4. Unfold yourself, green one,  
Cheer up my heart.  
5. Oh, it is sorrowful,  
Since it was betrothed.  
6. His mother betrothed him,  
When I was little.  
7. She betrothed, betrothed him,  
But did not teach him how to work.  
8. People will teach Marusya,  
You will regret it, mother.

1. Grape, little grape,  
I ask you for a small favor.  
2. If I have to follow an unkind one,  
Grape, do not bloom beautifully.

I for the first time,***  
I in God's time...  
**  
Bless, God,  
Both father and mother,  
To their child  
To begin a wreath.  
*** The same for the second and third time.

1. Oh, under the orchard,  
Under the green cherry tree.  
2. Along that way  
Young Vasyl was coming from a wedding.  
3. Oh, he thought  
That a cuckoo was calling.

1. And under the little chest, lady's bedstraw,
Under the little chest, sage.
There the gentlemen sat,
Braiding wreaths
Rue wreaths, rue wreaths, rue wreaths.
2. Oh, a young gentleman came to them,
Asked for a rue wreath.
– I won’t give you a wreath,
You are not her boyfriend,
Not yours, not yours, not yours!

1. Oh wreath, my wreath,  
From the cross-shaped periwinkle.  
2. I buried you, wreath,  
I hid you in a chest.  
3. And now I will part with you,  
And I must weep.

Oh my wreath, my wreath,  
Made of birch wreath,  
I cherished you,  
Locked you in the chest,  
Now I will untie you,  
I must cry.

As I was going to the korovai,  
I was carrying a box of eggs,  
The boys wrestled me down  
And broke the eggs,  
They caused me trouble,  
So what will I take to the korovai now?

As I was going to the korovai,  
I was carrying a box of eggs,  
The boys knocked me down,  
And they broke the eggs,  
They caused me trouble,  
So what will I take to the korovai?

1. We were walking to the wedding bread,  
Carrying a box of eggs,  
The boys ran into us,  
They hit the box,  
They caused me trouble,  
So what will I take to the wedding bread?

No one will guess  
What is in this korovai:  
Water from seven wells,  
Flour from eight mills,  
Eggs from seven hens  
For our korovai.

How we kneaded the korovai,  
Carrying water from the Danube,  
Not from the Danube, – from the well,  
The sisters kneaded the korovai.

Filename: Vinnytsia_434_A my na tik vodu nosyly
translation:
And we were carrying water to the well  
And asking God:  
– God, let the rye grow,  
Rye and also wheat,  
Pink flowers,  
So that the children would love each other.

To the senior bread-baker,  
Look out the little window,  
Is the sun high,  
Or is it early,  
Let us go for a walk,  
And if it gets late,  
We will part ways separately.  
And if it gets rather late,  
We will part ways quite separately.

The senior bread maker,  
Do you have beaten eggs  
To glaze the wedding bread?  
* Probably, beaten. (L. Ye.)

I swept, elder, I swept  
So that your little head  
Would not burn in the oven,  
If I do not sweep,  
Still your little head will pay for it.

Our oven is laughing out loud...
And our oven is laughing out loud,
It desires a korovai (wedding bread),
And the hearth is smiling,
It is hoping for a korovai.

Our oven is laughing...
And our oven is indeed laughing,
It desires a bread loaf,
And the hearth smiles,
Hoping for the bread loaf.

A loaf of bread sits in the oven,  
Oh, a loaf of bread sits in the oven,  
And a loaf of dough lies on the oven,  
Oh rise, dough,  
Take care of the loaf.

Filename: Vinnytsia_441_ Khoch techy, pyvonko, khoch ne techy
translation:
Whether the beer flows or not,  
Our loaf is already in the oven,  
And we will not go home  
Until we take the loaf out of the oven.

Filename: Vinnytsia_442_ Shos ty, Hanuniu, bez rodu
translation:
1. – Ganunya, you seem so lonely,  
Why is it quiet with you on Saturday,  
No one comes to dance,  
To sing a song.  
2. – Oh, I have a family,  
So the house will be full,  
So they will come, light a fire,  
And sing a song.

Meet us, God,  
The first time on the road,  
The second time on the threshold,  
The third time on the donkey,  
Nadunia is coming to visit.

445. Have I, mother, been a nuisance? (OR-V4-09)  
1. – Have I, mother, been a nuisance?  
Have I been a nuisance to you,  
Because I walked around your yard?  
2. – It wasn’t you, daughter, who was a nuisance,  
Your girlfriends were the ones who bothered.  
3. Running up to the window,  
Calling out to the street.  
4. – Wait, mother, until Sunday,  
You won’t see a single girl.  
5. Wait, mother, until two o’clock,  
You won’t see a single one.  
6. They will come, passing the gates,  
They will cause you sorrow.  
7. And you will sit by the window,  
Won’t my daughter come out?  
8. They will come to weave those wreaths,  
Carrying them across your yard.

1. – Did I bother you, mother?  
Did I really bother you,  
Walking around your yard?  

2. – It wasn’t you, daughter, who bothered me,  
Your friends did.  

3. Running up to the window,  
Calling out to come outside.  

4. – Wait, mother, until Sunday,  
You won’t see a single girl.  

5. Wait, mother, until two o’clock,  
You won’t see a single one.  

6. They will be walking, passing by the gate,  
They will cause you sorrow.  

7. And you will sit by the window,  
Wondering if my daughter will come out.  

8. They will be walking, weaving those wreaths,  
Carrying them through your yard.

Give us some butter, mother,  
For I have grazed the cow for you,  
Drove it to the dew,  
Give butter for the scythe.

Oh, mother, give me some butter,  
Because I was tending your cows,  
Driving them to the dew,  
Give butter for the scythe.  
I drove them and grazed them,  
Give butter or lard.

Oh, mother, give me some butter,  
For I have been herding your cows,  
Driving them to the dew,  
Give me some butter for the scythe.  
I drove them, I grazed them,  
Give me some butter or lard.

Oh, mother, give me a chair,  
A golden comb,  
A black sheepskin coat,  
So that the young woman may sit.  
The carpenters did not make the chair,  
The tailors did not sew the coat,  
The tailors did not sew the coat,  
So that the young woman may sit.

Bless, God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To braid the hair.  
And for the third time,  
And in God's time,  
Bless, God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To braid the hair.  
Slowly  
And for the second time,  
And in God's time,  
Bless, God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To braid the hair.

Bless, O God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To untie the braid.  
And for the third time,  
And in God’s time,  
Bless, O God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To untie the braid.  
And for the second time,  
And in God’s time,  
Bless, O God,  
Both father and mother  
To their child  
To untie the braid.

To the braid, brother, to the braid,  
Ask for gold for the day:  
Let the gold not shine,  
Let the sister have fun.

To the braid, little brother, to the braid,  
Ask for gold for a day:  
Let the gold not shine,  
Let the little sister have fun.

Filename: Vinnytsia_451v_A brat kisonku ta y rozplitaie
translation:
1. |: And the brother unties 
The little kitty,
Remembers the rose. :|
2. – Don’t go, sister,
Young to marry,
Because the rose is blooming.
3. The rose is blooming
And also the periwinkle
For the young one’s wreath.

Filename: Vinnytsia_451_A brat kisonku ta y rozplitaie
translation:
1. |: And the brother unties  
The little kitty,  
He remembers the rose. :|  
2. – Don’t go, sister,  
Young to marriage,  
Because the rose is blooming.  
3. The rose is blooming,  
And the periwinkle too,  
For the young one’s wreath.

And the brother was undoing his sister’s braid,  
Where did he put the plaits?  
He placed them on the fire – they do not burn;  
He placed them on the water – they do not float,  
It seems they will be lost to the elder bride*.  
* Possibly, they will be found? (L. Ye.)

I will sow marigolds in the forest,  
I thought, my dear mother, that they would sprout.  
And now those marigolds are blooming,  
And they are unbraiding my blond hair.  
And now my marigolds have bloomed,  
And now my blond hair has been unbraided.

1. It was winter, it was winter,  
And I am waiting for summer,  
I sowed marigolds  
In the spring garden.  
2. And now my marigolds  
Are blooming,  
And now my fair hair  
Is being untied.  
3. And now my marigolds  
Have already faded,  
And now my fair hair  
Has been untied.

1. And along the forest edge  
There was a narrow path,  
2. Along it walked  
Pan Lyoshka with the boyars.  
3. The viburnum  
Blocked his way,  
4. He drew his sabre,  
Began to cut the viburnum.  
5. The viburnum began  
To speak to him,  
The viburnum  
Began to speak to him:  
6. – Do not cut me down,  
I am not planted for you,  
7. I was grown  
Only for you.

1. Oh, from the meadow on Sunday  
Maria went into the sage:  
– Hey, whoever finds me in the sage,  
To them I will remain.  

2. The little girl went, but did not find,  
She picked a flower and came back:  
– My beautiful, green flower,  
My dear, beloved daughter,  
To whom did you remain?  

3. Oh, from the meadow on Sunday...  

4. The father went, but did not find...  

5. Oh, from the meadow on Sunday...  

6. That Sashko went and found,  
He picked a flower and came back:  
– My dear, beloved wife,  
You have remained with me!

I ask you, dear friend, I ask,  
Send a nice hat,  
I will sew a little flower  
From green periwinkle.  
I will sew a wreath  
Red, for the women.

Water us, mother,  
With rainwater,  
Send us off  
At a good hour.

Chop the viburnum,  
Carpet the valley  
For the young woman, the young man  
To the village house.  
As to the church:  
To God’s house.

Chop the viburnum,  
Cover the valley  
For the young woman, the young man  
To the village house.  
As to the church:  
To the house of God.

We are going from the wedding,  
Leading the bride,  
The young bride, very young,  
Like a bright red berry.

We thank the pop,  
Like a dear father,  
Who married us,  
Took little:  
Red gold  
From the young gentleman.

1. The viburnum is chopped, chopped,
Our Hannusia is already betrothed,
She has been given away to her mother-in-law,
And my daughter-in-law has already been wed.
2. Fly, fly, little falcon, ahead of us,
And bring better news than us,
Let them go out with a kalach (traditional bread),
Because Hannusia is going after the young master.
3. Not with the young master, not with the young master, but with a man,
Their white hands were tied with a towel.
Not with a towel, not with a towel, but with a fishing line,
So she wouldn’t remain a maiden.

Out of the way, enemies, out of the way,  
Do not cross the road,  
Let the family cross,  
So that there may be a happy hour.  
Let the man cross,  
So that he may be [young for a long life.]

Enemies, enemies, out of the way,  
Do not cross the path,  
Let the family pass,  
So there will be a happy hour.

Enemies, enemies, out of the way,  
Do not cross the road,  
Let the family pass,  
So that the hour will be happy.

Filename: Vinnytsia_465_A brat sestru za stil vede
translation:
65. And the brother leads his sister to the table (OR-V6)  
1. And the brother  
Leads his sister  
To the table,  
Early, early,  
And the brother  
Leads his sister  
To the table,  
Very early.*  
2. The little brother  
Then asks me:  
3. – Who is, sister,  
The dearest to you?  
4. – The dearest to me  
Is my father.  
5. – Who is, sister,  
The most faithful to you?  
6. – The most faithful to me  
Is my mother.  
7. – Oh sister,  
That is all untrue.  
8. The little brother  
Then asks me:  
9. – Who is, sister,  
The sweetest to you?  
10. – The sweetest to me  
Is my beloved.  
11. Oh sister,  
That is the truth.

1. The dove walks  
On the table,  
Oh dear God,  
The dove walks  
On the table.  
2. Up to its knees  
In honey,  
3. With its wings  
Scooping honey,  
4. Toasting all the friends,  
5. Only passing by Hannusia,  
6. Because Ihor  
Is already stepping,  
7. Intending to kiss.  
8. May God help him.

1. Dear father walks around,  
Like a little gray dove,  
He walks around the house, carrying a glass,  
So that they sit down to have dinner.  
2. Dear mother walks around,  
Like a little gray dove,  
They walk around the house, asking the servants,  
So that they sit down to have dinner.  
Oh, there is something to eat, oh, there is something to drink,  
There is someone to ask.

Here is our cow,  
Beloved, dear,  
She gave birth to a son,  
She brought him lunch.

From the table to the threshold  
The well-trodden path,  
The young family  
Used to bring dinner.

1. Blow, blow, little wind,  
To our light.  
2. To our dear guests,  
Who are sitting on the pier.  
3. May they eat and drink,  
And talk cheerfully.  
4. May they drink,  
And talk happily.

We ask you:
Have some food with us—
Whether it’s chicken,
Or whether it’s goslings,
Then they reply:
But we are such butterflies,
When we start eating,
You won’t keep up carrying it,
Thank you!
But you are the swans
That float on the water,
We ask you!

A matchmaker asks another matchmaker,  
Bringing honey vodka:  
– Oh, my matchmaker,  
My little dove,  
I ask you  
For the chicken,  
For the goslings,  
For those swans  
That swim on the water,  
And for those partridges  
That run around the house.

The walls moved,
Like matchmakers sat down,
And they will move even more,
When they drink vodka.
– Don’t move, walls,
We haven’t sat long,
We will drink the vodka
And then go home.
Alternative 2nd stanza:
Don’t groan...
Honey-wine...
We will go home.

The walls moved,  
Like matchmakers they sat down,  
And they will move even more,  
When they get drunk on vodka.  
– Don’t move, walls,  
We haven’t sat long,  
We will drink the vodka  
And then go home.  
Alternative 2nd stanza:  
Do not groan...  
Honey-wine...  
We will go home.

The walls shook,  
As the boyars sat down,  
And they will shake even more,  
When they get drunk on vodka.

Glasses and plates clinked,  
They gave us a little vodka,  
We don’t want much,  
Just to wet our lips.

Where is that uncle Joseph,  
Who brings the vodka?  
Let him bring it,  
So that we have enough.

Where is that uncle Joseph,  
Who brings the vodka?  
Let him bring it,  
So that we have enough.

Where is that uncle Joseph,  
The one who carries the vodka?  
Let him carry it,  
So that we have enough.  
When he presses it,  
Let it turn sour on him.

1. Was the wedding mother somewhere around here?  
She promised to give vodka.  
She promised something sweet,  
But only gave something bitter.  
And she didn’t drink herself,  
And didn’t give us any.  

2. The matchmaker was good,  
But the female matchmaker was bad,  
She brewed vodka,  
But didn’t give us any.  

3. I crept, crept  
Along the hearth,  
Found a little hole,  
Drank the vodka,  
And didn’t drink myself,  
And didn’t give us any,  
Because she was cunning.

Don't bend, father, don't bend,  
You have a heifer in the barn,  
So go ahead and sell her,  
But give us some vodka.

Why, friends, do you not sing?  
Because you have teeth made of radish,  
You are afraid to sing,  
To show teeth made of radish.

Why, friends, do you not sing?  
Because you have teeth of radish,  
You are afraid to sing,  
To show radish teeth.

You are stubborn fellows,  
[crawled under the benches],  
May your eyes pop out.

The eldest bridesmaid is a ribbon,  
The second like a pillow,  
The third like a grave,  
Killed a piglet with her breast.

The elder bridesmaid is a ribbon,  
The second is like a pillow,  
The third is like a grave,  
Killed the piglet with her breast.

From under the wild pear tree  
Came out three bridesmaids:  
One was toothy,  
The second was clumsy,  
And the third was shod,  
Like a swollen frog.

On the shelf stood a mug,  
And a kitty peed in that mug,  
Oh, give that mug  
To water the older friend.

You, boors, are not to revel,  
You, boors, are to herd pigs,  
To scold the young man,  
Because he does not know how to kiss.

You, rowdies, are not to revel,  
You, rowdies, are to herd pigs,  
To scold the young man,  
Because he does not know how to kiss.

The elder girl with a braid,  
Ate her fill of millet,  
Turns this way and that,  
Millet spills out of her.

The elder millet stalk,  
Has eaten millet,  
Turns this way and that,  
Millet spills out from it.

– The older servant girl by the wall,  
Her shirt is not hers,  
Her mother scolded her for being a servant,  
She borrowed a shirt from a neighbor.  
– You lie, girlfriends, like pigs,  
I have three chests of shirts,  
My mother scolded me for being a servant,  
She took a shirt from the chest.

The senior bridesmaid by the wall,  
Wearing a shirt that isn’t hers,  
A neighbor came, recognized her:  
– Give back the shirt, you stole it.  
– You lie, bridesmaids-pigs,  
I have two chests of shirts,  
My mother sewed them for me,  
So I could go in good health.

Oh, it’s strange to us, strange,  
That our matchmaker is not visible.  
Does she not have a coat?  
Is she borrowing boots?  
Or is she putting on galoshes?  
Or is she returning somewhere with an uncle?  
To this same melody:  
The matchmaker will stay overnight with the matchmaker,  
Where shall we lay her to sleep?  
We’ll lay her on the stove—it’s stuffy,  
We’ll lay her on the floor—it’s boring,  
We’ll lay her on the [boards],  
Let’s drink a little vodka.

The matchmaker arrived at the matchmaker’s,  
Where shall we lay her down to sleep?  
If we lay her on the stove – it’s stuffy,  
If we lay her on the floor – it’s boring,  
We lay her on a blanket,  
And the matchmaker on the pile.  
Hold on, matchmaker, to the pile,  
So you won’t fall off your backside.

1. – Oh, where are you going,  
My son,  
Are you leaving,  
That you are not waiting  
For my evening meal?  
2. – Oh, I have  
A girl  
In the garden,  
She cooked me  
The evening meal.  
3. She cooked me  
The evening meal,  
She laid out  
Two handkerchiefs for me.

1. Oh, the young man goes to the girl,  
Like the bright moon to the star,  
And he asks his mother,  
What to say to the girl.  
2. – Don’t worry, son, about anything,  
For there are matchmakers for that,  
Who will lead and speak  
On your behalf, young one.

A golden grain strikes the wall,  
Like a son-in-law visiting his mother-in-law,  
But to that proud mother-in-law,  
To that young girl.

1. Step wide, horses,  
For we have far to home,  
Lay thin and white  
Papers  
Under the black horses.  
2. Step narrowly, horses,  
For we have near to home,  
Lay thin and white  
Papers  
Under the black horses.

Oh, how strange it is for us, strange,
That the son-in-law is nowhere to be seen:
Does he not have a coat,
Or is he borrowing boots,
Or putting on verzuны*,
That he is taking so long to come out?
Did he fall through the bridge,
That he is delayed for so long?
*There were no boots; they made various verzuны: leather, rubber, leather ones, whoever could sew what. (Translator's note)

Oh, it's raining outside,  
It's slippery under the threshold,  
Let me into the house,  
Because evening is near.

Do not be angry, matchmaker,  
That there are so many of us,  
Thirty-three  
At your yard.  
Thirty-four more,  
And all with black eyebrows.

Do not be afraid, matchmaker,  
There are not many of us:  
Only thirty-three  
At your doorstep.

Come out, Hanunya, come out,  
Do not harm yourself,  
Stand on the doorstep,  
[Listen to] Vasyl in God.

Oh, that ragged one,  
They closed the gates,  
For a shot of vodka  
They don’t let me to the girl.

1. Come, mother, you come,  
Come, mother, come  
And take the bandages off me.  
2. I cannot walk in them,  
It's time to share with friends.

1. She loved having a daughter,  
She bought ribbons.  
2. Now she became an enemy,  
She took the ribbons off.  
3. She took the ribbons off,  
And hid them in her pocket.

Filename: Vinnytsia_503_Oi shcho zh bo tse ta pryveziano
translation:
Oh, what has been brought here  
And placed on the table,  
As white as pain,  
As thin as silk,  
Collected from the shop  
And laid on the table.

And all the matchmakers are colorful  
In expensive necklaces,  
They are proud of themselves,  
They hug, they kiss,  
They have pride,  
That they cover the young couple,

Be ashamed, Maria, be ashamed,  
Do not let yourself be covered,  
You will no longer have luxury,  
You will no longer have [maidenhood].

Don't get up, Hannusya, don't get up,  
Until Ivanko leads you,  
Takes you by the right hand,  
Calls you dear in his heart.

I protect you, sister,  
Bestow happiness and health upon you,  
So that you may be as rich as the earth,  
So that you may be as joyful as spring,  
So that you may be as healthy as water,  
So that you may be as beautiful as a full moon.

I bless you, sister,  
Granting happiness and health,  
So that you may be rich as the earth,  
So that you may be joyful as spring,  
So that you may be healthy as water,  
So that you may be beautiful as a full moon.

I cover you, sister,  
Granting happiness and health,  
So that you may be rich like the earth,  
So that you may be cheerful like the spring,  
So that you may be healthy like the water,  
So that you may be beautiful like a full bloom.

1. – My dear mother,  
Old, beloved,  
2. Do not let me  
Be away from you this evening.  
3. – Keep, daughter,  
The wreath to yourself.  
4. Daughter, you will be  
Healthy because of the wreath.  
5. Because those people  
Have been sitting since yesterday.  
6. They want to take you  
And give you to Sashko.

1. – Good evening, tables set,  
Even the walls are painted.  
2. Who will paint you,  
When the mother gives away her daughter?  
3. – The mother will paint,  
Remembering her daughter:  
4. – Oh, where is my child,  
Like a blade of grass in the field?  
5. Oh, she works hard,  
But pleases no one.  
6. Neither the mother-in-law nor the mother-in-law,  
Nor the young sister-in-law.  
7. They sat down to dinner,  
The daughter-in-law went to fetch water.  
8. The daughter-in-law went for water  
To the wide ford.  
9. She stepped on the stones,  
Did not fill the bucket.  
10. Did not fill the bucket,  
But filled it with tears.  
11. By the time she came back with water,  
Dinner had ended.  
12. They themselves began to drink water,  
The daughter-in-law was washing spoons.  
13. – Wash the spoons, daughter-in-law,  
And look into the pots.  
14. – I have already eaten,  
If only mother knew.  
15. If only mother knew,  
She would have sent dinner.  
16. With a bright full moon,  
A dear little brother,  
17. With a bright star,  
A dear little sister.

1. Think, Hannusia, think,  
Will you swim across the Danube?  
2. Will you swim across all the rivers  
To your father-in-law forever?  
3. Oh, there you will have grief,  
There will be no breakfast.  
4. Dinner at midnight,  
...  
5. She swept, she washed,  
And then sat down and cried.  
6. If my mother knew this,  
She would have sent dinner.  
7. Not by the moon, then by the star,  
Not by the brother, then by the sister.  
8. Not by the bright moon,  
But by the dearest brother.  

To the same melody:  
1. Run, mother, to the pantry,  
Talk to the chest.  
2. And bring a scarf  
For your daughter's head.  
3. Run, mother, to the pantry,  
Do not speak to the chest.  
4. But take the scarf  
For your daughter's head.  

To the same melody:  
1. Evening star has risen,  
Hannusia has come from the village.  
2. She walks around the yard,  
Speaks to the maple:  
3. – Oh maple, little maple,  
Unfold your green leaves.  
4. Unfold your green leaves,  
Cheer up my heart.  
5. Because it is sad,  
Since it was betrothed.  
6. My mother betrothed me,  
When I was little.  
7. She betrothed me, she betrothed me,  
But did not teach me how to work.  
8. Strangers teach me,  
You will feel sorry, mother,  
9. Gutinichka teaches me,  
Ivan’s mother.  
10. And also my sister-in-law teaches me,  
Ivan’s little sister.  
11. Strangers teach me,  
You will feel sorry, mother.

1. My mother does not pity me,  
She is giving me away young in marriage.  
2. Young and small,  
To a foreign place.  
3. There I have no family,  
There I do not know the people.

1. Light the candle, mother,
Place it on the table,
Let me see,
If there is a match for me.
2. The mother lit the candle,
It burns brightly,
With whom I wanted, with them I sat,
My heart does not hurt.
Var.:
The mother lit the candle,
It burns dimly,
With whom I did not want, with them I sat,
My heart hurts.

1. – You are giving me away, mother,  
Far beyond the forest,  
Tie a nightingale for me  
To the rear wheels.  
2. So that it sings to me early  
In the morning,  
So that it wakes me, young one,  
Early.  
3. – Oh, you have, my daughter,  
Your mother-in-law,  
Let her wake you  
Just like I do.  
4. Oh, whether the mother-in-law manages to wake you  
Or not,  
She will go around to the neighbors  
And talk:  
5. – Oh, my daughter-in-law is sleepy and drowsy,  
Oh, she won't get up early  
Like I do.  
6. If I didn’t wake her,  
She wouldn’t get up,  
If I didn’t stir her,  
She wouldn’t eat.

1. Oh mother, my mother,  
How you thought of marrying me off.  
2. How you thought of marrying me off,  
You painted me inside the house.  
3. In the hallway, on the doors,  
In the light on the walls.  
4. You looked at the drawing,  
You remembered your daughter.  
5. Oh, somewhere our little child is,  
With a strange father.  
6. Oh, somewhere she works hard,  
Pleases no one.  
7. But here with us she has settled,  
And pleased us all.  
8. She was reaping rye in the field,  
Spinning flax in the light.  
9. She was reaping rye, singing,  
Spinning flax, dozing.

1. Let's sit down, mother, and have dinner,  
After dinner, let's have a talk.  
2. What is mine, give to me,  
What is yours, keep for yourselves.  
3. After dinner, let's make peace,  
And share what is fair.  
4. My chest with towels,  
Your basket with spindles.  
5. My chest also has a popovnytsia (priest's robe),  
Your basket will still fall into ruin.

1. Let's sit down, mother, let's have dinner,  
After dinner, let's share. Hoo!  
2. After dinner, let's share,  
Let's part on good terms. Hoo!  
3. For you, mother, borscht and dumplings,  
For me, mother, a chest and pillows. Hoo!  
4. For you, mother, a little cow,  
For me, mother, a little box. Hoo!

Scatter, sorrels,  
Say goodbye, sisters,  
It would have been better for us with you,  
As with a native sister.

1. Comrade, let's part ways,  
Let's divide the ribbons.  
2. You get the ribbon and also the wreath,  
I get the jacket and also the [ochipochok].

1. – Farewell, farewell, comrade,  
We are already leaving,  
We will not find a comrade  
Like you.  
2. Even if we find a comrade,  
Not like you,  
Who will tell the sincere truth  
Hey, and not that one.  
3. Who will tell the sincere truth,  
Half of it,  
Wait, wait, comrade,  
Hey, at least for an hour.  
4. – I won’t wait, comrade,  
I won’t wait,  
Because I have no conversation  
With you.

1. – Farewell, farewell, comrade,  
We are already leaving,  
We will not find a comrade  
Like you.  
2. Though we find a comrade,  
Not like you,  
Who would tell sincere truth  
Hey, but not that one.  
3. Who would tell sincere truth,  
Half of it,  
Wait, wait, comrade,  
Hey, at least for an hour.  
4. – I won’t wait, comrade,  
I won’t wait,  
Because I have no conversation  
With you.

1. – Oh, I will sow rye  
Among the sedges,  
Will you not feel sorrow,  
Mother, for me?*  
2. – Yes, daughter, I will feel sorrow,  
My heart withers,  
And who, daughter,  
Will take care of me in old age?  
3. – I leave for you, mother,  
Three balls inside the house,  
Why is it so sad  
In the house without me?  
4. Oh, play, musicians,  
To my rue,  
Be healthy,  
All the people there.  
5. Oh, play, musicians,  
Beat the drums,  
Oh, I am leaving  
From my dear mother.  
6. Oh, play, musicians,  
Take away the things,  
For I am leaving  
From my mother to my mother-in-law.

1. Oh, flowers in the garden,  
Bow down, children.  
2. To father and mother  
And to the whole family.  
3. There are cornflowers in the garden,  
And Han's harvest festival.  
4. Thank God, she lived,  
Married off all the children.  
5. May God grant to live long enough  
To see the grandson married off.  
6. May God grant to live long enough  
To see the great-grandson arrive.

1. – Hey, get ready,  
My love,  
Your crying won't help at all.  
The horses are standing at the threshold,  
And your tears won’t help,  
My dear.  

2. – Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my father.  
Oh, thank you, father,  
For the smooth life I had with you,  
But now I won’t.  

3. Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my mother.  
Oh, thank you, mother,  
For the good life I had,  
But now I won’t.  

4. Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my brother.  
Oh, thank you, brother,  
For bringing the boys to the house,  
But now I won’t.  

5. Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my sisters.  
Oh, thank you, sisters,  
For going to the evening gatherings,  
But now they won’t.  

6. Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my thresholds.  
Oh, thank you, thresholds,  
For the boys stubbing their feet,  
But now they won’t.  

7. Wait, wait, my dear,  
At least a day and a half,  
I haven’t yet thanked my gates.  
Oh, thank you, gates,  
For standing at the boys’ mouths,  
But now they won’t.

We fired, brother, from the shotgun,  
Show the way to sister:  
Here is the road for you, sister,  
Go to mother-in-law in good health.

Filename: Vinnytsia_524_Shche zh bo ya ne yikhala
translation:
1. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked you, mother.  
Thank you, dear mother,  
For striving for me,  
You will not anymore, you will not.  

2. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked you, father.  
Thank you, father,  
For building me a house,  
You will not anymore, you will not.  

3. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked you, brother.  
Thank you, brother,  
For leading the boys into the house,  
You will not anymore, you will not.  

4. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked you, sister.  
Thank you, sister,  
For teaching me to sew and spin,  
You will not anymore, you will not.  

5. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked the gates.  
Thank you, gates,  
For standing against the boys’ company,  
You will not anymore, you will not.  

6. I have not yet gone away,  
I have not yet walked away,  
I have not yet thanked the thresholds.  
Thank you, thresholds,  
For knocking the boys’ feet,  
You will not anymore, you will not.

1. – The pike fish is swimming, swimming
Quietly on the water,
Come, come, my dear father,
Come to me now.
2. – I will not come, my daughter,
To visit you,
They have piled raw earth
On my chest.
3. They have piled raw earth,
So that I cannot rise,
They have covered my dark eyes,
So that I cannot look.
4. They have closed my dark eyes,
For all nights,
May God help you,
My daughter.

1. They poured raw earth  
On my chest,  
Dark brown eyes closed,  
Oh, and my lips too.  
2. Dark brown eyes closed  
For all the nights,  
Let the whole family  
Come to help.  
3. You have, my daughter,  
A relative aunt,  
Let her help you,  
Oh, like a mother.  
4. You have, my daughter,  
A dear uncle,  
Let him help you,  
Oh, like your father.

Filename: Vinnytsia_527_Oi shcho zh tse za selo
translation:
Oh, what kind of village is this,
Where is it so joyful?
All around it is very green,
Inside it is very cheerful.
All around it is blooming viburnum,
Inside is the whole family.

Woodland, father, forest,
We are leading the bride,
And the little forest is very green,
The bride is very young.
On this note:
We did not think about this here,
The horses came by themselves,
Along the path, the road
After our young bride.
       *       *       *
And at our matchmaker’s
A straw house,
[Do not be afraid] to enter,
So as not to break it down.
       *       *       *
Horned moon,
Do not know the way,
Poor our little head,
Where will we spend the night?
Where will we spend the night,
What will we have for supper?
Our mother did not light a fire,
Did not cook supper.

1. Oh, yesterday evening  
In the evening  
Our goose flew away  
With our gander.  
2. But do not beat her,  
Do not scold her,  
But let her go to her mother.  
3. And do not growl at her,  
If she does not know how to do something,  
Then teach her.

1. Our mother is tall,  
Our mother is broad,  
|: Our mother became happy,  
Because she waited for her daughters-in-law. :|  
2. Your mother is small,  
Your mother is short,  
|: Your mother bent down,  
Because she lost her little daughters. :|

Oh, we were at the mother-in-law’s,  
Eating, drinking like little pillows,  
We ate, drank, and toasted,  
They welcomed us nicely.  
Oh, we were at mom’s,  
They didn’t scold us,  
We had just sat down,  
And already finished eating.  
We were at the groom’s,  
We didn’t eat anything,  
Didn’t eat, didn’t drink,  
Just wandered around foolishly.

On Monday morning  
It hurts me, Mom,  
Because on Sunday late  
Something got on me.  
And your body  
Seemed to tremble like hell.

Matchmaker Monday,  
You brought us a maiden,  
You tousled her braids,  
You scratched her eyebrows.

What will we do tomorrow?  
We will not drink vodka,  
I will put grandpa on grandma,  
And grandpa will make children,  
And we will drink vodka.

The matchmaker was walking through the garden,  
Pinched the matchmaker's wife from behind,  
And the matchmaker's wife is purring,  
Still wants it from the front too.

1. Oh my God, God,  
How radiant You are,  
How much I want to live,  
But death takes me  
And I must die,  
Lie in the damp earth.  

2. Oh my God, God,  
From the great sky,  
Take my sinful soul,  
Take my soul  
To Yourself in heaven,  
But the body to the damp earth.  

3. Build me  
A high grave mound,  
It will be overgrown with grass,  
Plant on me  
A red viburnum,  
In spring it will bloom.  

4. Then birds will come  
To visit me,  
They will chirp to me,  
But I will not hear,  
Because I will be lying  
In the deep damp earth.  

5. A deep grave,  
A small hut,  
And it has no windows,  
And I in that hut  
Lie alone,  
There is no one with me.  

6. Once my little feet  
Walked the world,  
Oh God, what a time that was,  
Once my little hands  
Worked so hard,  
Now they lie on my chest.  

7. Farewell, relatives,  
Dear friends,  
I will not come to you anymore,  
See me off  
To the eternal home,  
There I will lie forever.

1. Oh my God, God,  
How radiant You are,  
How I want to live,  
But death takes me away  
And I must die,  
Lie in the damp earth.  

2. Oh my God, God,  
From the great sky,  
Take my sinful soul,  
Take my soul  
To Yourself in heaven,  
And my body to the damp earth.  

3. Heap a high grave for me,  
It will grow over with grass,  
Plant red viburnum on me,  
It will bloom in spring.  

4. Then birds will come to me,  
They will chirp to me,  
But I will not hear,  
Because I will be lying  
In the deep damp earth.  

5. The grave is deep,  
A small hut  
Without any windows,  
And I in that hut  
Lie alone,  
No one is with me.  

6. Once my feet  
Walked the world,  
Oh God, what a time that was,  
Once my hands  
Worked so hard,  
Now they lie on my chest.  

7. Farewell, relatives,  
Dear friends,  
I will not come to you anymore,  
See me off  
To the eternal home,  
There I will lie forever.

1. Holy God, holy, mighty,  
I am about to die,  
But I ask my whole family  
To come and say goodbye.*  
2. My family came, came,  
Stood by me,  
Unexpected death, sent by God,  
Did not allow me to say goodbye.  
3. Make me a coffin,  
And lay me to rest,  
And pray to God  
For my sinful soul.  
4. They are taking me to the grave,  
The wind is blowing,  
My whole family follows me  
And faints from sorrow.  
5. When they brought me to the grave,  
They said to open it,  
As I looked upon God’s world,  
How we used to live here.  
6. As they closed my coffin  
And lowered me into the pit,  
Oh, open it, oh open it,  
Let me look once more.  
7. Oh, open my coffin,  
Look once again,  
My home, wealth, my children,  
Now share them.  
8. I have silver and gold,  
I do not need it,  
Four boards, a plot of land,  
And salvation from heaven.  
9. Cover my grave  
And plant a pear tree,  
You will all go home,  
I must lie here.  
10. You will all go home,  
I will lie here,  
Maybe my children will come  
To visit me.  
11. My family came,  
Stood all around me,  
With tiny tears  
They embraced the grave.

1. Farewell, soul, for the last time,  
Farewell [forever] today,  
For I am going on a long journey,  
Leaving sorrow to my family.  

2. I leave behind my footprints,  
Which I have walked,  
I leave behind my words,  
Which I have spoken.  

3. The footprints will be overgrown with grass,  
No longer visible,  
I leave my words to you,  
I will remember them.  

4. My time has come  
To set off on the road,  
And I have gone on a long journey,  
I have given my soul to God.  

5. And that path is so hard,  
It spares no one,  
All peoples walk it,  
They do not return.  

6. The body will be laid in the grave,  
The soul will go to heaven,  
They take no riches with them,  
Because they are not needed there.  

7. Forgive me, my family,  
Do not cry, do not sob,  
I will not come to visit you,  
Do not wait for me anymore.  

8. And you, my friends, my family,  
Work for me  
And pray to God  
For the repose of my soul.  

9. Never pass by my grave,  
My friends,  
And for the repose of my soul,  
Give a piece of bread.

1. I will come to the grave, kneel down,  
Bow my head to the cross,  
Oh, why don’t you advise me,  
Mother, how will I live without you.  

2. I will plant periwinkle around the grave,  
It will bloom blue for you,  
Bringing sorrow to my heart,  
Oh God, how I mourn for you.  

3. The blood in your chest has already congealed,  
The heart has stopped beating,  
Now, mother, you are indifferent  
To my groan, lament, and tears.  

4. Wake up for a moment, dear one,  
Say at least a word to me,  
Wipe my tears with your hand,  
Hold me close to your heart.  

5. My dear mother,  
Why do you lie so peacefully,  
And my crying does not disturb you,  
As it did once in childhood.  

6. You lie peacefully, mother,  
And I stand over you,  
A prayer and a moment of silence  
Disturbed my soul.  

7. I will pray for you  
And ask God  
To take your soul to heaven,  
And your body to the moist earth.  

8. You lie peacefully, mother,  
My crying does not disturb you,  
In the spring, periwinkle will bloom  
Blue for you.

Filename: Vinnytsia_540_Sydzhu ya tak hirko i plachu
translation:
1. I sit here bitterly and cry:  
– My mother, forgive me,  
Open your eyes, dear mother,  
Look at me at least once.  

2. You gave birth to me, mother,  
You shed your blood,  
You prayed to God for happiness,  
So that I would be happy.  

3. Mother rocked me,  
Mother bathed me,  
Mother slept little at night,  
She cared for and raised me.  

4. Taking the first steps  
Mother helped me,  
The first word "oh mom,"  
I already said it.  

5. Many times, mother, you grieved,  
When I was ill,  
You quietly washed your tears,  
And called upon God for help.  

6. You took me to church,  
Taught me to pray to Christ,  
And to love His mother,  
The immaculate, holy Virgin.  

7. Thank you, dear mother,  
For your kindness and your sufferings,  
For teaching me how to live,  
And helping me in trouble.  

8. Maybe I have offended you,  
Many things happened in life,  
I beg you greatly, mother,  
Forgive me for everything, dear.  

9. I remember, dear mother,  
You saw me off from home  
On a long, distant road of life,  
And tears streamed from your eyes.  

10. Forever, dear mother,  
You are leaving the native home,  
We are laying you to rest,  
You will not come to us anymore.  

11. Your eyes are already closed,  
Your lips, mother, are silent,  
Your heart no longer beats,  
Your hands lie on your chest.  

12. You had great joy,  
You had great sorrow,  
I always hurried to you,  
Now to whom shall I come?  

13. My dear mother,  
How heavy this moment is,  
How hard it is to part with you,  
This is God's will.

1. Oh, on the hill, on the hill,  
There the bells were ringing,  
There the poor widow  
Went to pray.  

2. She prayed, she prayed  
Before the icons,  
The widow washed herself  
With small tears.  

3. Angels flew  
From the high sky,  
They took the widow’s soul,  
Because it was needed there.  

4. The poor widow lay  
Two days and three nights,  
The earth pressed  
On the widow’s eyes.  

5. The rich gathered,  
Started to speak:  
– We will not  
Bury this widow.  

6. Because she neither ate nor drank with us,  
Because she went to church  
To pray.  

7. When the holy ones gathered,  
They began to say:  
– We must bury this widow  
Ourselves.  

8. When they started  
To carry the widow out of the house,  
All the bells  
Started ringing in the church.  

9. They brought her to the church,  
Opened the gates,  
When they carried her into the church,  
They lit the candles.  

10. They brought her to the cemetery,  
The earth parted,  
They brought her to the grave,  
She bowed to everyone.  

11. When they started  
To lower the widow into the grave,  
The angels began  
To sing mournfully.  

12. The rich gathered  
And began to cry:  
– Forgive us, widow,  
Like a dear mother.  

13. Forgive us, widow,  
Like a dear mother,  
Because we poor  
Did not want to bury you.

1. We dance the crooked dance,
We won’t find its end.
Chorus:
Sometimes uphill, sometimes downhill,
Sometimes at the rowan, sometimes at the viburnum.
2. Wild goats were jumping
Under the priest’s vines,
Chorus.
3. And after them, the kids
Broke their little fingers.
Chorus.

1. Oh, on the water,  
On the Ardan.  
Generous evening,  
On the holy evening.  

2. Ardan is pouring water,  

3. The Mother of God  
Is bathing her Son.  

4. As she bathed Him  
She also swaddled Him,  

5. And placed Him  
On the little table.  

6. Three angels  
Flew in,  

7. They began to wonder  
How to give Him a cradle.  

8. They gave Him  
Matchmaker Pavlo.  

9. The Mother of God  
Did not like it,  

10. And did not place Him  
On the little table.  

11. Three angels  
Flew in,  

12. They began to wonder  
How to give Him a cradle.  

13. They gave Him  
Matchmaker Petryk.  

14. The Mother of God  
Did not like it,  

15. And did not place Him  
On the little table.  

16. Three angels  
Flew in,  

17. They began to wonder  
How to give Him a cradle.  

18. They gave Him  
Jesus Christ.  

19. The Mother of God  
Did like it,  

20. And placed Him  
On the little table.

Christ is born,

Angels are flying,

1. Mister Host,  
And your yard is sharp.  
Hey, God grant! *  
* This refrain is performed after each verse of the song. (L. Ye.)  
2. And your yard is sharp,  
There the gathering was held.  
3. There the gathering was held, –  
I am all the matchmakers.  
4. I am all the matchmakers  
And we sat on the bench.  
5. Saint Nicholas  
Finally sat at the table.  
6. Hey, he leaned on the sword,  
Bowed his head,  
7. [Bowed his head,]  
Shed a little tear.

The Danube flowed.  
9. And in that Danube  
The Lord also bathed  
10. And with Saint Peter.  
Hey, while bathing,  
11. They argued  
About heaven and earth.  
12. And Peter says,  
That heaven is bigger.  
13. And the Lord says,  
That the earth is bigger.  
14. – Oh Peter, Peter,  
Let’s not argue,  
15. Let’s send down from heaven  
Two or three angels,  
16. To measure  
Heaven and earth.  
17. Hey, when they measured,  
At that time they debated.  
18. They found  
The Lord’s truth,  
19. [The Lord’s truth]  
And Peter’s wrong.  
20. Heaven is small,  
Because everything is equal.  
21. [Because everything is equal]  
[But] the earth is bigger.  
22. Mountains and valleys,

The whole Christian world.  
24. And with this word  
Be healthy,  
25. Mister Host  
Not by yourself,  
26. But with children, with wife,

27. May God grant you

28. Happiness to the yard,

To the household.  
30. To the livestock

32. And with this  
word  
Be healthy,

1. Oh, on the hill

Generous evening,  
On the matchmaker's evening.*

2. Behind them walks

3. Walk, walk,

Brass,

Golden.

Opened,  
All the matchmakers  
Bowed.  
11. Matchmaker Pavlo,

Of hell.  
12. [Take the keys  
Of hell,]  
Release the souls  
Saved.  
13. Only do not release those  
Who on Friday  
Sang songs,

Did not wash early,  
And on Sunday  
Had breakfast early.

1. Master of the house,
I am in your yard.
Rejoice, earth, rejoice,

Is the Holy Nativity,
5. On the second throne,

6. On the third throne,

1. Malanochka—  
Petrivochka,  
Malanochka  
Didn’t get enough sleep.*  
*The second half of each stanza is sung twice. (L. Ye.)  
2. Our Malanka  
Nistrianyanochka,  
Floated down the Nistru,  
Washed her feet.  
3. Floated down the Nistru,  
Washed her feet,  
Soaked her thin apron.  
4. – Blow, wind,  
Here and there,

People are watching.  
5. Blow, wind,  
Wild one,  
Dry the thin apron.  
6. Our Malanka  
Is a hostess, –  
Seven times a day  
She washed the pots,  
7. Put them away  
Under the bench,  
They grew  
Into an anthill.  
8. Put them  
On the shelf,  
They grew  
Into a little cave.  
9. Oh, in Kamianets  
At the market,  
Let Malanochka  
Run around the house,  
10. Let her  
Have fun,  
Like a pike-fish  
On the Danube.  
11. Like a pike-fish  
With the puffers,  
Our Malanya  
With the Cossacks.

1. Hey, hey, should I plow  
Near your house?  
2. Hey, hey, not plow

1. Shchedryk, shchedry[k],  
Shchedrivочка,  
2. A little swallow flew in,  
3. Sat herself down  
To chirp,  
4. To call the master:  
5. – Come out, come out,  
Master,  
6. Look at the sheepfold:

8. And the lambs were born.  
9. Your stock  
Is all good,  
10. You will have

11. If not money,  
Then grain,  
12. You have a wife  
With black eyebrows.  
13. In your house,  
Like in a wreath,  
14. A towel hangs  
On a little peg.  
15. Kiss  
Christ’s feet,  
16. Bring us  
Some pies.

49. Generous evening (OK-ЩК-05a)  
1. Generous evening,  
On the holy evening.  
2. I sit high,  
I see far.  
3. In the clear field  
A plow is plowing.  
4. The beam with oxen  
With golden horns.  
5. Behind that plow  
The Lord Himself walks,  
6. Saint Peter  
Drives the oxen,  
7. The Pure Virgin  
Brings food.  
8. Brings food,  
Asks God:  
9. – Let it grow, God,  
Spring wheat,  
10. Spring wheat  
For prosphora.  
11. Let it grow, God,  
Spring buckwheat,

For little pies.

1. Oh, beyond the gates,
On the anthill
There they play...

4. – Oh, my daughter,

8. – Oh, my daughter,

1. Hey, God!  
I am in the garden, in the garden,  
I am in the vineyard.  

2. Hey, God!  
There gathered  
A rye maiden.  

3. Hey, God!  
When dawn rose,  
She went to church.  

4. Hey, God!  
She is met by  
Two or three kings.  

5. Hey, God!  
They ask her:  
– Whose daughter are you?  

6. Hey, God!  
Are you a princess,  
Or a queen?  

7. Hey, God!  
– I am neither a princess,  
Nor a queen.  

8. Hey, God!  
I am my mother’s daughter,  
My own young lady.  

9. Hey, God!  
We crown you  
With a Christmas carol.  

10. Hey, God!  
With a Christmas carol,  
With father, mother!  

11. Hey, God!  
With happiness  
And also with health!  

12. Hey, God!  
With a green crown,

1. On the steep mountain
Peacocks walk,
God grant,
Peacocks walk.*
2. Peacocks walk,

9. They began to ask,
To find out:
10. – Are you a priest’s daughter,

5. She took from the apron,

1. The father had  
One daughter, oh,  
The father had  
One daughter, oh!

2. Three matchmakers came to her.

3. One matchmaker  
By the gates,

4. The second matchmaker  
In the cherry orchard,
