On a horse-driven cart.

6. – Oh mother, mother,  
What should I give them?

7. What should I give them  
By the gates?

8. – Oh, give, give  
White towels.

9. White towels,  
With silk edges.

10. – Oh mother, mother,  
What should I give them?

11. What should I give them  
In the cherry orchard?

12. – Oh, give, give  
White rolls.

13. White rolls  
Made of spring wheat.

14. – Oh mother, mother,  
What should I give them?

15. What should I give them,  
Those on the horse-driven cart?

16. – Oh, give, give  
Buckwheat honey.

17. Buckwheat honey,  
Honey from lady Hanusia.

*

1. The moon rose,  
Crossed the sky, oh,  
The moon rose,  
Crossed the sky, oh!

2. And it entered  
The new covenants.

3. The sinful maiden  
Was sewing a shirt.

4. Sewing a shirt,  
She fell sound asleep.

5. Three matchmakers came to her.

6. She tied a wreath  
Out of white snow,

7. And when she threw it at him,  
He was terrified.

(They knew no end.)

1. Come on, come on, girls,  
To braid Shum's hair,  
And our Shum  
Has a green coat.  

2. [Cucumbers  
So green,]  
Our girls  
So young.  

3. Cucumbers are turning yellow,  
Make an effort, lads.  

4. Make an effort, lads  
To court the girls.  

5. To court the girls,  
For good meetings.  

6. Come on, come on, girls,  
To unbraid Shum's hair,  
And our Shum  
Has a green coat.  

7. Cucumbers  
So green,  
Our girls  
So young.  

8. And Shum walks  
Behind the garden,  
And Shumykha  
Is fishing.  

9. What she caught,  
She drank away,  
She didn’t provide  
The girl with a coat.  

10. – Wait, my daughter,  
Until Tuesday,  
There will be a coat  
And lacing too.  

11. But Tuesday  
Passes by,  
The girl has no coat.  

12. – Wait, my daughter,  
Until Saturday,  
There will be a coat  
And boots too.  

13. But Saturday  
Passes by,  
The girl still  
Does not have a coat.  

14. – Wait, my daughter,  
Until Sunday,  
There will be a coat  
And hope too.  

15. But Sunday  
Comes,  
The girl of Shum  
Walks barefoot.  

16. And after her...

Filename: Vinnytsia_527v_Oi shcho zh tse za selo
translation:
Oh, what kind of village is this,
That is so merry?
All around it is green,
Inside it is joyful.
All around it is blooming viburnum,
Inside is the whole family.

1. By the Jordan River  
The water stood still and quiet,  
There the Most Pure  
Was bathing her Son.  

2. And having bathed Him,  
She swaddled Him on the throne,  
And having swaddled Him,  
She laid Him  
In a golden manger.  

3. Somewhere appeared  
By that Child  
Three oxen,  
All three oxen  
Breathed the Spirit of God.  

4. All three of them  
Warmed that Child,  
And by sunrise  
Three kings appeared.  

5. All three of them  
Served at that Child’s service:  
One king  
With myrrh and incense  
Calmed Him,  

6. The second king  
Called Him Christ with frankincense,  
And the third king  
Gave Him a golden flower.  

7. But it is not a flower,  
But the holy Nativity,  
Oh, so that  
The whole world would be joyful.  

8. Rejoice,  
Master of the house,  
In your home,  
Like angels  
In the heavens  
In God’s paradise.

1. On the Jordan River  
The water stood still,  
There the Most Pure  
Was bathing Her Son.  

2. And having bathed Him,  
She wrapped Him on the throne,  
And having wrapped Him,  
She laid Him  
In a golden manger.  

3. Somewhere there appeared  
Over that Child  
Three oxen,  
All three oxen  
Breathed with the Spirit of God.  

4. All three of them  
Warmed the Child,  
And before sunrise  
Three kings appeared somewhere.  

5. All three of them  
Served the Child:  
One king  
With myrrh, incense,  
Pacified Him with peace,  

6. And the second king  
Called Him Christ with a cup,  
Slowly,  
And the third king  
Gave Him a golden flower.  

7. It is not a flower,  
But the holy Nativity,  
Oh, so that  
All the world would be joyful.  

8. Rejoice,  
Master of the house,  
In your home,  
Like angels  
In the heavens  
In God's paradise.

1. Oh, beyond the mountain,  
Beyond the rocky one,  
There stands a maple  
Thin, curly.  
2. On that maple  
Two eagles sit, –  
Two eagles sit,  
They see far.  
3. They see far, –  
To the blue sea,  
On the blue sea  
A ship is sailing.  
4. On that ship  
A handsome young man,  
A handsome young man –  
[Name] boy.  
5. He aims his bow,  
Is about to shoot.  
– Don’t shoot at us,  
For we are not eagles.  
6. For we are not eagles,  
We are angels,  
The Lord sent us  
From heaven to earth.  
7. And on this earth  
There is no king,  
There is no king,  
So there is no good.

1. Oh, beyond the mountain,  
Beyond the rocky one,  
There stands a maple tree  
Thin, curly.  
2. On that maple tree  
Two eagles sit, –  
Two eagles sit,  
They see far.  
3. They see far, –  
To the blue sea,  
On the blue sea  
A ship is sailing.  
4. On that ship  
A strong young man,  
A strong young man –  
[Name] boy.  
5. He aims his bow,  
He must shoot.  
– Don’t shoot at us,  
Because we are not eagles.  
6. Because we are not eagles,  
We are angels,  
The Lord sent us  
From heaven to earth.  
7. And on this earth  
There is no king,  
There is no king,  
So there is no good.

1. Luli, luli, lulilechky,  
Silk garlands,  
2. A lady’s cradle,  
And a noble child.

1. Luli, luli, luli,  
To other children, nonsense,  
2. But to Alyoshka, little pears,  
So that the girls would love him.

1. Ah-ah, two cats,
Both gray and white,
2. The third one is haired,
They are asking to come inside
To rock the child.
3. The cat did not listen
And went to the field.
4. And sat on the viburnum,
And the viburnum is breaking.
5. The little cat fell and lies,
His kitty runs to him.
6. – Meow, meow, kitty,
Run for some water.
7. The kitty went for water
And fell into the well.
8. The little cat went to rescue,
To save his kitty.
9. He grabbed her by the paw
And threw her into the column.
10. – Hush, cheerful kitty,
We will go to the villages,
And we will take Vasylka.
11. Vasyl will play the pipe,
And we will dance.

1. The grandmother rocked the grandfather  
From evening until noon:  
2. – Oh sleep, grandpa, until evening,  
I will cook a duck and a drake.

1. Lulyu, lulyu, lulyu,  
The woman went to get some onions,  
2. And Alyoshka went for parsley,  
To cook soup for the children.

1. Sway, sway,  
Where the mare is, there is a foal.  
2. And the mare is in the forest,  
And the foal is in distress.

1. Tosi, Tosi,  
Let's go to Zosia,  
2. And from Zosia to Orysia,  
Where the horses are pale..

1. Forge, forge the leg,  
We will go on a journey,  
2. The road is crooked,  
The little mare is blind.

1. I bake, I bake bread  
For the children’s lunch,  
2. Smaller for the smaller one,  
Bigger for the bigger one.  
3. And for the young masters, rolls,  
So they can sit by the stove.  
Shoo into the oven!

1. Kish-mish, sparrow,  
Don't peck at our hemp,  
2. Because there is a little hemp,  
For the children's stockings.

1. The duck was flying,  
Wagging her tail,  
2. And behind her, the drake  
Grabbed the duck by her tuft.  
3. And behind her, the drake said:  
"Come on, duck, let's dance!"

1. Come out, come out, little sun,  
To grandpa’s little field,  
2. To grandma’s little herbs,  
To our little yard,  
3. To the spring flowers,  
To the little children,  
4. Here they are playing,  
Waiting for you!

1. Go, go, little rain,  
I will cook you some borscht.  
2. Porridge for me, borscht for you,  
So that a strong rain comes.

1. – Caroling, caroling, caroler,  
A good round loaf with honey,  
2. But without honey, not the same,  
Give me, uncle, a five-kopeck coin,  
3. And you, aunt, money—  
A good caroler.  
Good evening!

1. Carol, carol, carolers,  
And I am my father's only one.  
2. My father sent me  
To collect money.  
3. But I did not find any money  
And came to you instead.  
Good evening!

1. Shchedryk-bedryk,  
Give a dumpling,  
2. A portion of porridge,  
A ring of sausage.  
Good evening!

1. – Our spring is beautiful,  
What have you brought us?  
An apple, or some sorrel,  
Or a glove for the hand?  
2. – Neither an apple, nor sorrel,  
Nor a glove for the hand.  
To the men, a little plow,  
To the boys, a little whip.  
3. To the young women, a sickle,  
To the girls, a wreath,  
To the old grandfathers, a cane,  
To the old grandmothers, a small hoop.  
4. To the old grandmothers, a mushroom basket,  
To the little children, a dumpling.  
To the old grandmothers, spinning flax,  
To the little children, herding goslings!

1. A little bug walks on the twig,  
And a little bug climbs the ladder.  
Play, little bug, play!  
2. I have a little bug  
With little white hands,  
3. On the little white hand  
A golden ring.

1. I cannot lead the crooked dance  
And cannot find the end.  
2. It must be led  
Like weaving a wreath.  
3. Oh my wreath, wreath,  
Unfortunate periwinkle.  
4. I wove you still  
Yesterday evening.  
5. And I hung you  
In the hawthorn, on the tree.  
6. In the hawthorn, on the tree,  
On a golden peg.  
7. On a golden peg,  
On a silk cord.  
8. And mother took you  
And gave you to the unloved one.  
9. Oh, if only I had known,  
I would have taken him  
10. And torn him apart in two,  
And trampled him underfoot,  
11. With yellow boots,  
With golden horseshoes.  
12. Oh my wreath, wreath,  
Unfortunate periwinkle.  
13. I wove you still  
Yesterday evening.  
14. And I hung you  
In the hawthorn, on the tree.  
15. In the hawthorn, on the tree,  
On a golden peg.  
16. On a golden peg,  
On a silk cord.  
17. And mother took you  
And gave you to my beloved.

1. Oh, don’t grow, dill,  
Tall in the garden.  
2. Oh, don’t walk, old man,  
Near my house.  
3. Oh, don’t trample, old man,  
The curly mint.  
4. I never loved that old man  
By birth.  
5. Along his footprints  
I rolled a stone.  
6. Oh, how hard and heavy  
It is for a stone to roll,  
7. But even harder and heavier  
For an old man to marry.  
8. Oh, grow, dill,  
Tall in the garden.  
9. Oh, come, Cossack,  
Oh, come, young man,  
Near my house.  
10. Oh, trample, Cossack,  
Oh, trample, young man,  
The curly mint.  
11. I loved that young man  
By birth.  
12. Along his footprints  
I rolled a wreath.  
13. Oh, how easy and light  
It is for a wreath to roll,  
14. But even easier and lighter  
For a young man to marry.

1. Oh, on the hill, two little oaks  
Bent close together.  
Cut, chop the wood,  
Wherever you look – green,  
Two little oaks.  
2. There stood young men,  
Talking about girls.  
3. And Ivanko for his own,  
For young Halyna.

1. Hey, by the meadow and the shore  
People ride with a plow,  
Friendly songs are flowing,  
Everyone rejoices in spring.*  
2. The earth is already steaming,  
Cheering the sower.  
Gardens are blooming, –  
There will be abundant fruits.  
3. The fields are turning green,  
The earth has awakened,  
Came to life, blossomed  
From village to village.

1. I will sow rye,  
I will set a guard,  
I will set a guard –  
For my dear father.  

2. The rains come from the side,  
Everything from the side  
Beyond the small rye field  
And full.  

3. The guard is not sincere –  
The rye is nibbled.  
I will set a guard –  
For my dear mother.  

4. The guard is not sincere –  
The rye is nibbled.  
I will set a guard –  
For my beloved.  

5. Oh, a sincere guard –  
The rye is not nibbled.  
The rye does not fear  
Neither sun nor frost.

1. Oh, on the city linden trees, linden trees,  
So that our boys' bellies swell,  
2. Oh, let them swell, let them know,  
Let them not break the Kupalo.  
3. Because our Kupalo is blessed,  
And our boys are fierce.  
4. Our Kupalo is up to the stars,  
All the girls were gathering it.  
5. Our Kupalo with branches,  
And our boys with ailments.  
6. Our Kupalo up to the moon,  
Let our boys hang themselves.

1. Oh, in the garden is linden, linden,  
So that those boys’ stomachs may swell,  
2. Oh, let them swell, let them know,  
Let them not break the Kupala bushes.

1. Oh, the sun is rising behind the forest,  
There is playing,  
There Ivanko is saddling  
His little horse.  
2. Then his father comes to him  
And quietly asks his son:  
3. – Where, where, my son,  
Do you think  
You are saddling your black horse?  
4. – Oh, I will go, my father,  
On a journey,  
And I will take a gift –  
A wreath!  
5. Halya is preparing a gift for me,  
Embroidering a little scarf  
With silver and gold.

№ 25  
1. Oh, the sun is in the pine forest,  
In the pine forest,  
There Ivanko is building  
A barn.  
2. – Build, build, Ivanko,  
High,  
So that it can be seen  
Far away!  
3. Oh, somewhere my Halunya  
Is walking,  
Waving silk ribbons.  
4. Waving silk ribbons,  
Shining with golden rings.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,
There the little cuckoo was bathing.
2. She came out to the shore and was drying herself,
The girl stood and was sorrowful:
3. – I haven’t even spun the towel yet,
And I already liked a boy.
4. I haven’t even bleached the towel yet,
And I have already fallen in love with a boy.
5. The towel is still on the loom,
And the elders were already in the house.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There the little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. She came out to the shore and dried herself,  
The girl stood there and was sorrowful:  
3. – I have not yet woven the towel,  
But I have already liked a boy.  
4. I have not yet bleached the towel,  
But I have already fallen in love with the boy.  
5. The towel is still on the loom,  
But the matchmakers were already in the house.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There a little swallow was bathing.  
2. There the little swallow was bathing,  
On the little shore it was drying itself.  
3. On the little shore it was drying itself,  
The girl Hanusya was sorrowful,  
4. Because she hadn't finished washing the towels yet,  
But Ivan already liked her.  
5. Because the towels were still on the loom,  
And Ivan was already sitting in the house.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day,  
There the little cuckoo was bathing.  
2. On the little shore, she was drying herself.  
The girl Oksanka was sorrowful,  
3. Because I haven’t yet washed the handkerchief,  
And Nikolai has already taken a liking to me.  
4. And already the bushes are on the loom,  
And Nikolai is already in her house.

Filename: Kmeln_270_Oi tam u luzi, pry luzhechku
translation:
1. Oh, there in the meadow, by the little meadow,
There Ivan is mowing buckwheat.
2. There Ivan is mowing buckwheat,
And behind his belt, he carries cakes.
3. And there Halyunya was washing a scarf,
She stole Ivan’s cake.
4. – Oh you, Halyunya, oh you, girl,
Return my cake, thief.
5. Return the cake, why did you steal it?
May you never see the evening!
6. May you never see the evening,
May you never wash your scarves!
№ 13  
1. Oh wind, wind is blowing,
It opens the little gate.
2. And I thought it was just the wind blowing,
But it was the mother-in-law returning from the market:
3. – Open the gate, daughter-in-law,
Let my daughter rest.
4. The daughter-in-law opens the gate,
Cursing her own fate.
№ 14  
1. It was a young night on Ivan’s day,
Where did you spend the night, Halyunya?
2. – I spent the night in Horodyshche,
And for dinner I had horseradish in borscht.
3. Because there the girls are wealthy,
They make embroidered towels,
4. And decorated headbands,
Because they are the worst themselves.

1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
A girl went out beautifully dressed.  
2. People are looking at her,  
But who is courting her?  
3. There is nothing to be surprised about—  
Neither father nor mother cares.  
4. Neither father nor mother cares,  
Only Ivan, who is supposed to marry her.

1. – The little star was shining, shining,  
Oh, with whom were you standing, Halyunya?  
2. – With you, Ivanko, with you,  
Oh, like a bright moon with a star!  
3. Oh, like a bright moon with a star,  
Oh, I, a young girl, am with you.

1. Our Kupailo with little nails,  
The boys have scabs in clusters.  
2. Our Kupailo is by the moon,  
The boys will hang themselves on it.

1. Oh, up, little sun,  
Up,  
Build, build, Ivanko,  
A barn.  

2. Build, build, Ivanko,  
Tall,  
And make wide  
Windows.  

3. So that the nightingale  
Will fly to me,  
So that he will wake me  
Early in the morning.  

4. Because I have a mother-in-law,  
Not a mother,  
She will not wake me  
Early in the morning.  

5. She will not wake me  
Early in the morning,  
She will just go outside  
And judge:  

6. "Other daughters-in-law  
Do the chores,  
And my daughter-in-law  
Sleeps until noon."  

7. Let her, let her,  
Let her sleep,  
Let her head  
Not ache.  

8. Because if her head  
Hurts,  
Then work  
Won't be desired.  

** Here the "и" approaches the sound of "і" and sounds somewhat in between. (L. Ye.)

1. Oh, the night of St. Peter is short,
The daughter-in-law hasn’t had enough sleep.
2. She drove the oxen, blinking,
Stubbing her feet on the stumps.
3. Oh, my oxen are gray,
Oh, my feet are very pale.
4. I walk along the ditch, dozing,
Calling my Ivanko:
5. – Come and tend the gray oxen,
Because at home the little children are crying.

№ 3  
1. Oh, I am going, traveling overnight  
To another village for a girl.  
2. Because in my own village she isn’t liked,  
The cattle won’t thrive for us.  
3. Though the cattle may not have horns,  
Our girl is not wealthy.  
4. Though the cattle may have sharp horns,  
Our girl has black eyebrows.

№ 4  
1. And in Krasnosiltsi* the rains fall,  
The rivers flow towards Shepetivka.  
2. Oh, they flowed and roared,  
Right up to Ivan’s porch.  
3. And Ivan has a big yard,  
Where the young men’s gathering took place.  
4. They gathered and assembled,  
Pooling money for mead and beer.  
5. Young Halya gave the most,  
She liked Ivanko.

№ 5  
1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
A swallow was bathing in the river,  
2. It came out on the shore to dry,  
And Halunya began to grieve.  
3. And she hasn’t even woven towels yet,  
But she already took a liking to Ivanko.  
4. And she hasn’t even bleached the towels yet,  
But she already fell in love with Ivanko.

№ 6  
1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
A witch went wandering.  
2. As she climbed the oak—she gnawed the bark,  
And she fell from the oak, tearing herbs.  
3. She fell from the oak, tearing herbs,  
And she enchanted our boys.  
4. Let her enchant, let them know,  
Let them not break Kupala!  
5. Our branches are green,  
Our girls are young.  
6. Our Kupala reaches to the moon,  
Let all the boys hang themselves!  
7. Our Kupala with bright eyes,  
And our boys with ailments!  

* Or another place where these songs are sung. (Note)

1. Oh, the night of St. Peter’s Day was short,  
The young bride did not get enough sleep.  
2. She drove the oxen, blinking,  
Stubbing her feet on the tree stumps.  
3. Oh, my oxen are gray-haired,  
Oh, my feet are very pale.  
4. I walk along the ditch, dozing,  
Calling for my Ivan:  
5. “Come to look after the gray oxen,  
Because the little children are crying at home.”

№ 3  
1. Oh, I am riding, riding overnight  
To a stranger’s village for a girl.  
2. Because in my own village she is not liked,  
Our livestock won’t thrive there.  
3. Even if it thrives, it won’t have horns,  
Our girl is not wealthy.  
4. Even if it has sharp horns,  
Our girl has dark eyebrows.

№ 4  
1. And in Krasnosiltsi* the rains are falling,  
The rivers flow toward Shepetivka.  
2. Oh, they flowed and roared,  
Right up to Ivan’s porch.  
3. And Ivan has a big yard,  
Where the young men’s gathering took place.  
4. They gathered, they assembled,  
Pooling money for honey and beer.  
5. The young Halia gave the most,  
She favored Ivan.

№ 5  
1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
The swallow was bathing in the river,  
2. She went out to the shore to dry off,  
And Halia began to grieve.  
3. And she had not yet woven the towels,  
But she already favored Ivan.  
4. And she had not yet bleached the towels,  
But she already loved Ivan.

№ 6  
1. Oh, on Ivan Kupala Day  
A witch went about singing spells.  
2. As she climbed an oak tree, she gnawed the bark,  
But fell from the oak and gathered herbs.  
3. She fell from the oak, gathered herbs,  
And enchanted our boys.  
4. Let her enchant, let them know,  
Let no one break Kupalo (the wreath)!  
5. Our branches are green,  
Our girls are young.  
6. Our Kupalo (festival) is under the moon,  
May all the boys get married!  
7. Our Kupalo is with wreaths,  
And our boys with aches!

Filename: Kmeln_287_Oi u lisi, oi u lisi
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, oh, in the forest,  
On the maple,  
There hung a cradle  
On it.  
2. – Oh, rock me, boys,  
High up,  
I will see my beloved  
Far away,  
3. Where my dear, dark-browed one  
Is walking,  
Shining with golden rings!  
4. Shining with golden rings,  
Walking with another girl.  
5. Walking with another girl,  
But forgetting about me, the young one!  

№ 8  
1. Oh, in the forest, oh, in the forest,  
On the oak,  
There hung a cradle  
On it.  
2. And in that cradle  
Was rocking,  
Young Ivanko  
Was swinging:  
3. – Oh, rock me, boys,  
High up,  
Oh, let me see  
Far away,  
4. Oh, where my girl  
Is walking,  
Waving a silk scarf.  
5. Waving a silk scarf,  
She is waiting for me, the young one!

Filename: Kmeln_288_Oi u lisi, oi u lisi
translation:
1. Oh, in the forest, oh, in the forest  
On the ridge,  
There hung a cradle  
On it.  
2. And who was swinging  
In that cradle?  
Young Ivan  
Was rocking.  
3. Oh, rock me, boys,  
High up,  
Let me look  
Far away.  
4. Oh, where is my Mary  
Passing by,  
Waving her embroidered sleeves.  
5. Waving her embroidered sleeves,  
Shining with golden rings.  
6. Shining with golden rings,  
Clinking with silver keys.

Filename: Kmeln_294_Shcho v seli za dym kuryt
translation:
1. What is that smoke rising in the village?  
The host is brewing beer.  
2. The hostess is at home,  
Preparing dinner for us.  
3. The dinner is not dear to us,  
The field has tired us out.

1. Quickly, reapers, quickly,  
Finish harvesting the field,  
We will weave wreaths  
From golden wheat.  

2. Quickly, reapers, quickly,  
Hurry with the harvest:  
People are already waiting for you,  
We will soon have dinner.  

3. Quickly, reapers, quickly,  
There will be honey, there will be beer,  
The tables are already set  
And guests are invited.  

4. Hey, reapers, don’t wander off,  
Don’t let the pies go to waste,  
The pies are already in the bowl  
In a place of honor.  

5. Hey, reapers and mowers,  
There are dumplings on the table,  
There is baked sausage  
And also boiled goose.  

6. Hey, you skilled reapers,  
Hey, you glorious reapers,  
Leave at least a little grain  
To weave wreaths for us.  

7. The field is finished, the end,  
We will weave the wreath,  
With it we will crown the reapers  
And sing a good song.

1. Little moon with a mountain*,  
Light our way,  
2. So that we do not get lost here,  
And do not lose the wreath.  
3. Because if we get lost on the way,  
Then we will lose the wreath.  
4. That will be a reprimand for us,  
From the master-lord.  

No. 3  
1. The wreath rolls from the field  
To the master’s gate,  
2. From under the gate to the barn,  
From under the barn to the storage.  

No. 4  
1. Reap, reapers, quickly,  
There will be vodka and beer.  
2. Oh, beer is no wonder to us,  
Nor is vodka dear.  
3. Nor is vodka dear,  
The field has exhausted us.  
4. The field has exhausted us,  
Broken the sickles.  
5. The golden sickles,  
The young reapers.  

* Probably, "star". (L. Ye.)

No. 4  
1. Our master has  
A silver sash,  
Oh, come out, master,  
Against us.  
2. Oh, come out, master,  
Against us,  
And bring a ruble  
For us too.  
3. Whether it’s a ruble or not,  
Or three,  
Because we are your young reapers.  

No. 5  
1. The mistress was walking  
Through the orchard,  
Breaking off  
The red viburnum.  
2. Breaking off  
The red viburnum,  
She was laying it  
On the master’s head.  
3. Oh sleep, oh sleep, master,  
Enough,  
Your wheat  
Is already in the barn.  
4. Oh, in the barn or not,  
In the sheaves,  
But by then it will be  
In the stacks.

№ 1  
1. Oh, harvest celebration, master,  
Harvest celebration,  
Try your best, master,  
For some vodka.  
2. But don’t say, master,  
That we were sleeping,  
We have already reaped  
Your wheat.  
3. As we were gleaning  
This wheat,  
We broke  
The new sickles.  
4. And our sickles  
Are golden,  
And our reapers  
Are young.  

№ 2  
1. Oh sleep, oh sleep, master,  
Enough,  
And your wheat  
Is already in the barn.  
2. Whether in the barn or not,  
In sheaves,  
Still it will be  
In stacks.  
3. – Oh, we were not sleeping  
In the field, master,  
We have reaped  
Your wheat.  
4. As many stars  
[Stars]  
Are in the sky,  
So many sheaves, master,  
You have.  

№ 3  
1. Oh to the end, reapers,  
To the end,  
Then we will go home  
By sunset.  
2. Oh, let’s stack the wheat  
In stacks,  
And the master will stack them  
In a hayrick.

1. Oh, the little wheat was boasting,  
Boasting,  
That it had filled itself  
With golden grains.  
2. And we mowed that little wheat  
And carefully gathered  
The grains.  
3. We left only the finest  
Ears of wheat  
For our glorious harvest,  
For wreaths.  
4. We will weave little wreaths  
From the ears of wheat,  
So that the edge will bloom  
As richly as the wreath.  
5. So that every year we may have  
Good harvests,  
So that every year we may celebrate  
The harvest festival.

1. |: Rejoice, whole village,  
For we are joyful, :| hey,  
|: The reapers are coming  
With wreaths. :|  
2. |: Rejoice, whole village,  
For we are joyful, :| hey,  
|: The reapers bring  
Glad tidings: :|  
3. |: Rejoice, whole village,  
For we are joyful, :| hey,  
|: The wheat  
Is already reaped. :|  
4. |: Rejoice, whole village,  
For we are joyful, :| hey,  
|: All is reaped  
And threshed. :|

1. Oh sheaf, sheaf,  
Sheaf with ears,  
What is in you, sheaf,  
Pure gold.*  
2. Oh sheaf, sheaf,  
Big sheaf,  
The ear is filled  
With golden grain.  
3. Oh sheaf, sheaf,  
Sheaf with stalks,  
The clean wheat  
Has grown well.  
4. We will have  
Plenty of bread,  
Because we worked  
Together in the field.  
5. Oh sheaf, sheaf,  
Sheaf with whiskers,  
You bring plenty  
To our home.

1. In Podillia, from long ago,  
We have a glorious tradition:  
We greet every holiday  
With a rosy korovai.  
White, white, bright-faced,  
Made from fine wheat,  
Both sweet and buttery,  
And on top of that, fragrant.  

2. Whether for an occasion or a holiday,  
We have many kinds of dishes  
On every table in every house—  
We have no small abundance.  
But in the center, as is proper,  
Like a bright-faced moon,  
And blooming like May—  
Our luxurious korovai.  

3. Whether we are meeting guests,  
Or seeing someone off on a journey,  
Our korovai is “on guard” —  
On an embroidered towel.  
White, white, bright-faced,  
Made from fine wheat,  
Both sweet and buttery,  
And on top of that, fragrant.  

4. In it is our glory and strength,  
Its growth nurtured by labor.  
We hold it in honor  
And share it with all the honest.  
May the harvest of the worker  
Gladden our hearts,  
May the Podillia korovai  
Be praised from land to land!

1. Oh, in the garden –  
The grapevine  
Is growing herbs.  
It begins  
At [such a]* wedding.  
2. God gives to him,  
Mother helps.  
God gives to him,  
Mother helps.  
3. |: – Oh, advise me,  
My mother,  
Whom to take to the festivities. :|  
4. |: – Take, my son,  
The servant boy,  
All your family. :|  
5. Oh, which one  
Is the closest family,  
Seat them near yourself,  
Oh, which one  
Is the distant one,  
Seat them at the end of the table.  

* The name of the groom. (L. Ye.)

I didn’t do anything today,  
All day I was looking out the window:  
To see if they were coming  
To ask to knead the wedding bread?

1. Bake me a red korovai,  
Bake me a red korovai,  
Like the bright moon in the sky.  
2. Bake me one with pine cones,  
Like the moon with stars.

Filename: Kmeln_342_Oi ishly my ta y na korovaiets
translation:
Oh, we were walking with a loaf of bread  
And carrying a box of eggs,  
When the boys caught up  
And broke the eggs in the box.  
Then the men caught up  
And broke the eggs on the lid,  
And caused me trouble—  
Oh, how will I go with the bread now?

Knead, knead the little bread makers,  
So there will be no smoothing out.  
Because if there is smoothing out,  
You will get a scolding.

Knead, knead the dough for the korovai,  
So that it won’t be lumpy.  
If it is lumpy,  
You will be reprimanded.  
And you, brave women,  
So that you are not dishonored.

As we kneaded the korovai,  
We carried water from the Danube,  
From the Danube, from the spring,  
The sisters kneaded the korovai.

Korovainyky from the city,  
Don’t steal dough from your pockets,  
Because if there is dough in your pockets,  
You will get a scolding.

Our korovai, our paradise,  
I am decorating you.  
With rue, with sage,  
With pink flowers,  
So that the children love each other.

Grow, korovai,  
Wider than the Danube,  
Taller than the ridgepole,  
Better than gold.

Our oven has  
Golden shoulders,  
And silver wings,  
To knead the korovai.

Our oven is laughing,  
It wants the korovai,  
And the oven shelf is smiling,  
Waiting for the korovai.

1. The little cuckoos fly  
In three rows,  
The little cuckoo is in front.  
And all the little cuckoos  
Sat in the meadows,  
The little cuckoo – on the viburnum.  

2. – Oh, why are you calling,  
Why are you lamenting,  
Gray little cuckoo?  
Oh, do you grieve  
For the dark meadow,  
Or for that viburnum?  

3. The girlfriends were walking  
In three rows,  
Hannusia – in front.  
And all the girlfriends  
Sat on the benches,  
Hannusia – for her dowry.  

4. And all the girlfriends  
Sat on the benches,  
Hannusia – for her dowry.  
And all the girlfriends  
Began to sing,  
Hannusia started to cry.  

5. – Oh, why are you crying,  
Why are you lamenting,  
Young Hannusia?  
Oh, do you grieve  
For your father and mother,  
Or for your little yard?  

6. – Oh, I grieve, I grieve  
For my father and mother  
And also for my little yard.  
But I grieve even more  
For my fair hair,  
For my girlhood.

1. Oh, beneath the forest  
A trodden path.  
2. Along it was riding  
Ivanko with the boyars.  
3. The guelder rose  
Blocked his way.  
4. He drew his saber,  
Began to cut the guelder rose,  
5. The guelder rose  
Started to speak to him:  
6. "Do not cut me down,  
I was not planted for you,  
7. Only for you  
Oksana was raised.  
8. Oh, raised,  
Planted in the garden."

1. Oh, by the forest  
A beaten little path.  
2. That way rode  
Ivanko with the boyars.  
3. The guelder rose  
Blocked his way.  
4. He drew his saber,  
Began to cut the little guelder rose,  
5. The guelder rose  
Began to speak to him:  
6. – Don’t cut me,  
I’m not planted for you,  
7. Only for you  
Oksana is grown.  
8. Oh, grown,  
Planted in the garden.

1. Through the little gateways into the cherry orchard  
Marusya went into the vineyard.  
Whoever finds her in that vineyard,  
That one will sit in the garden seat.  

2. Father went, did not find her,  
Plucked a flower and came back:  
– My painted daughter,  
Who is your betrothed?  

3. [Through the little gateways into the cherry orchard  
Marusya went into the vineyard.  
Whoever finds her in that vineyard,  
With that one she will sit in the garden seat.  

4. Mother went, did not find her,  
Plucked a flower and came back:  
– My painted daughter,  
Who is your betrothed?]  

5. Vanyusha went [and found her,]  
Took her by the hand and came back:  
– My flower, my painted one,  
My beloved Marusya,  
You are my betrothed.

1. We untangled, grew tired,  
The black eyes have seen enough.  
2. If there is no payment,  
We will braid them again.

Under the forest, a black cloud,  
Ahead, a fine couple,  
Like the moon with a star,  
Stepanko with Oksana.

Stamp the little step,  
Lay the little pathway  
For the young woman, the young man  
To the Lord’s house.

Toll on the road,  
In the severe frost,  
If giving, then give,  
So as not to stand long.

A ford on the road,  
Horses stand in the cart.  
If something is to be given, let it be given,  
As long as we don’t have to stand still.

Filename: Kmeln_391_Oi lety zh ty, holubochok, speredu
translation:
1. Oh, fly, my little dove, ahead,  
Tell, tell my mother that I am coming.  
2. Let her come out to meet me with a kalach,  
For I am coming with my husband Vasyl.  
3. Tie the white hands with a rushnyk,  
So that we may live in happiness with Vasyl.  
4. Oh, roll, little apple, to Raihorod,  
Come out, come out, my mother, to the gate.  
5. Treat your son-in-law well, mother, in sight,  
So that he loves your young daughter.

Filename: Kmeln_394_Druzhko, chom ty nas ne prosysh
translation:
Druzhko, why don’t you ask us 
To eat and drink, 
To be cheerful, 
Like a bee in spring 
That goes to the field, buzzing, 
And returns from the field, playing, 
Like a poppy blooming.

Druzhko wants to eat,  
He has nowhere to sit,  
He stands behind the shoulders  
With black eyes,  
Looking at the table,  
Because he has no spoon.

Oh, there is a barrel in the pantry.  
Oh, there is a barrel in the pantry.  
It was saying something.  
No matter how much we drink it,  
It will make us burst.

The matchmaker is glad in her heart,  
She tore off some grapes,  
Placed them on a plate:  
– Let’s drink, matchmaker, vodka.

Oh, how strange it is for us, strange,  
That the matchmaker does not leave.  
Whether looking for a boot,  
Or putting on slippers,  
He does not greet the matchmakers.

The matchmaker’s matchmaker got scared,  
Hid in the pantry.  
Peeks through the hole,  
To see if there are any switches.

Our matchmaker, viburnum-raspberry,  
How sweet it is to look at you,  
Your black-browed eyes,  
Your eyes all silky,  
Your face is rosy,  
Our heart is tender.

Filename: Kmeln_404_Oi de zh taia visilnaia maty
translation:
Oh, where is that wedding mother,  
She promised to give three shots.  
The first with cherries,  
The second with berries,  
And the third with viburnum,  
And for her own child.

The maid of honor,  
Like a pillow,  
Jumps on the table,  
A child cries after her.

Out of the house, boyars, out of the house,  
For we will drive you with a club,  
Not with a club, but with a crowbar,  
We will set dogs on you.

She [the young woman] went for water,  
She [the young man] went for water,  
The beads fell into the water.  
Guests must be invited  
To retrieve the beads.

Bend, viburnum, bend,  
You, Vitya, do not worry,  
We will break off the viburnum,  
We will persuade the young one.

Bright little moon,  
Light our path,  
So we don’t stray from the road,  
So we don’t lose Halyna.

1. Oh, my aunt was combing me,  
She did not smooth my little curls,  
2. Oh, she combed me with a comb,  
Stuck a rose flower in my hair.  
3. And my mother was watching,  
Washing her face with tears.  
4. Look, mother, at my place,  
All the girls are sitting with braids.  
5. But I combed my own,  
Because dew fell on mine.  
6. Dew fell from the white blossom  
From today and forevermore.

1. You will be, mother, and remember,  
How you go to wash the tablecloths:  
2. – I had a girl [such-and-such],  
She worked faithfully for me,  
3. She worked faithfully for me,  
She pleased me.  
4. But with the mother-in-law, the work is hard,  
She does not please her.  
5. Neither with a bowl, nor with a plate,  
Nor with honey, nor with vodka.

1. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my father:  
Thank you, father,  
For building me a house,  
You will be no more,  
No more.  

2. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my mother:  
Thank you, mother,  
For waking me up early,  
You will be no more,  
No more.  

3. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my brother:  
Thank you, brother,  
For bringing boys to the house,  
You will be no more,  
No more.  

4. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my sister:  
Thank you, sister,  
For teaching me to braid my hair,  
You will be no more,  
No more.  

5. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my doorsteps:  
Thank you, doorsteps,  
For knocking boys down,  
They will be no more,  
No more.  

6. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my cherry tree:  
Thank you, cherry tree,  
For standing tall with the boy,  
I will be no more,  
No more.  

7. Wherever I traveled,  
Wherever I went,  
I thanked my gates:  
Thank you, gates,  
For blocking the boys’ path,  
They will be no more,  
No more.

1. The pine was burning, blazing,  
|: The pine was burning, blazing,  
A girl stood beneath it. :|  
2. A girl stood beneath it,  
|: A girl stood beneath it,  
Combing her red braid. :|  
3. – Oh my braids, my braids,  
|: Oh my braids, my braids,  
You have served me for a long time. :|  
4. Now you will serve no longer,  
|: Now you will serve no longer,  
You will go under a white veil. :|  
5. Under a white veil with a flower,  
|: Under a white veil with a flower,  
You will no longer be a girl. :|  
6. Under a white veil with ends,  
|: Under a white veil with ends,  
You will no longer go with the lads. :|  
7. Under a white veil under a headscarf,  
|: Under a white veil under a headscarf,  
You will no longer go as a bride's attendant. :|  
8. The pine was burning, blazing,  
|: The pine was burning, blazing,  
An old woman stood beneath it. :|  
9. The lads were putting out that pine,  
|: The lads were putting out that pine,  
Carrying water in a sieve. :|  
10. As many holes as are in the sieve,  
|: As many holes as are in the sieve,  
That many girls the lad has. :|  
11. As much water as is in the sieve,  
|: As much water as is in the sieve,  
That much truth the lad has. :|

Where else can we go,  
When the wedding is over?  
For the master already has masters,  
And does not care about the servants.

1. Oh mother, this is not like our way,  
Oh mother, this is different.  
2. Here, the evening star does not shine,  
And breakfast is around midnight,  
3. And breakfast is around midnight,  
She has cried out her eyes.

103. Collective Farm Owner (OK-KSh-rad.)
Moderate

1. Collective farm owner,
And in your yard
Paradise has blossomed.
Oh, paradise has blossomed,
The people have rejoiced,
A new world
Has been born for us.*
* This chorus is sung after each verse of the song. (L. Ye.)

2. And in your yard
A little tree has grown.

3. And that little tree
The brigade cut down.

4. The brigade cut down —
Built the collective farm building.

5. The collective farm building was built,
Given to the youth.

6. The village rejoiced,
A new world was born.

7. Everyone goes to the club,
Finds leisure,

8. They dance, sing,
Praise the authorities.

1. The Pure Virgin
Went around the world,
Carrying Jesus Christ
With her.*

* The last two lines of each stanza are sung twice. (L. Ye.)

2. She came
To the master,
To the rich man:
– Accept me, master,
Into your house.

3. Oh, go away, gentlemen,
From my house,
For I will unleash
Dogs against you.

4. She came
To the master,
To the poor man:
– Accept me, master,
Into your house.

5. – I would gladly
Accept you,
But there is no place to sleep,
For I have four children
And an old mother.

6. Oh, come, gentlemen,
To my stable,
There you will sleep
In peace.

7. The master woke up
At midnight,
Bright stars were shining
Over his stable.

8. Bright stars were shining
Over his stable,
That the Virgin Mary
Had given birth to a Son.

9. Oh, if only I had known
That it was the Mother of God,
I myself would have lain
To sleep under the threshold.

10. Oh, if only I had known
That it was the Mother with God,
I myself would have lain
With the children under the threshold.
Good evening!

1. Oh, in the field, in the field  
A fir tree grew.  
There the Most Pure,  
The Pure Virgin Mother  
Gave birth to a son.*  
* The second half of each stanza is sung twice. (L. Ye.)  

2. And when she gave birth,  
She sat down to rest:  
– Luli, luli,  
My little Son,  
And I will lie down to sleep.  

3. – My dear mother,  
Mother,  
Wait just an hour.  
And I will go all the way to heaven,  
Bring back a featherbed.  

4. My son, my Son,  
What are you already doing?  
It’s not yet two o’clock,  
And you are already talking.  

5. – My dear mother,  
Mother,  
That is why I was born,  
He created the heavens,  
I was not born.  

6. Oh, in the field, in the field  
A fir tree grew.  
There shepherds  
Were grazing their flock,  
And news came to them.  

7. An angel of God  
Flew down from the high heavens,  
And brought them a cup  
Of what they needed.  

8. Oh, do not be afraid,  
Little shepherds,  
For this is God’s power.  
For this is wine from Jesus,  
From the Son of God.  

9. Oh, in the field, in the field  
A fir tree grew.  
There the Most Pure,  
The Pure Virgin Mother  
Gave birth to a son.

1. Oh, in the field, in the field  
Grew a fir tree.  
There the Most Pure Mary  
Bore a Son.*  
* The second half of each stanza is sung twice. (L. Ye.)  
2. And when she gave birth,  
She began to swaddle him:  
– Luli, luli, my little Son,  
And I will lie down to sleep.  
3. – Mother, my mother,  
Wait at least an hour,  
And I will go all the way to paradise  
To bring a featherbed.  
4. – Oh my Son, my Son,  
What are you doing already,  
It's not yet two hours,  
And You are already speaking.  
5. Oh, in the field, in the field  
Grew a fir tree,  
There shepherds were grazing their flock,  
News came to them.  
6. The angel of God flew down  
From the high heaven  
And brought them a cup of wine  
Of the kind they needed.  
7. – Do not be afraid, shepherds,  
For this is God's power.  
For this wine is from Jesus,  
From the Son of God.  
8. Oh, in the field, in the field  
Grew a fir tree,  
There the Most Pure Mary  
Bore a Son.

1. [I went in spirit]
To the city of Bethlehem,
And knocked on the manger
And saw inside it.

2. That Christ the Savior,
King, Creator, and God,
Was born of a Virgin
And lies naked

3. In the manger, speechless,
Among the lambs,
And above Him the geese
Sit with the mother.

1. [A new joy has come,
Which has never happened before:]
Above the manger, a bright star
Shone over the whole world.
2. Where Christ was born,
Was incarnate from the Virgin,
As a man, wrapped in swaddling clothes,
He was humbly laid.
3. The shepherds with a lamb
Before that Child
Kneel down,
Praising the King-God.
4. – We glorify You, King,
Heavenly ruler,
Grant happy years
To the master of this house.
5. To the master of this house,
And his mistress,
Grant years, happiness, and fortune
To our mother Ukraine.

1. [Heaven and earth  
Rejoice today,]  
Angels, people  
Celebrate joyfully.  
Chorus:  
Christ is born,  
God incarnate,  

2. In Bethlehem  
Joyful news:  
The pure Virgin  
Has given birth to a Son!  
Chorus.  
Angels sing,  
They greet the King,  
They bow,  
Shepherds play:  
"Miracle, miracle!" –  
They proclaim.

122. Christ the Savior (OK-HK-22)  
1. Christ the Savior  
Was born at midnight,  
In a dark manger  
The light appeared.  
2. And above the manger  
A star shines:  
– Christ the Savior! –  
Proclaims to all.  
3. Suddenly an angel  
Flies down from heaven  
And brings the news  
To the shepherds in the field.  
4. With great fear  
They stood by the manger  
And on their knees  
They prayed.  
5. They stood a long time –  
Praying to Christ  
And were enlightened  
By the bright faith.  
6. The elders bowed  
To the King at the manger,  
Bringing Him  
Precious gifts.  
7. And wicked Herod,  
With a deceitful heart,  
Searches everywhere  
For the infant Christ.  
8. He kills innocent children  
And spills  
Holy blood.  
9. The Holy Virgin  
Leaves at night,  
Finding refuge  
For Christ in Egypt.  
10. Among pagan peoples,  
In the land of idols,  
(The line unintelligible)  
The Savior of the world.  
11. When Herod  
Died an evil death,  
Christ appeared  
In His native land.  
12. And in Galilee  
He returned,  
Settled to live  
In Nazareth.  
13. Christ the Savior,  
On Your birthday  
Grant us, the darkened,  
The light of enlightenment.

1. Yesterday  
In the evening still  
Malanka was herding  
Two drakes.*  
* The second half of each verse is sung twice. (L. Ye.)  
** This refrain is performed after each verse of the song. (L. Ye.)

Short sheepskins,

My toes are frozen).  
If you don’t give sausage,  
I will tear the house apart.  
If you don’t give pie, –  
I’ll grab the cow by the horns!
